1.content:
My name is Marcus Scott, I’m 18 years old; I live with my parents and my younger brother, Glenn. My two closest friends are Eric Williams and Kat Maddox; Eric being a friend from childhood and Kat being my 13-year-old next-door neighbor.

Now I know what you’re probably thinking; why is an 18-year-old guy best friends with a 13-year-old girl. It’s nothing sexual, I assure you; I’ve known Kat since her family moved in six years ago. I don’t know why, but the two of us just clicked; our relationship becoming something akin to siblings.

Anyway, onto the story – I’ve been in a wheelchair since I was 14 years old; paralyzed from the waist down after being struck by a drunk driver while crossing the street. It took some time before I was able to come to terms with being paralyzed; but with help from my friends and family, I was able to do so.

Everything was going normally until about 3 years after the accident; when out of the blue, a medical researcher came to the house. Dr. Kevin Sheppard was developing a potentially groundbreaking cure for paralysis, and he wanted to test it on me. If it worked, I would regain the use of my legs. There was, however, a small catch; in addition to potentially curing my paralysis, the procedure would revert me back to the age I’d been at the time of the accident.

While I wasn’t exactly thrilled by the thought of being 13 years old again, I knew it was something I could learn to deal with; especially if it meant getting my legs back.

Little did I know, however, there was going to be an unforeseen side effect; one that would change my life forever...
1.title: In the Beginning

11.content:
After a year of tests and examinations, the day was finally here – the day I’d get the use of my legs back after four years of being stuck in a wheelchair. It was just after 8:00 in the morning when we got to the hospital. As Dad parked the car in one of the handicapped spots, I realized this was the last time I’d have to use one of them – I mean, if everything went the way it was supposed to.

Once everyone was out of the car, Glenn started wheeling me towards the hospital entrance. Leaning forward to talk in my ear, Glenn said, “I’ll tell you; I’m looking forward to no longer pushing you around.”

“Same here, brother.” Chuckling, I said, “I can’t wait to be rid of this wheelchair.”

“I know you’re excited, Marcus; but don’t get your hopes up.” Looking at me, Dad said, “After all, this is an experimental treatment; there’s no guarantee that it will work.”

There he goes again; Dad ever so subtly saying he didn’t think I should go through with the procedure. As always, Mom was right there trying to bring him around. “There’s nothing wrong with a little optimism, Kent.” Sighing, Mom said, “The worst that could happen is that it doesn’t work. But if it does, our son will get his life back.”

“I’m sorry, Gloria; you’re right.” As we walked into the hospital lobby, Dad looked at me and said, “I truly hope this works out for you, son.”

“Same here, Dad.” Pausing, I said, “Has anyone heard from Eric or Kat this morning?”

“Eric called why you were in the shower; he’s got college orientation today but wishes you luck.” Looking at me, Mom said, “Kat texted your brother and said that she’d stop by before the procedure.”

************************


It only took another 15 minutes before we were in my hospital room. Glenn effortlessly lifted me from the wheelchair onto the bed. Once he was finished, I looked at him and said, “I bet you’ll be glad to never have to do that again.”

Glenn had the misfortune of being the one who, more often than not, had to help me with my everyday tasks – like getting dressed, showering, or using the bathroom. “It may not be the most glamorous job, but I’m happy to do it.” Looking at me, a goofy grin on his face, Glenn said, “You’re my little brother after all; it’s my job to help you.”

“Hey, I’m not your little brother yet.” Chuckling, I said, “I’ve still got a few more hours to enjoy being the oldest child.”

“Enjoy it while you can, brother.”

Dr. Sheppard walked in a few minutes later; a clipboard in his hands. “Is everyone ready for the big day?”

“More than you can possibly know, Doc.” Smiling, I said, “I can’t wait to have my legs back.”

“I must say, Marcus, your sunny optimism is quite infectious.” Looking at the clipboard, Dr. Sheppard said, “I’ll have a nurse come in shortly to check your vitals. Once I’m certain that you’re ready, I’ll have you brought to the procedure room.”

“Sounds good to me.” Pausing, I said, “Not to be rude or anything; but would it be alright if I had some time to myself?”

“Of course not, sweetie; I have to update the family anyway.” Smiling, Mom said, “We’ll be outside if you need us.”
11.title: Arriving At The Hospital

111.content:
It had been an hour or so since the nurse came to check my vitals. My folks were somewhere in the hospital, most likely at the cafeteria having a quick breakfast. I, unfortunately, I couldn’t eat anything prior to the procedure. So I was passing the time by watching Manifest on my tablet; trying to imagine how strange it would be to discover years had gone by for everyone else but you.

I was in the middle of watching an episode when I heard a knock on the door. I quickly paused the episode before turning to face the door. “Come in!”

I got a smile on my face when I saw Kat standing in the doorway; an equally big smile on her face. Running into the room, she hugged me and said, “I’m glad I got to see you before they took you in for the operation.”

“It’s not an operation, Kat; I won’t even be giving anesthesia.” Smiling, I said, “But, I am glad you got here before they brought me in.”

“Are you nervous?” pulling a chair up to my bedside, Kat said, “I’d be pretty nervous if I were in your position.”

“I’m a little nervous; mostly out of fear that it won’t work.”

“I know it’s going to work.” With a goofy grin, Kat said, “In a few days, once you’re out of the hospital, we’ll be able to hang out without you being in a wheelchair.”

“That’s the best news I’ve heard in a long time.” Looking at Kat, I couldn’t help but notice she was nervous about something.
“You’re asking me if I’m nervous; while you look worse than you did before your ballet recital.”

“I seem to recall breaking my leg at that recital; so I guess I had good reason to be nervous.” Sighing, Kat said, “But, you’re right, I am nervous; because there’s something I’ve been meaning to tell you for a while now.”

I couldn’t help but feel hurt that Kat seemed to be keeping something from me. We never kept any secrets from each other; after all, she was the first one outside the family that I told about the procedure. “Are you keeping something from me, Kat?”

“Yeah, but it’s only because I’m afraid of how you’ll react to it.” Taking a deep breath, Kat said, “I’ve had a crush on you since 7th Grade.”

If I’d had any liquid in my mouth, I probably would have done a spit take at that news. Looking at Kat, dumbfounded, I said, “Y-You have a crush on me? Why’d you never tell me before this?”

“I never said told you because it felt wrong to think of you that way; you’ve been like my big brother since I moved in next door.” Blushing, I said, “Plus there was the fact that you were 5 years older than me.”

“Yeah, well that’s not going to be an issue for much longer.” Pausing, I said, “Once we’re the same age; perhaps we could see where things go.”

“Really!?” louder than she probably intended, Kat looked at me and said, “Please tell me you’re not joking right now.”

I’d be lying if I said I didn’t think Kat was attractive; because she is. I just felt weird thinking about her that way; because, as she said, I thought of her as my little sister. We’re talking about a girl who I taught how to ride a bike. The more I thought about it, however, the more I realized there wouldn’t be any issues once we were the same age. “I’m not joking, Kat; I’d like to go out with you sometime.” Looking at her, I said, “Just promise me one thing; you won’t get mad if, for one reason or another, one of us decides that the two of us dating is weird.”

“I suppose I could agree to that.” Leaning over, Kat kissed me on the cheek and said, “I’ll see you later, Marcus.”

Kat left a few minutes later; walking past Mom and a nurse as they came into the room. Once Kat was out of earshot, Mom turned to me and said, “Kat seemed pretty happy; is there something I should know about?”

“Not right now, Mom.” Blushing, I said, “I’ll tell you later.”

“Okay then.” Gesturing to the nurse, Mom said, “Are you ready for the procedure?”

As the nurse helped me into the wheelchair, I looked at Mom and said, “I’ve been ready for the last four years, Mom.”
111.title: A Welcome Visitor

1111.content:
I was brought down the hall to an exam room; entering to find the room filled with monitors and what looked to be recording equipment of some kind. I briefly wondered what if was all for, but then I remembered this was an experimental procedure; Dr. Sheppard probably wanted everything recorded to look over later.

The nurse, an attractive woman in her early 30s, lifted me from the wheelchair onto the exam table. After turning me over onto my back, she started strapping me down. As she worked, she said, “These are only in case you have a reaction to the medicine; it wouldn’t do for you to injure yourself because of this.”

“I guess you have a point.”

Mom stood by in the corner as Dr. Sheppard walked into the room; holding a clear box with what looked to be four syringes filled with a reddish-orange liquid. While the nurse finished strapping me to the table, Dr. Sheppard said, “Now Marcus, the procedure is going to be quite simple; I’m going to inject the serum into four locations on your spine.” Looking at me, he said, “If all goes well, they should go about repairing the damage done by the accident.”

“It’s not going to hurt, will it?” wincing, I said, “I’m not exactly the biggest fan of needles.”

“You won’t feel a thing, my boy.” Grabbing the first syringe, Dr. Sheppard said, “Now, if you’re ready, I’m going to inject the first needle just below your neck.”

“I’m ready, Doc.”

***********************************


The procedure itself only took about 20 minutes; thankfully there were no complications. Once he was finished, Dr. Sheppard said, “The injections are all finished, Marcus.” Coming around the table, he knelt down to where I could see him. “I included a mild sedative in there as well, so don’t be surprised if you start to feel a little drowsy.”

“Thanks, Dr. Sheppard.” Smiling, I said, “I really hope this works.”

The nurse waited a few minutes before removing the straps holding me to the table. Once that was finished, I was brought out of the room; to find Dad, Mom, and Glenn standing there. Looking at me, Dad said, “How’d it go?”

“Everything went off without a hitch, Kent.” Walking out of the room, behind me, Dr. Sheppard said, “With any luck, he’ll start to regain sensation in his legs later this afternoon. If all goes according to plan, he’ll be taking his first steps tomorrow morning.”

“Thanks for everything, Kevin.” Tearing up, Mom hugged Dr. Sheppard and said, “You’ve given my baby his life back.”
1111.title: The Procedure

11111.content:
The sedatives that I’d been given knocked me out shortly after I got back to the room; I didn’t wake up until shortly after 4:00. As I lay there in bed, wondering what to do now that I was awake, I became aware of a tingling sensation in my toes. The sensation slowly started to travel up my legs; reminding me of what it felt like to have my arm fall asleep.

I tried my best to keep my mind off the sensations; watching more shows on my tablet. I made until just before 5:00 before the sensations started to feel a little painful. I started to panic a little, so I reached over and pushed the call button on the side of my bed. It wasn’t long before a nurse, the one that brought me to the procedure room, came running into the room. “What’s wrong, Marcus?”

“It’s almost like my legs are being stabbed with needles.” Wincing, I said, “Is that normal?”

“That’s nothing you need to worry about, Marcus.” A vague smile started to form on the nurse’s face. “That’s just your legs starting to repair themselves.”

I’d been so caught up with the alien sensations I’d been feeling, that I hadn’t stopped to consider what they meant. “Are you serious?” tearing up, I looked at the nurse and said, “Th-The procedure worked?”

“It certainly looks that way.” Her smile growing bigger, the nurse said, “I’ll page Dr. Sheppard; then I’ll go find your folks.”

As the nurse ran out of the room, I started crying. Of course, I’d hoped the procedure would work, but I never allowed myself to figure out how I’d react if it did. After four years, I’d finally regained the use of my legs.”
11111.title: A Tingling Sensation

111111.content:
It was 10 minutes before Dr. Sheppard and my family arrived. Seeing how crowded the room was, Glenn said, “What’s going on?”
Walking over to my bedside, Dr. Sheppard said, “The nurse told me your brother is experiencing tingling sensations in his legs.”

“Okay.” Confused, Glenn said, “What’s that supposed to mean?”

“It means the procedure worked, Glenn.” Ecstatic, I said, “I’m getting the use of my legs back.”
While Mom and Dad struggled to contain their excitement, Glenn ran over and hugged me. “I’m so happy for you, Marcus.”

“Let’s not jump the gun here; there are still tests I need to run in order to be 100%.” Removing a pen from his pocket, Dr.
Sheppard said, “Marcus, I’m going to run this down your leg; please let me know when you start to feel it.”

I watched, with apprehension, as Dr. Wilcox moved the pen down my leg. It wasn’t until he got a few inches above my knee, that I started to feel it. Gasping in shock, I said, “I-I felt that!”

As they stood behind Dr. Sheppard; Glenn, Mom, and Dad were exchanging hugs with each other. Looking at me, Dr. Sheppard said, “It’s still early; but, after what I’ve seen, I’m confident the procedure has worked.” With a smile, Dr. Sheppard said, “Just one more thing; please wiggle your toes for me.”

I concentrated on my toes for a few seconds; gasping again when I saw them move ever so slightly. Mom was unable to contain herself; she started crying as she came over to hug me. “My baby’s cured!”

While Dad and Glenn came over to hug me as well, Dr. Sheppard said, “I’d advise waiting until tomorrow before you try walking, but I’m happy to say the procedure was successful.”

Tears streaming down her face, Mom walked over and hugged him. “Thank you, Kevin, for everything you’ve done.”
111111.title: An Exam

1111111.content:
After the positive news that the procedure had worked, Mom got permission from the hospital to have food delivered from my favorite restaurant. Having not eaten anything since last night, I wasn’t surprised at all when I ate an entire 12-ounce steak in 5 minutes. That got me a look from Glenn, who said, “Glad to see your appetite hasn’t changed at all.”

“Dr. Sheppard said I have to eat a lot of protein to regain my muscle mass.” With a grin, I said, “So enjoy being able to outrun me while you can.”

“We’ll see about that.” Chuckling, Glenn said, “I’m not going to go easy on you just because you’re my little brother.”

“Would you stop calling me your little brother; you make it sound like I’m way younger than you.” Sighing, I said, “At most, I’m only 5 or 6 months younger than you now.”

“Yeah, well you’re still younger than me.”

I wanted to say something in response, but I was suddenly interrupted by the ringing of my cell phone. Putting my fork down, I reached over and grabbed it off the table. A smile appeared on my face the instant I saw it was Kat calling. Answering the phone, I said, “Hey Kat, what’s up?”

At the mention of Kat’s name, I noticed Mom starting to usher Dad and Glenn out of the room. Mom followed suit just as Kat said,
“Not much, I just wanted to see how the procedure went.”

“The procedure went fine; didn’t feel a thing.” Pausing, I said, “After a while though, I did start to feel a tingling sensation in my legs.”

“Oh?” sounding concerned, Kat said, “Is that good or bad?”

I was tempted to string Kat along for a few moments, but I quickly figured she wouldn’t like that very much. “It’s a good thing.” Chuckling, I said, “The procedure worked; I was able to wiggle toes about an hour ago.”

I had to pull the phone away from my ear as Kat’s scream was almost deafening. After a moment, she calmed down and said, “It worked? I-It really worked?”

“Yeah, it worked.” Smiling, I said, “I have the use of my legs again.”

“Marcus, I’m so happy for you.” Breathing a sigh of relief, Kat said, “I was so worried that it wouldn’t work.”

“Dr. Sheppard wants me to take my first steps tomorrow morning.” Pausing, thinking about what I was about to say, I said, “I’d really like it if you were there to watch.”

“Are you kidding? I wouldn’t miss this for the world.”

I felt some butterflies in my stomach when I heard that. With a smile, I said, “Great, I’ll text you the details tomorrow.”

“That sounds good to me.” Pausing, Kat said, “I’ll really happy for you, Marcus.”

“Thanks, Kat.” Grinning from ear to ear, I said, “I’ll see you tomorrow.”

I hung the phone just as Mom walked back into the room. Looking at me, she said, “I take Kat was excited?”

“My ears are still ringing she screamed so much.” Smiling, I said, “She’s going to be here tomorrow to watch my first steps.”

“I’m glad to hear that, honey.” Coming next to the bed, Mom said, “Do you have a problem with your father, Glenn and I going home for the evening?”

“No, go right ahead.” Yawning, I said, “I’ll see you guys tomorrow.”
1111111.title: A Celebratory Dinner

11111111.content:
I was brought into the physical therapy room down the hall, the next morning, to find that both my family and Kat’s were already there. With everyone watching, and Kat even recording on her phone, the physical therapist brought me over to a set of horizontal bars.

As she helped me to my feet, she said, “Okay Marcus, now hold on tightly to those bars.” Pausing, she said, “I’m going to let go of you, and I want you to try putting your weight on your legs.”

I did as I was told; although my legs wobbled a little, and wouldn’t straighten out; I was standing on my own. That got me applause from everyone in the room, even from another patient nearby. “What’s next?”

“You just jump right in, don’t you.” Smiling, the therapist said, “Now I’d like to see how far you can get.”

I took a deep breath, and put my right foot in front of my left. Then, I put my left in front of my right. I did it again before my legs gave out under me. I wanted to cry as the therapist helped me back into the wheelchair. "Three steps," I said. "Three lousy steps."

“But that’s three more steps than you’ve been able to do for years.” Looking at me, she said, “I didn’t expect you to climb Mount Everest on your first day.”

The group came over and hugged me. With tears in her eyes, and her voice cracking, Mom said, “My baby is walking again.”

I wanted to say that I’d only gone three steps, but I realized that she and the therapist were right. I may have stumbled, but I had been walking before that. My tears of humiliation were replaced by tears of triumph. The last person to congratulate me was Kat. She hugged me tightly and gave me a kiss on the cheek, before saying, “I’m really proud of you, Marcus.”
11111111.title: My First Steps

111111111.content:
I was feeling pretty drained by the time I got back to my hospital room. Kat’s parents gave me some flowers and a teddy bear before they left the room with my folks. That left only Glenn in the room with Kat and me.

After a few minutes, I noticed Kat giving Glenn a weird look. I was about to ask what was going on, when she said, “Glenn, do you think you could give Marcus and me some privacy?”

Glenn turned and left without saying a word; leaving me alone in the room with Kat. Sitting up in the bed, I said, “What’s on your mind, Kat?”

“I made a terrible mistake yesterday; I never should have said I had feelings for you.” Looking at me, she said, “It’s obvious you don’t think of me that way; so I’d like it if you just forget I said anything.”

I thought back to yesterday after Kat came clean about her feelings for me. I was still shocked to learn that she felt that way about me. “Look, Kat, I meant what I said yesterday.” Taking a breath, I said, “I’d like to go on a date with you; so long as our parents are okay with it.”

“Why wouldn’t they be okay with it?” chuckling, Kat said, “My mom’s known for months that I had feelings for you. I even talked to her about it last night.”

“R-Really?” shocked, I said, “What did she say?”

“She wants me to be happy; and if being with you will make me happy, then she’s all for it.” Blushing, Kat said, “It’s just my dad we’ll have to convince.”

Growing concerned, I said, “Is that going to be a problem?”

“I hope not; it’s just that he’ll probably ask if we ever did...anything before this.”

“We haven’t done anything before this.” Looking at her, I said, “Hell, the closest I’ve ever been to seeing you naked was seeing you in a bikini at the beach.”

“Oh really?” raising an eyebrow, Kat said, “Did you like what you saw?”

I’d be lying if I said no. “I’ll just say it became clear you were no longer that little girl who used to make me check her closet for monsters.”

Until that point Kat had been sitting rather close to me; after my response, she leaned back in the chair. “The two of dating is going to be weird, isn’t it.”

“Why do you say that?”

“It’s just...you and I have that brother/sister dynamic.” Looking at me, Kat said, “The idea of us dating just seems weird.”

I really don’t know what came over me at that point. Leaning forward, I pulled Kat towards me and kissed her on the lips. After a few seconds, I pulled back and looked at her. “Did that feel weird to you?”

Kat was silent for a moment. "That didn't feel weird at all." Looking at me, blushing a little, she said, "That feels so much better than I could've imagined."

So began my relationship with Kat; as she climbed into bed with me and we started making out right then and there.
111111111.title: Back In the Room

1111111111.content:
Kat and I made out for what seemed like forever; but, in reality, was only about 10 minutes. I got the sense we both wanted to keep going, but neither of us wanted our families for walk in on us.

It was a good thing we stopped when we did; because, not even 5 minutes later, Kat's dad walked into the room as we were watching something on my tablet. Completely unaware of what we'd been doing just moments ago, he smiled and said, "I'm glad to see this hasn't ruined the relationship you two have."

Kat and I exchanged a mischievous look, before she turned to her Dad and said, "If anything, Dad, our relationship has improved now that we're the same age."

"Well I hate to break you two up, but I'm afraid we need to go sweetie." Looking at me, Mr. Maddox said, "Congratulations on a miraculous recovery, Marcus."

"Thanks Mr. Maddox."

Kat waited until her father left the room before turning to look at me. With a sigh of relief, she said, "For a moment there I thought we were in trouble."

"Relax Kat, no one knows that we were making out." Sitting up in the bed, I said, "Once I'm out of the hospital, I'll talk to your dad and ask if he's alright with us dating."

"That's something I'll have to get used to." Kissing me on the lips, Kat said, "But I like the way that sounds - the two of us dating."

"Me too." Smiling, I said, "You going to visit to tomorrow?"

"Yeah, but it won't be until early afternoon." Grabbing her cellphone, Kat said, "I've got cheer tryouts in the morning."

"Can things get any better?" Chuckling, I said, "Not only did I get the use of my legs back, but I'm also dating a cheerleader?"

"I haven't made the team yet, Marcus." Walking towards the door, she turned around to say, "But I appreciate your confidence."
1111111111.title: Post-Make Out

11111111111.content:
I woke up shortly afternoon to the sound of rain hitting the window; as a distant clap of thunder echoed outside. After making sure I could still wiggle my toes, I looked over to the table and found a tray of food sitting there; with a note next to it. Reaching for the note, I saw that it was from Mom:

I brought you some food but decided not to wake you. I will be by later to visit.
Love – Mom

Putting down the note, I reached for the tray and pulled off the lid; revealing what I hoped was a delicious roast beef sandwich. I was about to bite into it when I heard a knock on the door. “Come in!”

I watched as the door opened and a familiar face walked into the room – Allison Park. Allison was a nurse at the hospital; she’d been working in ICU when I’d been brought in after the accident. Throughout the 3 weeks that I had been in the ICU, she did everything she could to make sure I was comfortable. It goes without saying that I grew fond of her; we quickly became like family.

“Hey Allison, how are you?”

“I should be asking you that, Marcus.” Closing the door, Allison walked over to my bed and said, “I got worried when I heard you were back in the hospital.”

“I’m fine, Ally.” Smiling, I said, “I’m actually feeling better than I have in a long time.”

“Well, that’s a relief.” Hugging me, Allison said, “Given your history, I feared the worst.”

Early on Dr. Sheppard had warned us against informing any hospital staff about the procedure; he was worried someone might steal his work. I felt terrible about having to lie to Allison for the last 10 months, but it had to be done. Now that the procedure was done, however, I was free to tell her; I was just waiting for the most memorable time to do so.

“How was your trip to Bermuda?”

Allison and her girlfriend Jamie had gone to Bermuda for a vacation a few weeks earlier. I’d been so focused on everything, I hadn’t had the opportunity to ask how it went before now. “Jamie proposed on our second day there; so I’d say it was really good.”

“Oh my god, that’s great!” spotting the ring on Allison’s hand, I said, “I’m so happy for you guys.”

“Before you ask; we’ve already decided to have Glenn and you be a part of the wedding party.” Hugging me, Allison said, “My big day wouldn’t be the same without my brothers up there with me.”

As I’d mentioned, Allison had grown close to my family over the last four years; Glenn and I thought of her as our big sister, and she thought of us as her little brothers. “I’d be honored, Ally; I just hope I’m feeling better by then.”

“What’s the supposed to mean?” Allison grew concerned. Looking at me, she said, “Are you okay, Marcus?”

It was at that moment I realized now was the perfect opportunity to surprise her. “I’ll tell you, but first I have to go to the bathroom.”

While Allison looked around the room for my wheelchair, I couldn't help but grin mischievously. After a moment, she gave up and turned back to me. "Where's your wheelchair, Marcus?"

"Oh, I don't need it anymore." Smiling as I pointed to the walker by the foot of the bed, I said, "Could you hand me that walker?"

Allison was clearly confused; wondering why a paralyzed patient had a walker in their room. Bringing the walker over to me, she said, "Why do you have a walker? You can't even walk."

"I can now." Hopping out of bed, I said, "That's why I'm here at the hospital."

With that, I slowly started walking towards the bathroom while Allison just looked on in shock.
11111111111.title: Later On

111111111111.content:
I stood outside the bathroom door while Allison just stared at me in shock. Once she was able to compose herself, she ran over and hugged me; knocking the walker to the side in the process. “Oh my God!” looking at me, Allison said, “H-How is this possible?”

I proceeded to tell Allison about the serum and the procedure; from when Dr. Sheppard asked my family to be in the study, to when I took my first steps. When I was finished, I looked at her and said, “I’m sorry we couldn’t tell you before now; we were sworn to secrecy.”

“Don’t apologize for that, I totally understand.” Hugging me again, Allison said, “I’m just so happy that you’re walking again.”

“Believe me, so am I.”

“That’s going to make college so much better for you; now that you’re mobile again.”

I quickly realized I’d left something out about the procedure. “Actually I’m won’t be going to college; at least not yet anyway.” Wincing, I said, “One side effect of the serum is that I’m now 4 years younger.”

“Huh?” perplexed, Allison looked at me and said, “Are you serious?”

“You had no problem buying that I was walking again, but the fact that I’m 13 years old again is something you can’t grasp?”

After a moment, Allison burst out laughing and said, “You’re right, I guess that is kind of stupid.”

A feeling of pressure in my bladder reminded me that I had to use the toilet. “If you don’t mind, I’m going to use the bathroom now.”

*********************


I couldn’t help but relish the fact that I was going to the bathroom without anyone’s help. It’s funny how you get enjoyment out of even the simplest things - such as no longer needing your brother to help in the bathroom.

After I got off the toilet and washed my hands, I went back into the room; to find Allison sitting on my bed. “I suppose you’re happy about no longer needing Glenn’s help on the toilet?”

Chuckling, I said, “I think we’re both happy about that.”

Allison stood up as I approached the bed; giving me a strange look as I went past. “That’s funny; I didn’t realize until just now, but _______.”
111111111111.title: Telling Allison

1111111111111.content:
Allison stood up as I approached the bed; giving me a strange look as I went past. “That’s funny; I didn’t realize until just now, but you could really use a haircut.”

I had no idea what Allison was talking about; since I’d just had a haircut last week. “I just had a haircut last week, Ally.”

“Well, you should get another one because you need it.” Grabbing my arm, Allison gently pulled me back into the bathroom. “If your hair was any longer, you could be mistaken for a girl.”

Looking at my reflection in the mirror, I realized Allison was right. Had I not remembered getting a hair cut last week, I would have thought it had been months since my last one. While not overly long, it still covered my ears and was working its way down my neck. “Dr. Sheppard didn’t say anything about the serum affecting my hair.”

“You said the serum turned you into a 13-year-old again, right?” looking at me, Allison said, “You’re basically back in the middle of puberty again; your hormones are all in flux.”

“Okay, what do hormones have to do with hair growth?”

“You didn’t do well in biology, did you?” sighing, Allison said, “Certain hormones are known to affect hair growth.”

“I suppose that makes sense.” Looking back at the reflection in the mirror, I said, “Do you think this is something I should bring to Dr. Sheppard’s attention?”

“I don’t think it’s necessary to tell him; it’s probably just connected to your becoming younger.” Looking at me, Allison said, “I’ll tell you what; my shift ends in a few hours. If you want, I can come back and give you a quick trim.”

“You don’t have to do that, Ally.” Pausing, I said, “You’ve probably had a busy day; go home to Jamie.”

“She’s actually out of town for a conference, so it’s just me at the apartment.” Smiling, Allison said, “This will give me something to do; besides, we could hang out like we did after you first got paralyzed.”

As the two of us walked back into the room, I hugged her and said, “If you really want to, I suppose that would be okay.”

“Then it’s settled, I’ll come back after my shift and we can hang out like old times.” Smiling, Allison said, “I’ll see you in a few
hours.”
1111111111111.title: Your Hair Is Longer

11111111111111.content:
Allison, always one to keep her promises, walked into my hospital room just after 4:00; with a pizza and a 2-liter bottle of soda in her hands. Placing it on the counter, Allison looked at me and said, “I thought you might be hungry, so I had one of the swing shift nurses pick up a pizza.”

“Thanks, I’m getting tired of hospital food.”

As she took some scissors from her purse, Allison said, “Let’s get that hair cut before we start eating.”

I followed Allison into the bathroom as she put a chair in front of the mirror. As I sat down in the chair, I looked at her and said, “That’s probably a good idea; I wouldn’t want hair in the pizza.”

The two of us talked while Allison went to work cutting my hair; mostly about Jamie’s proposal, but we did discuss changes in my life. I was surprised when she didn’t seem all that shocked about Kat. “I’ve seen how you two are together, I’m not entirely surprised to learn she had feelings for you.” Looking at me, she said, “Although, I would highly recommend the two of you talk to your folks before you do anything.”

“We already agreed to do that.” Looking at Allison’s progress in the mirror, I said, “After I get out of the hospital, I’m going to talk to her dad; explain everything to him and ask if he’ll let me date her.”

“I’m sure there won’t be any reservations about you guys.” Brushing some hair off my shoulders, Allison said, “After all, you’re both the same age now and it’s not like you ever did anything before.”

“Well, we make out a little this morning; but beyond that, we’ve done nothing.”

“Oh really?” with a grin, Allison said, “How’d that go?”

“I thought it would be weird kissing her, but it actually wasn’t.” blushing a little bit, I said, “Kat even said it was more enjoyable then she imagined it would be.”

“It seems like this procedure definitely changed your life, Marcus.” Smiling, as she looked at something in the mirror, Allison said,
“Not only did you get the use of your legs back, but you’ve also gotten yourself a girlfriend.”

It was a few more minutes before Allison finished cutting my hair. After she cleaned me up a bit, I looked at her handiwork in the mirror. It actually looked better than it had after my last haircut. “Thanks for the trim, Ally.”

“Anything for you, Marcus.” Heading towards the door, she said, “Now come on, let's go watch a movie and have some pizza.”
11111111111111.title: Hanging With Allison

111111111111111.content:
Mom stopped by around 6:30 to bring me a change of clothes for tomorrow; since, assuming tomorrow’s physical went well, I was going home. Allison had left by then, so it was just me in the room. The first thing Mom noticed when she entered the room was my hair cut. “What happened to your hair, Marcus?”

“It must’ve grown a good inch or so; Allison offered to give me a trim.” Chuckling, I said, “We figured it was just a side effect of the medicine.”

“I don’t see any reason why it should be.” As she placed the bag of clothes on the counter, Mom looked at me and said, “I spoke with Aunt Marcie on the way over; she said Rachel and Mackenzie are looking forward to the party.”

The party that Mom was referring to was our traditional end of summer party. Normally we’d only have Kat’s family over for a BBQ; but since it happened to fall shortly after the procedure, my dad decided to invite both sides of the family to celebrate my cure.

Rachel and Mackenzie were my only cousins on my dad’s side; their Mom, Marcie, was my Dad’s younger sister. Rachel surprised the entire family last summer when she came out as trans and announced she was starting her transition. I hadn’t seen her since then; so I was curious to see how much she’d changed. “That’s great, I haven’t seen them in almost a year.” Pausing, I said, “I doubt I’d even recognize Rachel now.”

After we talked for a few more minutes, Mom said, “I should probably get going; the hospital’s visiting hours end soon.” Kissing me on the forehead, Mom said, “We’ll be back tomorrow morning.”

“Thanks, Mom.” Smiling, I said, “I’ll see you guys tomorrow.”
111111111111111.title: A Visit From Mom

1111111111111111.content:
Over the last 4 years, I’d probably spent just over a month in the hospital; so you can understand why I was anxious to finally leave. It was just after 7:15 in the morning when I woke up; opening my eyes to find a nurse looking through a cabinet nearby. Seeing me, she said, “I didn’t mean to wake you, I’m just doing an inventory of the room.”

“Don’t apologize; I’m usually up at this time anyway.”

The nurse looked at me as I climbed out of bed and slowly made my way to the bathroom. “If you don’t mind my asking, what are you in for?”

I wasn’t entirely sure Dr. Sheppard was ready to go public yet, so I had to play it cool. Looking at the nurse, I said, “I shattered my femur a few weeks ago, I’m just recovering from surgery to repair it.”

“Well, I certainly hope the surgery was successful.”

I entered the bathroom as the nurse left the room. Once I closed the door, I looked in the mirror to find that my hair had grown again; it was almost the same length it had been prior to the procedure. What the hell is going on with my hair? I quickly brushed that thought aside, no pun intended and sat down to use the toilet.
1111111111111111.title: The Next Morning

11111111111111111.content:
My second physical therapy session took place with less fanfare than my first; with only my Mom there for emotional support. The therapist had me start by doing some leg exercises, similar to the ones I’d been doing since yesterday.

After 20 minutes of leg exercises, the therapist helped me over to the horizontal bars. “Okay Marcus, take it slow, but I want you to try and get to the other side of the bars unassisted.”

As I situated myself on the bars, I decided now would be a good time to come clean about my relationship with Kat. Looking towards Mom, as I slowly started walking, I said, “There’s something I’ve been meaning to tell you, Mom.” Taking a deep breath, feeling nervous, I said, “Yesterday, after the first therapy session, I made out with Kat in my hospital bed.”

“I see.” With a shocked expression in her eyes, Mom said, “Please tell me it was consensual.”

“Of course it was; do you really think I’d force myself on anyone?” sighing, I said, “You guys raised me better than that.”

“I know you wouldn’t do anything like that; it’s just a little sudden.” Looking at me, Mom said, “It’s just, I always thought the two of you had a brother and sister relationship. To hear that you two were kissing just seems...wrong.”

“It’s not like we made the decision out of the blue; she’s had a crush on me for years.” Taking a breath, I said, “Now that we’re the same age, we decided to...well, give dating a try.”

“Why exactly are you telling me this, Marcus?”

“Because we knew it might be a touchy subject; both with you and dad, and with her folks.” Looking at her, I said, “That’s why I’m talking with you, and she’s talking with them. Once I’m out of the hospital, I’ll go talk with them and she’ll talk to you. Assuming everyone’s okay with it, we’ll take things from there.”

Mom was about to say something; when the therapist came walking over. “I’m sorry to interrupt this discussion; but, Marcus, you managed to make it all the way across.”

Turning around, I saw that I’d not only walked the length of the bars but also about 5 feet beyond them. “I-I did it!”

Mom came running over to hug me as the therapist congratulated me. “I’m not ready to let you walk without some support, so I’m going to send you home with a cane.” Smiling, the nurse said, “Congratulations, Marcus.”

Once the nurse walked off, Mom hugged me again and said, “I’m so proud of you, sweetie.” Looking at me, with tears in her eyes, Mom said, “You’ve earned a second chance at life.”

“Thanks, Mom.”
11111111111111111.title: One Last Session

111111111111111111.content:
Once my final therapy session was finished, Mom and I went back to the room to grab my things. As we walked through the halls, Mom kept telling me how proud she was. The two of us were about to walk back into the room when her cell phone suddenly rang. “It’s your father.” Looking at me, Mom said, “Why don’t you pack your things, I’ll be right in.”

I silently nodded and entered the room; only to find Kat sitting on my bed. “Hey Kat, how’d tryouts go?”

“I messed up a few times, but I managed to do alright.” Getting up, Kat said, “They told everyone we’d hear in a few days.”

“I’m sure you did great.” With a grin, I said, “I kind of like the thought of dating a cheerleader.”

Kat walked towards me, a smile on her face, and planted a kiss on my lips. She lingered there for a moment, until she said, “Enough about me; how’d your last session go?”

“It went great; I managed to walk the length of the bars and then some.” Gesturing to the cane, I said, “I have to use this for a few days, but I should be fine before school starts.”

“I’m so happy for you.” Kissing me again, Kat said, “After everything you’ve been through, the last four years, you were due for a win.”

“It paved the way for us to be together, so I’d this was a pretty big win.”

It wasn’t long before I completely forgot about packing my things; as I was too busy kissing Kat. I’ll admit it felt weird kissing her the first time; but the more we did it, the more it felt right. The two of us were so wrapped up in the kissing that we didn’t realize my mom was in the room until she cleared her throat. “I’m not interrupting anything, am I?”

“No, of course not.” Separating herself from, Kat said, “We were just...”

“I know what you were doing, Kat; Marcus told me everything.” Closing the door, Mom said, “Might I suggest you two refrain from doing anything until you know if anyone has a problem with the two of you being together.”

Kat and I realized Mom was right; there was no point in us kissing if our parents had a problem with us dating. Looking at Mom, I said, “Sorry Mom, we’ll try to restrain ourselves.”

“Good.” Handing me the bag of clothes she’d brought yesterday, Mom said, “Go get changed Marcus; your dad and brother are waiting in the parking lot.”
111111111111111111.title: Getting Ready to Leave

1111111111111111111.content:
I was grateful that the hospital waived its policy requiring me to leave in a wheelchair. After enduring an experimental procedure, I wasn’t keen about using a wheelchair again. With Kat and Mom following behind me, I walked down the hall to the elevator.

When we got to the hospital lobby, I saw Allison and Dr. Sheppard standing by the door. In an uncharacteristic move, I went over and hugged Dr. Sheppard. “Thanks, Doc, you gave me my life back.”

“It was my pleasure, Marcus.” Looking at me, he said, “Now don’t squander this second chance.”

“I promise that I won’t.”

I turned my attention to Allison who, between tears of joy, hugged me and said, “I’m so happy for you, Marcus.”

“Thanks, Ally.”

Once all that was finished, I followed Mom and Kat out to the parking lot. I had to admit it was weird not seeing the car in a handicap spot. As Dad and Glenn climbed out of the car, Glenn looked at me and said, “Dad and I thought you might like to sit in the front seat.”

A grin formed on my face; I hadn’t sat in the front seat in almost 4 years. Looking at Glenn, I said, “Thanks, Glenn.”

“Anything for my little brother.”

It only took a minute for everyone to get into the car; after which Dad left the hospital. As we left the parking lot, I hoped this would be the last time I’d be there for a while.
1111111111111111111.title: Going Home

11111111111111111111.content:
We got to the house a short time later; pulling into the driveway while Kat’s parents scrambled to put up a ‘Congratulations Marcus’ banner on the garage. The car had barely come to stop before I’d climbed out and onto the driveway.

Kat’s parents both came over and gave me congratulatory hugs. It was nice to see they were so supportive of me, I only hope that remains the case when I ask to date their daughter. As Kat helped Glenn take my things inside the house, I went to follow; only to be stopped by Mr. Maddox as he placed a hand on my shoulder.

I turned around and saw him looking at me with a serious look on his face; Mrs. Maddox had a similar look on hers. Feeling nervous, I said, “Is there something I can help you with, Mr. Maddox?”

“Can we talk in private, son.” Gesturing towards their house, Mr. Maddox said, “I understand there’s something you wish to discuss with us.”

I’d never felt so nervous in my life; as I prayed they would be accepting. Once the three of us were in their house, we all took a seat in the family room. Looking at me, Mrs. Maddox said, “Kat told us everything last night, Marcus; we just wanted to hear it from you as well.”

Okay, here goes nothing. “I want you both to know this never crossed my mind until yesterday; but, I really like your daughter.” Taking a deep breath, I said, “I would very much like your permission to date her.”
11111111111111111111.title: Arriving At Home

choices: The Beginning of a Beautiful Relationship
111111111111111111111.content:
The room was filled with silence for what seemed like an eternity; with everyone just staring at each other in silence. I was beginning to fear the worst when, breaking the silence, Mr. Maddox said, “I hope you know, Marcus, just how much my wife and I care for you; we think of you and your brother as family, as I’m sure your folks do with Katherine.” Taking a breath, he said, “With that being said, you can understand if we have misgivings about the two of you dating.”

“What kind of misgivings?”

“Physically you and Katherine are the same age now; but mentally you’re still an 18 year old boy.” Looking at me, Mrs. Maddox said, “We just want to make sure you’ve never done anything inappropriate with our daughter.”

I know they were just looking out for Kat’s safety; but I couldn’t help but feel a little hurt. “I can assure you that we’ve never done anything physical before yesterday; even then, all we did was kiss a little bit.” Sighing, I said, “The only reason that Kat and I are even considering this is because we’re the same age.”

I sat on the couch while Mr. and Mrs. Maddox talked amongst themselves for a few minutes. I felt like I was in the principal’s office awaiting punishment. After a moment, Mr. Maddox looked at me and said, “You have our blessing to date our daughter, Marcus; on one condition.” Taking a breath, Mr. Maddox said, “If I find out you two have had physical relations; then, so help me, you’ll regret it.”

“We’re only 13 years old, sir; I assure you that neither of us is ready to have sex.” Trying not to sound nervous, I said, “All we want is to try being a couple.”

“You have our blessing to do so, Marcus.” Standing up, extending his hand out, Mr. Maddox said, “I hope you understand we’re just looking out for Katherine’s safety; it has nothing to do with you.”

Shaking Mr. Maddox’s hand, I said, “Thank you, sir.”
111111111111111111111.title: Talking With Kat's Parents

1111111111111111111111.content:
I walked into my house a few minutes later to find Kat sitting on my family room couch; Glenn and my folks were nowhere to be seen. Getting to her feet, Kat walked over and said, “What did Daddy say?”

“We have his blessing, so long as we don’t get physical.” Looking at her, I said, “It bugged me when he asked if I’d ever done anything inappropriate with you; I thought he knew me better than that.”

“Don’t take it personally, Marc; he still thinks of me as his little girl.” Looking at me, she said, “Trust me; if he had any reason to think you’d done anything to me, he never would’ve given us the okay.”

“I suppose you’re right.” Pausing, I said, “So what did my folks say to you?”

“They just wanted to make sure I’m going into this with my eyes open. Which I’m doing for the most part.”

“What’s the supposed to mean?”

“I always close my eyes for this.” She closed her eyes and kissed me on the lips.

After she broke the kiss, I hugged her and returned it. "Remember, nothing physical."

“I think I have a way around that. Whenever somebody’s with us, all we’ll do is hug and kiss; but when it’s just the two of us, I think we’ll be fine as long as we keep our hands outside of each other's clothes. Does that sound good to you?"

My fingers were already doing a little exploring; as I looked at her and said, ”That sounds good to me.”

Kat did the same with me, and the two of us were enjoying ourselves...right up until she put her hands on my chest. While I winced and pulled away, she looked at me and said, “What’s wrong?”

“I don’t know; it just hurt when you touched my chest.”

Sitting back on the couch, Kat looked at me and said, “Let me have a look.”

I unbuttoned my shirt to find that my nipples were pink and puffy. “What could have caused that?”

“I have no idea.” Looking at me, hesitant to touch my chest, Kat said, “Maybe you had an allergic reaction to something at the hospital.”

“I guess that makes sense.” Buttoning my shirt back up, Kat said, “What do you want to do now?”

“I would love nothing more than to stay here kissing you, but I have some chores to do at home.” Giving me a kiss, Kat said,
“I still can’t believe this is really happening; I can’t believe you’re my boyfriend.”

“I know; it’s surreal.” Kissing her, I said, “Here’s to a long and happy relationship.”
1111111111111111111111.title: The Beginning of a Beautiful Relationship

11111111111111111111111.content:
I was lying on the couch, watching a movie on TV, when the front door opened. I muted the volume as Glenn walked into the house carrying what looked to be a cake. “Are you kidding me right now?” with an amused look on his face, Glenn said, “You’re able to walk for the first time in four years, and you’re in here watching TV.”

“Well I was making out with Kat for a little while, but she had chores to do.”

“So you two are really doing this, huh?” placing the cake on the stairs, Glenn walked into the family room and said, “You’re actually going to date each other?”

“Yeah, we’re actually going to date each other. You got a problem with that?” With a chuckle, I said, “Are you upset that your younger brother has a girlfriend before you?”

“I’m not jealous; if anything I’m happy for you.” Sitting next to me, Glenn said, “I’m just wondering why your first girlfriend had to be Kat.”

I quickly realized where Glenn was going with this conversation; after all, his relationship with Kat was identical to mine. “If either of us thought it would be weird dating, do you really think we’d be doing it?” Looking at Glenn, I said, “I care deeply about Kat; now I just care about her in a different way.”

“You two aren’t going to be doing anything physical, are you?”

“Absolutely not; her dad expressly forbade us from doing more than kissing each other.” Grinning ear to ear, I said, “It doesn’t really matter, we’re perfectly fine just kissing each other.”

“I still think it’s weird that you two are dating; but mom, dad, and her folks don’t have a problem with it.” Clapping a hand against my back, Glenn said, “I’m happy for you, Marcus.”

“Thanks, Glenn; me too.” Chuckling, I said, “I’ve got my legs back and a girlfriend all within a two day period.”

It was at that point Mom and Dad walked into the house. Looking at me, Mom said, “I hope you worked up an appetite, honey; we’re going to have a big dinner to celebrate.”

“Sounds good, Mom.”
11111111111111111111111.title: The Family Returns

111111111111111111111111.content:
After a filling dinner, I decided to call it a day. As I climbed into bed that night, I found myself looking forward to tomorrow morning. It felt good to know I’d never need a bar to sit up in bed, while I’d also never need help in the bathroom again. With a smile on my face, I drifted off to sleep.

******************************


I woke up the next morning feeling fantastic. I felt like I could run a marathon as I climbed out of bed. With a smile on my face, ignoring the cane, I walked down the hall to the bathroom.

I can’t tell you how excited I was for my first shower in years; it felt wondering to stand in the shower, to lather my own body, and to shampoo my own hair. After rinsing off, I stepped out of the shower and grabbed a nearby towel. It was then that Glenn knocked on the door. “Are you about done in there? My back teeth are floating.”

I groaned in annoyance; only Glenn could find a way to ruin my first shower in years. “I’ll just be a few more minutes.” At least, that’s what I thought I said; because, to my confusion, the voice coming out of my mouth wasn’t mine. Clearing my throat, I said, “You’ll have to use the downstairs bathroom, Glenn.”

Nope, that definitely wasn’t my voice; in fact, it sounded almost like a girl’s voice. I quickly realized how right I was when, after wiping the condensation off the mirror, I came face to face with the reflection of a naked girl.

I immediately opened my eyes and found that I was back in bed, and judging from the darkness outside, it was still early morning. After making a cursory look at myself, I was relieved to see it was only a dream. But that only created more questions; why would I dream I was a girl?
111111111111111111111111.title: A Strange Dream

1111111111111111111111111.content:
I had quite a bit of difficulty getting back to sleep after that; that strange dream and the irritation when I slept on my chest kept me from falling back to sleep. But eventually, I managed to fall asleep around 4:30 in the morning; only to be woken up at 6:30 when

Glenn came into the room. “Do you need to use the bathroom, Marcus?”

Glenn and I had a routine that, at 6:30 every morning, he’d check if needed to use the bathroom. Looking at him, annoyed that I was awoken early, I said, “I don’t need your help to use the bathroom anymore, Glenn.”

Glenn was silent for a moment until he realized what I was talking about. “Oh my God.” Bringing a hand to his forehead, he said, “I was so used to helping you, I completely forgot that you didn’t need it anymore.”

“It’s the thought that counts.” Sitting up in bed, I said, “But I don’t need to use the bathroom right now.”

“How are your legs feeling today?”

“I’m not used to feeling them, but I think they’re fine.” Looking at him, an eyebrow raised, I said, “Why do you want to know?”

“My track coach wants us to go for a run at least twice a week before school starts up.” Looking at me, he said, “I was going to make a run to the park, I figured I’d see if you wanted to come.”

“Sure, I don’t see why not.” Getting out of bed, I said, “You’ll understand if I have to take it slow now and then.”

“I have no problem with that.” Chuckling, Glenn said, “I’m going to leave in about 15 minutes; I’ll meet you downstairs.”
1111111111111111111111111.title: The Next Morning

11111111111111111111111111.content:
Because I had to stop a few times to rest, Marcus and I took about 20 minutes to jog the 2 miles to the park. One thing became apparent early on, however; if I was going to keep jogging, I’d need to find a shirt that didn’t irritate my chest.

Once we got to the park, I excused myself and went to the bathroom. Walking into the bathroom, making sure the room was clear; I lifted my shirt to see what I was dealing with. Not only was my chest even more inflamed then yesterday, but I could’ve sworn it was also puffier. I guess I should put some lotion on when I get home.

I walked out of the bathroom and quickly found Glenn leaning against a nearby tree; staring at a group of older girls doing yoga in the distance. Walking up to him, I said, “You know they’re way out of your league, right?”

Turning away, walking back down the bath, Glenn said, “Way to spoil my mood, Marcus.”

Catching up to him, I said, “Sorry.”

As we walked through the park, Glenn said, “Have you thought about doing any sports once school starts?” looking at me, he said, “Weren’t you going to try out for soccer before the accident?”

“You know, I hadn’t really thought about it.” Pausing to think, I said, “I suppose I could try out for soccer; I was always pretty good at it.”

“Do you even remember how to play? I mean, it’s been four years since you played last.”

“I’ll just watch some of the old videos Mom and Dad recorded of my games.” Shrugging, I said, “I’ll be back to my old self in no time.”

Checking his watch, Glenn said, “Come on, we should probably head home.”
11111111111111111111111111.title: The Park

111111111111111111111111111.content:
I was feeling pretty winded by time Glenn and I got back to the house; I had to stop in front of Kat’s house to catch my breath. I was standing in the road when I heard the door to Kat’s house open, followed by footsteps. I didn’t look up, so I had no idea who was coming until Glenn said, “Morning Kat.”

I looked up as Kat walked over to us. “Morning Glenn.” Looking at the two of us, she said, “Where are you two coming from?”

“We just took a jog to the park and back.” Smiling, I said, “It felt good to be able to run again.”

Coming over, Kat kissed me and said, “After four years, I’d imagine it would be.”

I watched Glenn grimace at the sight of Kat kissing me; I thought he was going to say something about it. In all fairness, he did, but not what I thought he would. “He’s all sweaty and smelly; are you really kissing him right now?”

“Damn right!” chuckling, Kat said, “He could be covered in grease and grime; I’d still kiss him. He’s my boyfriend after all.”

Feeling better, I leaned over and kissed Kat as well; saying, “I could get used to you calling me your boyfriend.”

Much to Glenn’s disgust, the two of us started kissing right there on the sidewalk. “Ugh, I don’t need to see that.”

The two of us kissed for another few moments until Kat stopped. “I got a call from the cheerleading coach.” With a smile on her face, Kat said, “I made the squad!”

“That’s great!” kissing her again, I said, “I’m so happy for you!”

“I’m in the mood to celebrate, what do you feel like doing?”

“To be honest I’m kind of tired from the jogging; perhaps we could just watch a movie?”

“Okay.” Smiling, Kat said, “I’m fine with that.”
111111111111111111111111111.title: Returning Home

1111111111111111111111111111.content:
Kat and I were watching Spider-Man: Far From Home in my living room. Actually we weren’t really watching it; it was simply noise playing in the background while the two of us made out on the couch. Of course, I would have enjoyed watching the movie; but what guy would pass up an opportunity to make out with his cheerleader girlfriend.

We were in the middle of kissing when Glenn walked into the room and, groaning, said, “Why do you guys even have the movie playing when you’re not even watching it?”

I broke away from Kat and, with an annoyed expression, looked at Glenn. “What’s so bad about me making out with my girlfriend?” pausing, I said, “You’re not jealous of your little brother, are you?”

“Why would I be jealous?” scoffing, Glenn said, “Why should I care if my brother has a girlfriend before me.”

“It does seem like you’re a little jealous, Glenn.” With a raised eyebrow, Kat looked over my shoulder at Glenn. “If you want, I can put in a good word with some of my squadmates.”

“Thanks for the offer, but I think I’ll pass.” With a weak smile, Glenn said, “I’d like to get a girlfriend on my own.”

“If you say so.” Turning around, as she leaned against my right shoulder, Kat said, “Let me know if you change your mind.”

Once Glenn left the room, I turned to face Kat. “Maybe we should watch at least some of the movie.”

“I guess you’re right.” With a chuckle, Kat said, “I always did have a little crush on Tom Holland.”

As the two of us went back to watching the movie, I found myself focusing on Peter Parker in the Spider-Suit. He is kind of cute.
1111111111111111111111111111.title: Time With Kat

11111111111111111111111111111.content:
It had been a little while since Kat left to go; everyone was doing their own thing around the house. Mom had mentioned that we’d be going out to dinner in a little while; so, in an attempt to pass the time, I went upstairs to watch TV.

I was lying on my bed, watching another episode of Manifest, when I decided I needed to get a closer look. Climbing out of bed, I went to lie down on the floor in front of my TV; lying on my stomach and crossing my legs as I did so.

As the episode progressed, I found myself fixating more on one of the male leads than anything else. Watching him kissing one of the female characters, I found myself imagining it was me he was kissing. I don’t think I’d mind a guy with a little facial hair.

Before I could even realize what just went through my mind, the door opened and Glenn walked into the room. “Come on, Marcus; we’ve been calling you for the last 5 minutes.”

“Huh?” snapping back to reality, I realized what time it was. Getting up off the floor, I said, “Sorry, I guess I was too focused on the show.”

After turning off the TV, I grabbed a coat and followed Glenn down the hall; briefly becoming aware of a slight swaying of my hips as I was walking.
11111111111111111111111111111.title: In My Bedroom

111111111111111111111111111111.content:

111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Car Ride

Looking up from my food, I almost dropped my sandwich; standing in front of me was probably the most attractive boy I’d ever seen. I almost immediately started giggling like an idiot; while Kat just looked at me in embarrassment. “Don’t mind her, she gets that way sometimes.” Turning to the boy, Kat said, “You can take the seat if you want.”

“Thanks, but is it alright if I sit with you two?” gesturing to the food court, the boy said, “This is the only table with an open seat.”

I finally decided to take a swig of my ginger-ale in an attempt to stop the giggling. After a moment, probably louder than I should have, I said, “Sure, have a seat!”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Lunch

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
“Thanks for the seat.” As he took a seat across from me, the boy said, “My name’s Jeff.”

Feeling like I was over the moon, I said, “I’m Cassidy, and this is my friend Kat.”

“It’s nice to meet both of you.” As he unwrapped his Subway sandwich, Jeff said, “I just moved here with my folks a few days ago.”

“Well, that’s a funny coincidence.” Chuckling, Kat glanced towards me and said, “Cassidy is new, too.”

“Really?”

“That’s right.” Nodding, I said, “I had some family problems at home, so my Aunt and Uncle offered to let me live with them.”

The three of us spent the next few minutes getting to know each other; only for the moment to be ruined when my cramps returned. Only this time they were different; they were accompanied by what could only be described as something pushing itself inside me.

I quickly realized what was happening. Getting up, I grabbed my purse and said, “Excuse me, I have to use the ladies’ room.”

Once inside the bathroom, I entered a vacant stall and dropped my pants. I gasped when I pulled down my panties and saw a bloodstain. With some toilet paper wrapped around my finger, I managed to confirm both my new anatomy and the source of the bleeding. There was no doubt about it – I was a girl.

Opening my purse, I saw half a dozen tampons lying on the bottom of the purse. Thanks, Mom. I thought it would feel weird putting one inside me; but, after following the instructions, I was surprised to find I didn’t feel it at all.

Satisfied that was taken care of; I grabbed my phone and sent a text to both my Mom and Kat – PERIOD!!!

They both responded within seconds of each other; Kat responding with ATTA GIRL! and Mom responding with Welcome 2 Womanhood. Once the texts were sent out, I got redressed and leaned against the wall. I started to cry as I slowly slid the wall until was sitting on the stall floor.

I knew this was coming, but I still wasn’t prepared for it. I’d spent the last 18 years of my life as a guy, but now I was going to spend the rest of my life as a girl. I suppose I should have been happy since I was no longer stuck in the middle of the gender fence; I was firmly on the female side now.

I sat there, crying, for a few minutes until I suddenly a knock on the stall door. Thinking it was Kat, I got up and opened the door; only to find myself face to face with the woman from the restaurant.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Welcome to Womanhood

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The two of us just stared at each other for a moment; I recognized her, but I highly doubted that she recognized me. Unlike last time when she had an angry look on her face, this time she had a look of concern. “Honey, are you alright?”

“No, not really.” Between tears, I said, “I-I’m just going through some things right now.”

“Oh, I see.” Placing a gentle hand on my shoulder, she said, “I understand what you’re going through; it’s something every woman understands.”

Believe me, there are no women like me. “Am I always going to be this emotional?”

I didn’t ask that question to string her along, I sincerely wanted to know. Was I going to be an emotional wreck every time I had my period? “I’m not sure it’s my place to tell you that, honey.” With a sympathetic smile, she said, “That’s something you need to discuss with your mother.”

This woman wasn’t that bad; she was kind of a bitch the last time, but now she was kind and understanding. “T-Thanks.”

Kat came walking into the bathroom at that moment. Seeing me crying, she got concerned and walked over. “Hey Cass, are you alright?”

“Not really, no.” sighing, trying to stop crying, I said, “I think I need to go home.”

After gathering my things from the stall, Kat gently escorted me from the bathroom; but only after we said goodbye to the woman.

As we walked back to the table, I saw Jeff stand up and walk over to me. “Hey, are you alright?”

I tried to respond, but couldn’t; so Kat said, “She’s having female problems.”

“Does she need anything?” concerned, Jeff said, “I have some...”

“You carry tampons?”

“My twin sister is always forgetting them; so I got used to carrying them around.” Turning to me, he said, “Are you set?”

“I’m fine,” finding my voice, I said, “I had a few in my purse.”

After that, while Kat stepped away to call her mom, I surreptitiously slipped Jeff my phone number. Even in my fragile state, I wasn’t going to pass up the chance to give a cute guy my number.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Continue

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
After walking into the house, I barely acknowledged anyone as I walked upstairs; not even paying attention as Mrs. Maddox spoke with my mom. Once I was in my room, I closed the door and threw myself onto the bed.

I lay in bed for a while, doing nothing but cry into my pillow. Damn these emotions! Everything I’d spent the last 18 years working towards was now useless; pretty soon my parents would declare Marcus dead, and I’d just be their live-in niece. I understood why they were doing it, but it felt as though they were casting my male past aside.

Turning over onto my back, I said to myself, “I guess it’s not all bad; after all, I can walk again.” Sighing, glancing down at my body, I said, “There are worse things than being a girl; I mean ½ the population is female, and they don’t have a problem with it.”

I sat up in bed at that point; realizing I’d been neglecting something for the last few days. I’d been so focused on the fact that I was becoming a girl; I hadn’t spent much time looking at my body. The last vestiges of my male mind couldn’t pass up to chance to see examine my body up close.

After drawing the curtains closed, I undressed and walked over to a floor mirror my Mom had given me. As I looked over my reflection, I couldn’t help but think that I was one attractive girl. God, I hope that doesn’t make me vain. I paid particularly close attention to my breasts, my waist, and my butt.

I was about to venture south, and examine my new anatomy up close when my bedroom door opened. Afraid that it was Glenn, I scrambled to cover myself with a sheet; only to look around and see Mom standing there with an amused look on her face.

Adjusting the sheet, I glared at mom and said, “Geez Mom, ever heard of knocking?”

“Relax Cassidy, you don’t have anything I’ve never seen before.” Closing the door, Mom said, “Besides, it’s perfectly normal for girls your age to explore their bodies.”

“I’ve was so focused on what was happening, I never gave myself a chance to...well, explore.”

“That’s kind of why I came up here; Kat’s mother said something about a boy at the mall.” Sitting on the bed, Mom gestured for me to sit next to her. “I think now would be a good time for us to have The Talk.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: At Home

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The first thing Mom did was give me a hand mirror. "Let's start where you left off, shall we? Hold this where you can get a good look at yourself."

I did, and Mom pointed out on the mirror the different parts that made up my new sex, sometimes guiding me as I used my hands to examine myself. When she had shown me everything she could down there, I said, "Wow. I had no idea of any of this stuff when I was a boy."

"It happens."

While I got dressed, Mom continued The Talk. "Periods are different for every girl. Some get cramps, and some don't. Some retain water, and some don't. Some become irritable, and some don't. Some have cravings, and some don't. You'll learn more as time goes by. It may take a while for your periods to become regular. So, whatever happens on your period, know that it's perfectly normal."

Next came the matter of sex. She began with sexually transmitted diseases, some of which only girls get, and some of which could interfere with my having children.

Speaking of having children, that was the next chapter. A lot of things could go wrong in pregnancy, and the risks were greater for teenage girls.

When it was over, I felt better. Yes, better. My body was still different from what I'd known for 18 years, but I knew more about it now.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Talk

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
A clap of thunder woke me up just after 8:30 the next morning. Sitting up in bed, I heard the rain hitting my window and knew that my day was ruined. Well, so much for going outside. With Kat at cheer practice the whole morning, and Glenn spending the night at a friend’s house, I had no one to hang out with.

It was Monday, so Mom and Dad were back at work; which meant I was also home alone. After taking a shower, I made myself a light breakfast; after all, a girl’s got to watch her figure.

I went back up to my room and saw the bags of clothes, from yesterday, sitting outside my closet door. “I suppose I should probably put those clothes away.”

So that’s exactly what I did; well, okay, not exactly what I did. I couldn’t resist trying them on again and taking photos of myself wearing them. I must’ve taken a dozen photos, many of which I posted to Instagram. I made a note to talk to Mom about this; was it vain of me to post these pictures of myself?

After about an hour of doing that, I decided I should finally start putting the clothes away instead of simply trying them on. I was in the middle of doing that when I got a notification alert on my phone. I thought it was a breaking news alert – what’s wrong with a girl being interested in the news. Stepping away from my closet, I saw that it was an Instagram alert – someone had liked 7 of my photos in the last 5 minutes.

I didn’t recognize the username until I clicked on their photo and saw that it was Eric. Okay, that’s kind of weird. My account had barely been active for a day, how could Eric have found it, let alone why did he like so many of my photos. I couldn’t help but be a little freaked out by this; here was an 18-year-old boy practically stalking a 13-year-old girl’s Instagram page.

Sitting down on my bed, I typed out a text to Kat – Is it weird that Eric liked 7 of my Instagram posts?

I put my phone down on the bed and went back to hanging up my clothes. Given that she was at practice right now, I had a feeling Kat wouldn’t respond for a while. I was proven wrong when, about 5 minutes later, I got a text notification. As I retrieved the phone from my bed, my eyes bugged out when I saw who the text was from – Jeff.

Opening the message, I quickly realized that I’d texted him instead. We’d spent an hour or so texting each other last night, I guess his messages were still up on my phone. After I calmed down, I looked at his response – That depends, who is Eric?
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Next Day

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I stared at the phone for what felt like an eternity; hoping against all odds that this wasn’t happening. Oh god, I can’t believe I texted Jeff by mistake. I paced around the room for a moment while I figured out what I was going to do next.

I decided pretty quickly that I wasn’t going to tell Jeff the truth about me; because I was worried that would scare him away. After a moment I decided it would be best to tell the truth - only with some minor omissions.

Me:

Eric is my cousin’s best friend. I met him a few days ago,

Jeff:

Didn't you say your cousin was 18?

Me:

Yeah. Why?

Jeff:

Just seems strange that his best friend would be looking at his cousin’s Instagram profile.

It seemed that Jeff and I were on the same page; if I was Eric’s age I could understand why he was looking at my profile. But I was only 13, so it seemed a little weird that he’d be on a minor’s page. That being said, I’d known Eric for years; I doubted that he would do anything inappropriate,

Me:

Yeah it does seem weird. But I don’t think I have to worry about anything.

Jeff:

Still, I’d feel better if I knew you were safe. Do you want me to come over?

The thought of Jeff coming over did sound appealing, but I knew Dad and Mom wouldn’t be okay with it. They did know about Jeff, but I’m sure they’d want to meet him before we hung out together.

Me:

I’d love for you to come over. But I don’t think my aunt and uncle would like that very much. They haven’t met you yet.

Jeff

That’s fair. Just promise that you’ll stay safe.

I couldn’t help but feel touched that Jeff was so concerned for my safety. We’d only met yesterday, but he was already offering to make sure I was safe.

Me:

I promise I’ll stay safe. I’ll text you later.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Continue

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
After the uncomfortable incident with Eric, I found that I wasn’t interested in trying on my clothes anymore. So, after putting all my clothes away, I went downstairs to make something for lunch.

One thing I’d noticed over the last few days, even before the changes became obvious, was that my appetite had changed. Normally I would have pigged out for lunch, making a personal pizza just for myself; but now I found myself concerned about my weight and maintaining my figure. After all, I’d just spent a fortune on clothes; I’d hate for them to go to waste because I gained a few pounds.

I was sitting at the kitchen table, with my sandwich and a can of diet coke, when I heard the doorbell ring. Putting my sandwich down, I wondered who it could be; Glenn had a key to get in, and, as far as I knew, Kat was still at practice.

Walking to the door, I peeked through the window and my heart skipped a beat when I saw Eric standing there. I quickly closed the curtain and leaned against the door; thankful that he hadn’t noticed me. I stood there, frozen, as the doorbell rang again. I caught a glimpse of Eric peeking through the window, as he said, “Hello? Is anyone home?”

Oh God, what should I do?
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Lunchtime

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
As uncomfortable as I was, I decided my best option was to open the door. Even if he was being weird around me, I still owed Eric the benefit of the doubt. Taking a breath, I turned around to open the door. “Hey Eric, what brings you here?” Putting a smile on my face, I said, “You know Marcus is still in the hospital, right?”

“Yeah, I know; I just got done visiting with him.” With a smile on his face, Eric said, “He said I should come over and check on you.”

I, of course, knew that Eric was lying through his teeth; especially since I was, or at least used to be, Marcus. What I couldn’t figure out though was why Eric would lie about something so insignificant. But again I decided to give him the benefit of the doubt; so I played along. “Yeah I talked with him last night; he’s anxious to get out of there.”

“Yeah, he said the same thing to me.” Eric did not attempt to hide the fact that he was looking at my chest, Eric said, “Anyway, he suggested I come over and see how you were doing. He mentioned that you didn’t have any friends here.”

I’m starting to wonder if we should be friends. “I’ve known Kat for a few years; we’ve been hanging out together.” I quickly decided it wouldn’t be wise to tell him about Jeff, so instead, I said, “Plus Glenn has been hanging out with me when he can.”

A creepy look formed on Eric’s face; enough to freak me out. “Well if you ever decide you want some more mature friends, you can always call me.”

God, was he this creepy in High School? “Thanks, but I think I’ll stick with friends my age.” Trying to think of a reason to close the door, I said, “I hate to cut this short, but I promised my Aunt I’d take care of some laundry. I think I just heard the dryer beep.”

I tried to close the door but Eric blocked it with his feet. “You’re being rude, Cassidy; I’m trying to be your friend here.”

I was getting scared now; I’d never seen Eric like this before. As I stared at him I became keenly aware of the fact that I was now both smaller and considerably weaker than him. I looked around the area to see if there was anyone who could help, but there was no one in sight.

I was getting ready to scream for help when I heard a car door close. Peeking behind Eric, I saw _____.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: A Disturbing Conversation

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I breathed a sigh of relief when I saw Glenn walking up the driveway. I elbowed my way past Eric, yelling out, “Glenn!” I was no longer alone; Glenn would play the big brother role and protect me from Eric.

I got a brief scare, however, when Glenn waved at Eric. “Hey Eric, long time no see.” As Eric walked towards us, he said, “Did you want to come in?”

It was all I could do not to scream. “No!” feeling panicky, as close to Eric’s face as I could get, I said, “He can’t come in! He can‘t stay!”

If Glen was surprised, he hid it well. Turning to Eric, he shrugged and said, “Sorry dude, maybe some other time.”

Before he turned to leave, Eric gave me a smile that made me want to take a shower. “I’m looking forward to it.”

After Eric left, I followed Glenn inside the house; quickly closing the door behind us. Before I could walk off, Glenn stopped me and said, “What the hell was that, Cass? I thought Eric was your friend; why were you so rude to him?”

“He’s not my friend anymore; not after I found out what a creep he is!” shuddering, I said, “If I never see his face again, it’ll be too soon!”

As I walked past him, Glenn said, “Oh come on, Cass; I doubt it was that bad.”

“Believe me, it was!” turning around, I said, “Perhaps you’d only object if he spent the entire conversation staring at your tits?!"

Glenn’s face went pale. "He... He what?"

I told Glenn everything that happened since I left the hospital; from Eric’s visits to the comments on my Instagram page.
When I was finished, Glenn looked as though he was going to throw up. “How did I not realize he was such a creep?”
