“It’s going to be okay, sis.” Hugging me, Glenn said, “I’ll make sure he’ll never come near you again.”

******************************


I told Mom and Dad everything as soon as they got home; even showing some of the lewd comments on my Instagram. Dad looked as though he was going to throw up, while Mom did her best to comfort me. “Alright, here’s a new rule. From now on Cassidy is not to be left alone; someone has to stay here when she’s home and go with her when she leaves the house.”

Without hesitating, Glenn said, “I’ll make sure that bastard doesn’t get near my little sister.”

The current situation notwithstanding, I was touched that Glenn was so willing to keep me safe. Turning to me, Dad said, “I’m sorry it has to be this way, sweetheart; you were just getting to explore your new freedom, but now things are back to the way they were before.”

“Don’t worry, Daddy; I understand.” Sighing, I said, “I just hope this doesn’t go on forever.”

“You haven’t called me Daddy in a long time.” Smiling, Dad said, “It sounds lovely in your new voice.”

“In that case, I’ll keep doing it.” Kissing him on the forehead, I said, “Thanks, Daddy.”

After that everyone went about their own thing; I went upstairs to my room. I was lying on my bed when there was a knock at my door. I looked up from my phone to find Mom standing in the doorway. “Can I come in?”

Sitting up, I said, “Sure, I guess.”

Walking into the room, Mom sat next to me on the bed. “I just wanted to see how you were holding up.”

“I’m still kind of shocked about all this.” Looking at her, I said, “I can’t believe I never realized that Eric was like this."

“You’ve seen how good Hollywood makeup artists are at their work; sometimes it’s hard to tell if it’s somebody else underneath.” Placing a hand on my shoulder, Mom said, “It’s unfortunate but there are people like that in real life. You can look right at them, and not even suspect that there's someone else beneath the surface."

“Did you...you know someone like that?"

“Every woman knows someone like that, honey.”

“I can’t stop thinking about my Instagram photos.” Sighing, I said, “If I hadn’t posted them, then maybe this wouldn’t have happened.”

“You can’t go blaming yourself for this, sweetie.” Looking at me, Mom said, “Eric should have known better than to harass a minor.”

“Look at these photos, Mom.” Showing her my phone, I said, “Am...am I vain for taking all these photos of myself?”

"Are you vain for walking around everywhere?"

"That's different."

"No, it's the same. Just like you can walk now, you are now a healthy young woman. Why shouldn't you be proud of how you look? Just remember that looks aren't everything. It's what’s underneath that counts."

"Or who's underneath."
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Glenn

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I ended up going to bed early last night; after barely even touching my dinner. When I woke up the next morning I found that my phone had a few text messages from Kat and Jeff. Jeff wanted to know about Eric; so I texted back and I said I would talk to him later. Kat mentioned that she had something to tell me and that she’d be over around 8:30.

A quick look at the clock showed that it was 8:15; so I quickly got dressed and went downstairs to wait for Kat. When she came over, I let her inside as she looked at me. “Whoa, you look like hell Cass; what happened?”

“Eric happened.”

I went on to tell Kat about what happened yesterday; she had the same reaction that everyone else did. When I was finished, she said, “Wow, I would have thought he’d learned his lesson after what happened a few years ago.”

“What’s that supposed to mean?”

“It was one of those summer days where I was hanging out with you guys at your pool.” Sighing, Kat said, “I was in the bathroom, getting changed to go home; when I looked up and saw him watching me from the bathroom window.”

“What?” shocked, I said, “Why’d you never tell me about that?”

“I didn’t think you’d believe me; since he was your best friend.” Looking at me, Kat said, “I told my folks what happened, and they told me not to spend any time with Eric after that.”

After hearing that, I remembered there was a time a few years back where Kat didn’t spend any time with me for a while. I didn’t understand why at the time, but after hearing this it all became clear. “You still could have told me; at the very least it would have prepared me for what he did to me.”

“I’m sorry I didn’t tell you before now.” Sighing, Kat said, “So what did your parents say?”

“I’m not supposed to be home alone, or go out in public alone; just in case Eric gets any ideas.”

“That reminds me, I came over here for a reason.” Looking at me, Kat said, “The twin sister of one of my squadmates is on the school’s soccer team.”

“Okay.” Confused, I said, “What’s that have to do with me?”

“She said they’re having tryouts today to fill some last-minute openings in the lineup.” Pausing, Kat said, “I remember you used to be good at soccer before the accident, I thought you might want to try out.”

It’s true; I was one of the best on my middle school team. I had hoped to play in high school but the accident changed that. “I don’t know Kat; I’m still getting used to my new body.” Gesturing to my chest, I said, “My center of gravity is all off; I’m not sure I’d be any good.”

“What’s the harm in trying, Cass?” Looking at me, she said, “If you don’t make the cut then it’s no big deal; but if you do, then you’ll be back in your element.”

I thought about it for a few minutes; until I came to a decision. “Okay, I’ll do it.” Smiling, I said, “When are tryouts?”

“They start at 10:00; if you hurry and get changed we can be there in 20 minutes.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Next Day

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
There were already about a dozen girls at the field by time Kat and I got there. As we locked our bikes up, I turned to her and said, “I don’t know if I can do this, Kat. I haven’t played soccer in over four years.”

“Where is this negative attitude coming from?” looking at me, she said, “I swear you’re more uncertain now then you were during the sex change.”

That gave me pause; the more the thought about it, I realized that she was right. “Well, when you put it that way.” Chuckling, I said, “If I could get through losing my boy bits and growing breasts, I can get through this.”

Following after me as I headed towards the field, Kat said, “That’s the spirit!” smiling, she said, “Just think of it this way; it’ll help get your mind off of Eric.”

My positive mood started to dampen at the mention of Eric’s name. Turning to Kat, I said, “Please don’t mention that name.”

Realizing her mistake, Kat winced and said, “Sorry.”

As the two of us went towards the bleachers, I saw another girl come walking up to us. “Are you guys here for the tryouts?”

“She’s here for the tryouts.” Pointing to me, Kat said, “I’m just here for emotional support.”

Shaking the girl’s hand, I said, “My name’s Cassidy, and her name’s Kat.”

“It’s nice to meet you guys.” Smiling, she said, “My name’s Miranda; I just moved here last week.”

After talking for a few minutes, the three of us took some seats on the bleachers as the coach walked onto the field with several older team members.

*************************


The tryouts lasted for almost 3 hours; Coach Schmidt had everyone run four laps around the field, run countless drills, and even had us do some scrimmages. I had a rough start with the running but eventually got a good rhythm going. As for everything else, I was amazed by how quickly I remembered all of my old moves. I got more than a couple of impressed looks from the other girls.

Coach Schmidt had everyone wait by the bleachers while she consulted with the other girls. Kat and I were standing with Miranda at the bottom of the bleachers; helping ourselves to some water. “You’ve got some impressive moves, Cass; I’d be really surprised if you didn’t make the team.”

“You’re one to talk; I’ve only seen bicycle kicks like that in the World Cup!”

“It’s funny you mention that; because I went to a few soccer camps over the years.” Taking a swig of water, Miranda said, “Some of the instructors at the camp retired from the US Soccer team.”

I started feeling a little bad for Kat; she was feeling a little left out. Turning to her, I said, “What did you think of the tryouts, Kat?”

“I heard Coach Schmidt was a strict coach; but damn, that looked more exhausting than what I went through for cheerleading.” Looking at both Miranda and me, she said, “But you both looked like you were having a good time out there.”

The way I felt on the field was something I hadn’t experienced in years. “Oh yeah, I was in the zone!”

“Plus, if nothing else, I’ll bet it took your mind off of Eric for a while.”

I briefly glared at Kat; I knew she only meant it in a positive way, but I wasn’t going to let Eric ruin this for me. Composing myself, although still annoyed, I said, “That it did.”

“I don’t mean to pry, but who’s Eric?” looking confused, Miranda said, “Is he an ex or something?”

Before I could stop myself, I spit the water out of my mouth; thankfully it didn’t hit Miranda or Kat. “Eric is not now, or ever will be, my boyfriend.” Turning to Miranda, I said, “He’s my cousin’s 18-year-old best friend; he’s been stalking me since I moved to town.”

I then went on to tell Miranda about what Eric had done. After I was finished, she said, “My dad’s a cop; if you want he can help you get a restraining order or something.”

“Thanks, but I don’t think it’ll go that far.” Sighing, I said, “My uncle and my cousin are going to pay him a visit tonight.”

Coach Schmidt walked back onto the field at that moment; calling everyone to gather around her. “You all did an excellent job today, girls.” Pausing, she said, “Unfortunately I only have 5 open spots on the team, so not all of you will make the team this year.”

I flashed Miranda a hopeful smile as Coach Schmidt read off the names of those who made the team; I wasn’t surprised when both Miranda and I made the team, although I felt sorry for the girls who didn’t. I thought I saw a few of them start crying as they walked off.

As I turned to leave with Kat, Miranda came up to us and said, “I hope I’m not being too awkward or anything, especially since we just met.” Pausing, she said, “But I was wondering if you guys wanted to see a movie tonight.”

Kat and I looked at each other; Kat nodded in agreement. Turning to Miranda, I said, “I don’t see why not; we’ve got nothing going on tonight.”

“Great!” smiling, Miranda said, “Why don’t you guys give me your numbers, I’ll text you later with the information.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: At Tryouts

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The two of us got back to the house about 15 minutes later; after making plans to meet up before the movie, we both went our separate ways. I was putting my bike away in the garage when my cell phone started to ring. Digging it out of my purse, I smiled when I saw that it was Jeff calling.

Answering the call, I said, “Hey Jeff.”

“Hey, Cassidy.” Pausing, he said, “Maybe I’m wrong, but I swear I saw you and Kat at the soccer fields.”

“Nope, that was us; I was trying out for the soccer team.” Chuckling, I said, “Big surprise, I made the team.”

“That’s great!”

As I let myself into the kitchen, I said, “What were you doing in that neck of the woods?” blushing a little bit, I said, “Were you
following me or something?”

“No nothing like that; I’m not some creep like your cousin’s friend.” Pausing, Jeff said, “My mom and I were dropping off some paperwork with the office; my vaccination records and some other stuff.”

“It’s funny you mention Eric because I had another problem with him yesterday.” Grabbing a can of soda from the fridge, I leaned against the counter and said, “He stopped by to visit, saying my cousin suggested he come check on me. He was being a total creep, doing nothing but stare at my chest the entire time.”

“Are you serious?” anger in his voice, Jeff said, “Did he do anything to you?”

I’ll admit that I was touched by Jeff’s concern for me; it made him all the more attractive. “He probably would have done something had my cousin Glenn not come home.” Sighing, I said, “Long story short, someone has to be home with me at all times and I can’t go out in public without someone with me. My uncle and cousin are going to pay a visit tonight; see if maybe they’ll scare him off.”

“I hope it works; but if it makes you feel better, I’m more than willing to look out for you.”

“That’s sweet, Jeff; but we just met.” Pausing, I said, “With everything going on right now, I don’t think having a cute guy following me around is the best idea.”

“I guess that’s fair...wait, did you say I was cute?”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Back Home

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The thoughts going through my head at that moment, as I dropped the can of soda to the floor, could only be summarized with the words - Oh crap.

Okay so crap wasn't the word I was thinking, but it got the point across. Oh crap, oh crap, oh crap, oh crap, oh crap, oh crap.

Regaining a semblance of composure, I said, “Oh please, I use the word cute all the time.” blushing, I said, “You know; like that’s a cute top, that puppy’s cute, or that’s a really cute car.”

“Apparently you also use it when talking about guys.”

“Exactly, it doesn’t mean anything. Now when I say that something is darling that’s high praise coming from me.” Grabbing some paper towels, I said, “But for the record, if I’m being completely honest, I do think you’re kind of cute.”

“I guess I can live with that.” Between laughs, Jeff said, “By the way, I think you’re kind of cute too.”

And just like that Oh crap was kicked out of my head by He thinks I'm cute. With the paper towels in my hand, I couldn’t help but smile. A boy that I liked thought I was cute; and to think that, just a few days earlier, I’d been a boy myself.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Panic Mode

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
After talking with Jeff for a few more minutes, and cleaning up my spilled soda, I quickly ran over to Kat’s house. I swear I came close to breaking the doorbell by pressing it so much. Barely a minute passed before Kat appeared at the door. “What’s going on, Cass?” stepping outside, Kat said, “Is it Eric?”

I wasn’t going to let that name dampen my good mood. “Jeff said he thinks I’m cute.”

That wasn’t what Kat expected me to say. “Wait...what?” looking at me, Kat said, “He said that?”

I then went into detail about how Jeff called to say hi and how that conversation went. Once I was finished, I said, “It all happened because I slipped up and said he was cute.” Giggling, I said, “I was so afraid I’d scared him off, but he thinks I’m cute too!”

“I don’t want to burst your bubble, but don’t you think you’re moving too quickly here?” sighing, Kat said, “You’ve come a long way in a short time, but I don’t think you’re ready to start dating.”

“Oh please, you’re one to talk.” Scoffing, I said, “I barely had time to recover from the procedure before the two of us were making
out.”

“Oh wow, I can’t believe you’re bringing that up.” Looking at me, stunned, Kat said, “You were just as much a part of that as I was.”

“I never said I wasn’t a part of it; I’m just saying don’t hold it against me if I get a little excited about a guy taking an interest in me.” Pausing, I looked at Kat and said, “Do I know if Jeff and I will work out? No, I don’t; but he’s the first guy outside my family who treats me like a girl.”

“I’m not trying to put a damper on any of this, I’m just looking out for you.” Embracing me, Kat said, “You’re my best friend and I don’t want to see you get hurt.”

“Then why can’t you at least pretend to be happy for me?”

“If that’s what you want, then I’ll do it.” Sighing, Kat said, “Jeff is a nice guy, I’m happy to hear that he likes you.”

“Seems kind of forced, but I’ll take what I can get.” Smiling, I said, “I’ll see you tonight for the movie.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Talking to Kat

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
My mom dropped Kat and me at the movies just before 6:30; after thanking her, we made our way inside to wait for Miranda. As it turned out, we didn’t have to wait long for her; as she walked out of the bathroom as we entered.

“Hey guys!” coming over to hug both of us, Miranda said, “I’m so glad you guys could make it.”

After we’d finished with the pleasantries, we bought tickets for a romantic comedy starring Tom Holland. I wasn’t sure that I was ready for something like that, but I decided to play along for Miranda and Kat. Besides, what girl doesn’t like a romantic comedy starring a cute British actor?

As we walked into the theater and took our seats, Miranda said, “So did anything interesting happen after tryouts?”

“Funny you should ask that because something did happen after I got home.” Giggling, I said, “A boy I’m interested in called and asked how I was doing; long story short, I accidentally let slip that I thought he was cute and then he said the same thing.”

“Damn girl, you’ve only been in town a couple of weeks and you’ve already got a potential boyfriend.”

“I wouldn’t go that far, Miranda.” Looking at me, trying to hide her annoyance, Kat said, “I think Cass is going too fast; she only just got out of her last relationship.”

“Oh please, that wasn’t even a real relationship.” Sighing, I said, “All we did was make out; we never went on any dates.”

“Maybe there would have been some dates, but life had other plans.” Staring at me, behind Miranda’s back, Kat said, “Like, I don’t know, realizing that you were attracted to boys?”

I was getting annoyed by this point; glaring at Kat while Miranda looked on in confusion. “Wait, were you two...”

“As Cassidy said, all we did was make out.” Turning towards the screen, Kat said, “Things went south after she realized she liked boys more than girls.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: At the Movies

choices: The Next Day (Hanging out with Jeff)
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The movie wasn’t really that good; in fact, the only memorable part for me was seeing Tom Holland walking around shirtless. But it was an excuse to hang out with friends and it got my mind off Eric.

After the movie was finished, I felt the need to use the bathroom. I had just finished in the stall and was walking to the sink when I saw Miranda come out of another stall on the end. As she came up next to me, I noticed she was giving me a weird look. It was the same look I’d seen her giving me a few times during the movie.

“Is something on your mind, Miranda?” looking at her, I said, “You’ve been giving me weird looks all night.”

“My bad; I didn’t know I was making you uncomfortable.” Grabbing some paper towels, she said, “It’s just...well, I was wondering if you still liked girls to some extent.”

One thing I’d noticed about Miranda was that she got right to the point. Looking at her, I said, “Why do you want to know?”

“I don’t mean to be weird or anything; but at my last school, some of my girlfriends and I would experiment with each other.” Blushing slightly, Miranda said, “I was just curious if that was something you and Kat were doing.”

“I was still learning about who I was, as a girl, when I was with Kat.” Sighing, I said, “It taught me a lot, but one of the biggest lessons was that I’m firmly attracted to guys.”

“Oh.” Looking disappointed, Miranda said, “That’s unfortunate because I’m still at that point where I’m trying to figure out who I am.”

“I don’t know if this will help, but perhaps you should talk to Kat.” Sighing, I said, “I think she’s dealing with the same issues that you are; perhaps you can help each other.”

“I suppose it’s worth a shot; the worst that could happen is she turns me down.” Looking at me, Miranda said, “I hope this doesn’t turn you off to being friends.”

“Why would it?” smiling, I said, “I like you, Miranda; just not in that way.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: After the Movie

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I rode with Mom to practice the next morning since as it was on her way to work. After saying goodbye to her, and grabbing my bike from the back of the car, I started walking towards the athletic field. I looked around but didn’t see anyone else; until I remembered that Coach said to meet in the locker room.

After locking my bike at the bike rack, I walked inside the school and made my way to the girl’s locker room. I stopped outside the door briefly; as I thought about how, just last week, I’d been a guy. Life can change in pretty drastic ways, I guess.

Walking into the locker room, I saw most of the other girls were huddled together in small groups. I figured they must’ve all known each other by the way they were talking. I felt a little depressed by the thought that I hardly knew anyone on the team; in fact, all of these girls had been in elementary school the last time I was this age.

I walked over to an empty locker and placed my purse inside. I was already wearing my new uniform, so I didn’t need to worry about changing. With no one to talk to, I hung around my locker until I saw Miranda walk into the locker room.

I was about to run over and say hi when I saw that she looked a little pale. Concerned for her, I walked over and said, “Hey, are you feeling alright?”

“Not exactly.” Leaning closer to whisper, Miranda said, “It’s that time of the month and I’m out of tampons.”

I thought back to the conversation I’d had with Mom; about always having extra tampons in case you or someone else needed them. “Come with me, I’ve got a few I can give you.” Grabbing Miranda’s hand, I said, “I just need to leave some extras for myself, because it’s my time of the month too.”


After I handed her three of the seven tampons I had, Miranda said, “Thanks Cass, you’re a lifesaver.”

“That’s what friends are for, Miranda; we look out for each other.”

Miranda then went off to the bathroom to take care of business; walking back in just as Coach Schmidt entered the locker area. “Okay girls, huddle up.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Next Day (The Locker Room)

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I get the feeling that most of the girls, including me, thought that tryouts had been tough. But we quickly learned how much tougher practices would be. After a rigorous 10 minute warm-up, consisting of stretches and jogging in place, Coach Schmidt called us over to her.

“Okay, girls; after reviewing your performances from tryouts, I’m pretty much settled on what positions you’ll be playing during the season.” After a brief pause, to look at some notes, she said, “But before I give that information out; just because I think you’ll be good in a certain position doesn’t mean I don’t want you to learn other positions.”

There was some grumbling from some of the veteran team members; likely worried that their position on the team was at stake. That didn’t bother me though; I just came here to play. It took a little while but eventually, everyone was given their assignments.

Because of my speed and agility, I’d expected to be one of the defenders; so you can imagine my surprise when I learned that I was going to be center forward, while Miranda was going to be one of the strikers. Once again there were grumbles from some of the veteran players; about novices taking such high-value positions.

While Miranda and I took it all in stride, we both wanted to understand why we’d been given those positions. Walking up to Coach Schmidt, after the others went off to get some water before starting drills, I said, “Coach Schmidt; we were wondering why you gave us such important positions. It’s not that we’re ungrateful, it’s just that we just joined the team.”

“I’d never seen such raw skill before, it’s like you two were born to be on that field.” Looking at us, Coach Schmidt said, “The way you girls worked together during the scrimmages; it was like you were reading each other’s minds.”

“That’s funny you say that, Coach.” Gesturing to me, Miranda said, “Because we only just met that morning before tryouts.”

“That’s even more impressive.” Smiling, Coach Schmidt said, “If you two can keep working together like that, then we’ll have a great season.”

Miranda and I thanked Coach Schmidt before walking off to join the others for drills. While we were walking, Miranda looked at me and said, “I didn’t think we worked together that well during tryouts; but I guess we must’ve impressed her.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Practice

choices: Later On (A Run In With Eric)
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
As soon as practice was over, everyone went back to the locker room for some much-needed showers. Other than being all sweaty and stinky, I felt great. I’d done more in two hours of practice then I’d been able to do for years.

Since there weren’t enough showers for everyone to go at once, we had to split into groups. I got into the second group while Miranda got into the first. As I stood in line, wearing nothing but a towel, I couldn’t help but notice the irony. For about as long as I’d been in a wheelchair, I’d fantasized about being in a shower surrounded by girls. Now that it was happening, it wasn’t a big deal to me; since I was just another girl.

I was waiting for my turn to shower when one of the older girls, Melissa, came up to me. She was one of the girls who’d grumbled about me getting the center forward position. Seeing the look on her face, I was worried she was going to yell at me. “You know I was center forward last season; I was pretty annoyed that Coach gave it to a rookie.”

“I’m sorry if I took your position, that wasn’t my intent.”

“Seeing you in action on the field, I can see why Coach gave you the position.” Sighing, a weak smile appearing on her face, Melissa said, “I’ve never seen anyone with that kind of raw talent before.”

“Thanks, I guess.” Pausing, I said, “So there are no hard feelings about me getting the position?"

“Not in the least.” Shaking my hand, Melissa said, “Welcome to the team, Cassidy.”

********************************

After coming out of the showers, I heard one new girl say, “If the practices are going to be this tough, I can only imagine what the games will be like. Maybe I made a mistake trying out.”

“You’re looking at it the wrong way.” Walking up to her, I said, “Because we’re working so hard in practice, the games will be easier. Each of us knows what the others are capable of, and that’s what will make us the team to beat.”

Once I toweled off and changed back into my clothes, I walked outside to find Kat and Miranda standing by the bike rack. Judging from how close together they were, I felt like they must’ve been talking with each other. My suspicions were confirmed when Kat put her hand on Miranda's shoulder.

It was only as I walked over to them that I realized how stupid it was to wear my practice outfit to practice. Now I had to ride home in sweaty clothes, which meant showering all over again as soon as I got home.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: After Practice (by Beejay)

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
Walking towards us, Jeff said, “So how did practice go?” smiling, Jeff said, “Did you make any new...oh, hey Cassidy.”

I was blushing at that point; as Jeff was the last person I expected to run into. “H-Hey Jeff.”

“Okay, hold on a second.” Looking at me, Miranda said, “How do you and my brother know each other?”

“We met here a few days ago; over in the food court actually.” Pausing, I said, “He surprised me by offering me tampons when the tide came in.”

“Speaking of which, these are for you.” Handing Miranda a bag from the pharmacy, Jeff continued, “You really need to get better about remembering these things; I’m willing to help, but I do get a lot of strange looks when I’m buying tampons.”

“I’ll keep that in mind.” Handing me three tampons from the pack, Miranda said, “Perhaps you should remember to tell me when you meet someone.”

“I’ll second that; you could have told me you had a sister.” Chuckling, I said, “Because you were so forthcoming with other details."

“I bet I can guess which details they were.”

******************************

After Kat finally caught up with us, the four of us took a seat in the food court. None of us ate anything; we just talked and got to know each other a little better, although I made sure not to tell them that I’d been born a guy.

Once the conversation started to die down, Jeff said, “So what does everyone want to do next?”

Looking at her brother, Miranda said, “Didn’t you say something about needing a new watch?”

Jeff had been largely oblivious to it; but I’d noticed Kat and Miranda giving each other looks for the last 20 minutes. My curiosity was peaked when Kat, nodding, said, “What a coincidence, I think Cassidy needs a new watch as well.”

“Okay then, so it’s settled.” Looking at Jeff and me, perhaps a little too eager, Miranda said, “You two go look at watches, and we’ll catch up with you later.”

As the two of us got up and started walking away, I looked back to find Kat and Miranda making a hasty retreat towards the exit. With a park across the street, I had a feeling I knew where they were going. Catching a glimpse of them, Jeff turned to me and said, “If I didn’t know better, I’d say those two wanted to be alone.”

“If you did know better, you’d realize that’s exactly what they wanted.”

It took a moment for Jeff to process what I was saying; but once he did, he said, “Some brother I am; I can’t believe I never saw that.” Stepping onto the escalator, “What do we do about it?”

“The way I see it, we could be a couple of busybodies. Or, we could have some alone time of our own.” Smiling, somewhat suggestively, I said, “Which idea sounds better to you?”

“You’ve made your point.” Smiling, Jeff said, “What do you say we go buy me a new watch; and then spend some time together, just the two of us.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Hanging Out At The Mall

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
"How did practice go?" Jeff asked. "Make any new... Oh, hi, Cassidy."

"Hi, Jeff," I replied. "This is my friend and neighbor, Kat."

"Wait a second," Miranda said. "How do you know my brother?"

"We met here at the mall," I explained. "Here in the food court, in fact. He surprised me by offering me tampons when the tide came in."

"Speaking of which," Jeff said, and handed Miranda a bag from the pharmacy. "Sorry I didn't tell you more about the girl I met."

"Like my name? Or telling me hers? 'Cause you were forthcoming with other details."

"Let me guess which," Miranda said, handing me three tampons.

******************************


After that, the four of us settled into a real foursome. We shared more details about ourselves (within reason: I was pretty sure they weren't ready to hear that I had been born a guy), and we were having a good time.

"So," Jeff asked, "what's next?"

"Hmmm," Miranda said, "didn't you say you wanted to look at the watches?"

"What a coincidence," Kat said, "Cassidy needs a new watch, too."

"Why don't you two go look at watches," Miranda said, "and we'll catch up with you later."

As Jeff and I went off to look at watches, he said, "If I didn't know any better, I'd say that those two wanted to be alone."

"If you did know better," I replied, "you'd know that's exactly what they want."

"Really? Miranda? I didn't know she was like that. What do we do about it?"

"Well, we could be a couple of busybodies. Or, we could take the opportunity to be alone together ourselves. Which sounds better to you?"

"Good point. Let's go look at watches, and forget that they came along for a while."
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Surprise

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
After helping Jeff pick out a new watch, the two of us went back to the food court and got some ice cream. As we took our seats in a quiet area, I said, “I want to apologize for the other day; if I put you on the spot with the whole cute thing.”

“No need to apologize.” Chuckling, Jeff said, “I can’t help if girls find me irresistible.”

“You’re cute; but not irresistible.” Smiling, I said, “If I had to choose between, say, you and Tom Holland; I’d go with Tom Holland.”

I guess I must have a thing for Tom Holland. “I wouldn’t have a problem with that all; Tom Holland’s a good looking guy.”

I raised an eyebrow at that remark. “Is there something else you’re not telling me, Jeff?”

“Just because I’m straight doesn’t mean I can’t think another guy is good looking.” Looking at me, Jeff said, “While we’re on the subject though; I’d totally choose Alexandra Daddario over you.”

I couldn’t blame him for that; I had a crush on Alexandra Daddario when I was a boy. Thinking back to how I acted at the restaurant, I was in no position to chide him for thinking a guy was good looking.

After going back to my ice cream for a few minutes, I started to get a nagging feeling in the back of my mind. “It’s fairly obvious that we both like each other; I mean we both think the other is cute.” Putting my ice cream down, I turned to Jeff and said, “I’m just curious when you’re going to finally ask me on a date.”

“I have every intention of asking you out. Cassidy; it’s just...I’m not ready to do it yet.” Sighing, Jeff looked at me and said, “Don’t get me wrong; I think you one of the most amazing girls I’ve ever met.”

I couldn’t help but smile; I’ve only known Jeff a few days and already he thinks I’m cute and an amazing girl. “Well please let me know when you’re finally ready.” Smiling, I said, “Because I can’t very well say yes to a date if you don’t ask me first.”

“You know, we should probably go find Kat and Miranda.” Getting up, Jeff said, “Lord knows what they’re doing right now.”

With that, we left the mall and walked across the street. After a few minutes of searching, we spotted Kat and Miranda under a nearby tree. It wasn’t until we got closer that we noticed they were making out. I couldn’t help but feel a tinge of jealousy; Kat and I were doing that only a few days before, only 50 feet away.

Before I could stop him, Jeff walked over and tapped Miranda’s foot. “You two shouldn’t be doing that here; this is a public park after all.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Alone with Jeff

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
Well it’s finally the weekend; which meant it was time for my family’s end of the summer BBQ. My family was due to start arriving around 11:00; so we could get the obligatory conversations about my new gender out of the way. Miranda and Jeff said they’d come by around noon with their family.

Speaking of Miranda; I suppose it’s only fair to say that she and Kat were in a relationship. After we came across them making out in the park; it seemed that every time they were together, they’d find a way to start making out. I did feel in a tinge of jealously; after all, it wasn’t long ago that I was in Miranda’s position.

While I was happy for them; I was beginning to suspect that Miranda may be a lesbian. I couldn’t know for certain; but some of the things she’s said, and her behavior, seemed to point in that direction. Don’t get me wrong, I was 100% supportive; after all, I was a straight girl who used to be a straight boy.

Getting back to the topic at hand, I had just finished getting dressed for the party when there was a knock at my door. “Come in.”

The door opened and Dad stepped inside. Looking at me, he said, “I thought you’d want to know; your cousin Rachel is here with her boyfriend.”

Rachel, as you know, was my trans cousin; I’d only just learned about her boyfriend last night. It seems that he was trans as well; they met during a support group for trans teens and I guess they hit it off. “Thanks, daddy, I’ll be right down.”

I must’ve looked worried about something; because Dad walked over and hugged me. Kissing my head, he said, “You’ve got nothing to worry about, pumpkin; everyone here understands that you didn’t ask for this to happen.” After a brief pause, he said, “I’m sure there will be some inquisitive looks, but you don’t have to worry about them misjudging you.”

“Thanks, Daddy.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Day of the Party

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
If there was any doubt about how Rachel would feel about my new identity, she put those to rest the moment I walked into the family room. Coming over, she hugged the stuffing out of me and said, “It’s so good to see you, cousin.” Smiling, as she stepped back, she said, “Now turn around, girl; let me get a good look at you."

So what did I do? I turned around and relished the looks I got from both Rachel and her boyfriend. Turning back towards Rachel, hands on my waist, I said, “What do you think?”

“You hit the jackpot, Cass; my only wish is that I look half that good.” Smiling, Rachel said, “You’ve got everything the boys
like, and in all the right places.”

I already knew that I looked good; but it didn’t hurt to have someone else say it. Looking at Rachel, I said, “Oh please; girl, you look great.”

While not as far along as me, Rachel did look great; possessing the body equivalent to that of a 12-year-old girl. Rachel tried to disregard what I said, but her boyfriend stepped up to me and said, “I’ve been telling her that for months; she’s constantly looking at teen celebs and wishing that she had their figure. I keep telling her that it all takes time; for both of us.” Extending his hand, the boyfriend said, “I’m Tommy Robbins.”

“Cassidy Scott.” Shaking his hand, I said, “It’s nice to meet you.”

If I didn't know better, I would have believed that Tommy was just a boy who hadn't started puberty yet. He still had a bit of a feminine face, but it wasn't easily noticeable. His brown hair was in a buzz cut. Stepping back to stand next to Rachel, Tommy said, “Same here; Rachel’s told me all about you.”

That raised some eyebrows; turning to her, I said, “Did she now?”

Putting her hands up, insisting, she said, “I only told him what I felt was appropriate.”

I suppose I walked right into that one, didn’t I?

******************************


So that was how I spent a half-hour telling Tommy about the experimental medicine that changed me from a paralyzed 17-year-old boy to a 13-year-old girl on the soccer team. “I suppose it’s fair to say that I was a little nervous about this get-together.” Pausing, looking at both Tommy and Rachel, I said, “I was worried about how my situation might look, especially when compared to what you two are going through.”

“I’ll admit that I was a tad jealous when I first heard; but it’s not like you asked for this to happen.” Sighing, Rachel said, “All you wanted was to be able to walk again; becoming a girl in the process...that must’ve been a scary experience.”

“You’re not kidding; first I was acting weird and then one morning I woke up to find that I’d started to grow breasts.” Taking a breath, I said, “By the way; do either of you know anyone who needs a chest binder? I bought one back when I was trying to hide them from the public.”

Neither of them knew anyone, so I dropped the subject. “The important thing, Cassidy, is that you’re healthy and happy.” Looking at me, Tommy said, “Trust me when I say it’s not good to hide who you are.”

“On that note; some people are coming who don’t know about Marcus.” Pausing, I said, “I’d like to keep it that way; until I’m ready to tell them.”

“We understand; don’t worry, we won’t dead name you.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Going Downstairs

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The party went off without a hitch; although I was unable to avoid all the questions my family members had. I had the feeling a few of my older relatives weren’t 100% of me or even Rachel for that matter; but if they weren’t they kept it to themselves.

The rest of the guests started arriving just before noon; first was Kat and her folks, followed by Miranda, Jeff, and their parents. As I walked up to greet them, I noticed that Kat and Miranda were keeping their distance from each other. At first I wondered if they’d had a fight or something; until Jeff pulled me off to the side. “Before you ask, Miranda hasn’t come out to my parents yet; she plans on doing it today, though.”

After agreeing to keep my mouth shut, I walked over and introduced myself to Jeff and Miranda’s parents. Looking me over, their Mom said, “Our children have told us a lot about you, Cassidy.” Smiling, she said, “Miranda says you’re a great soccer player, while Jeff can’t stop talking about how beautiful you are.”

Jeff and I both started blushing upon hearing that. Glaring at his Mom, Jeff said, “Mom; please, you’re embarrassing me.”

Pushing my embarrassment aside, I said, “It’s nice to meet you, Mrs. Anderson.”

************************


While the adults were off talking with each other, and Glenn was playing basketball out front with some of our male cousins, I found a secluded spot of the yard to hang out with my group.

Kat and the others had questions for Rachel and Tommy, but it wasn’t anything they weren’t willing to answer. Most of the questions revolved around their relationship; with Kat asking about how they met. “It’s not the most romantic story in the world; I was running late for a support group meeting.” Blushing, Rachel said, “It was raining and the floor was wet; I must’ve slipped because the next thing I knew, I was lying on top of Tommy.”

“I couldn’t be angry with her since I’d just slipped a few minutes earlier.” Chuckling, Tommy said, “One thing led to another; and we started dating.”

“I understand if it’s too personal; but part of me wants to know.” Looking at both Rachel and Tommy, Jeff said, “I know it’s still early, but have you two decided whether or not you’re going to go all the way?”

“You mean to transition?” looking at Tommy, Rachel said, “I think I speak for both of us when I say all we want is to be our correct selves. So, yes; we plan on transitioning when the time comes.”

The topic turned towards the coming school year for a moment, until Miranda said, “I have kind of a loaded question for you guys. If it wasn’t already obvious, Kat and I are dating.” Pausing, Miranda said, “I haven’t come out to my parents yet; and I was wondering if you guys had any advice.”

“Every person’s situation is different; so any advice we give might not be appropriate for you.” Looking at Rachel, Tommy said, “My parents knew from an early age that I was never really a girl; so when I told them, they weren’t surprised at all.”

“My situation was less than stellar.” Sighing, Rachel said, “My mom’s been nothing but supportive, while my father’s been the exact opposite. He thinks there’s something mentally wrong with me.”

“No offense, Rachel; but I hope my situation is more like Tommy’s than yours.” Turning her attention to Kat, Miranda said, “Should we do this now?”

“I don’t see why not.” Getting up, Kat said, “Maybe after we can all relax in the pool.”

Giving Kat a kiss, Miranda said, “Sounds like a plan to me.”

The rest of us watched, silently, as Kat and Miranda walked over to the grill; where Miranda’s parents were talking with my Dad.
As we watched, I heard Rachel said, “I certainly hope this is better than what happened with me.”

While the rest of us watched, Miranda cleared her throat and said, “Mom, Dad; can we talk?”

Taking the hint, Dad walked away; leaving the four of them alone by the grill. Looking at her daughter, Miranda’s mom said, “What’s on your mind, sweetie?”

“Mom, Dad...” taking Kat’s hand in hers, Miranda said, “I’d like you to meet Kat.”

Both her parents smiled and shook Kat’s free hand. “It’s a pleasure to meet you, dear.”

Taking a deep breath, Miranda said, “Kat and I are together.”

“Of course, sweetie.” Turning to Kat, Mrs. Anderson said, “Why don’t you tell us about yourself, Kat?”

It appeared as though they weren’t grasping what Miranda was saying, so she tried again. “No, Mom; I mean...we’re together.”

“Of course, sweetie; we’ve known for a while.” Smiling, both parents said, “We’re just glad to see that you’re happy. Kat seems like a wonderful young lady.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Cookout

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
As it turned out, Miranda’s parents had known for a while that she was interested in girls; they were just waiting for her to realize it. They were incredibly supportive and had nothing but nice things to say about Kat. Kat’s parents came over after a few minutes; and both families decided to have dinner at some point to get to know each other.

After everything was taken care of, we all decided it was time to hang out in the pool. Everyone else had already changed into their bathing suits, with Tommy and Jeff borrowing suits from Glenn; while I was still up in my room – eyeing the bikini on my bed.

It was the same one from the mall; the one that Kat tried to get me to purchase. It seemed that Kat had purchased it behind my back to give it to me as a gift. I told her I appreciated the sentiment, but that I didn’t think I was ready to wear it just yet.

Wrapped in nothing but a towel, I was still staring at the suit when Kat walked into my room. “Oh come on, Cass; you’ve been staring at that thing for 20 minutes.”

“I told you, Kat; I’m not ready to wear a two-piece just yet.”

Closing the bedroom door behind her, Kat said, “What does it say about you; if Rachel’s willing to wear one but you’re not?” standing next to me, Kat said, “If you’re worried about how everyone will react; I can assure you that no one will react badly.”

“That’s not what I’m worried about.” With a sigh, I turned to Kat and said, “I’m just afraid that once I put it on, that’s the final nail in the coffin for my life as Marcus.”

“Don’t you think you’re overthinking it a little?” with a chuckle, Kat said, “Personally, I would’ve thought having your first period would be the final nail in the coffin.”

The more I stood there and thought about it, the more I realized Kat was right. I’d been a girl for almost a week now; my life as Marcus was fading into the distance. After taking a deep breath, I said, “You’re right, I’m being silly about this whole thing.” Grabbing the suit of the bed, I said, “Besides, I want to see the look on Jeff’s face when he sees me in this.”

Calling out from behind as I walked towards the door, Kat said, “I’m actually more interested in how Glenn’s going to react.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Cassidy Feels Trepidatious

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
Jeff’s reaction, as I walked out onto the patio with Kat, was everything that I’d hope it would be. He stood there, mouth agape, with a look in his eyes like he’d died and gone to heaven. Glenn was staring at me as well; until Miranda came up and slapped him on the back of the head. “Hey dummy, that’s your sister.”

Once the reactions were out of the way, I dove straight into the pool; enjoying the fact that I could swim for the first time in years. I could’ve easily swum all day; but limited myself to five laps before stretching out on a deck chair.

After a few minutes, as the others were swimming around, Rachel came over and sat next to me. “I must say, you looked really comfortable in there.” Gesturing to my suit, she said, “You look amazing in that, by the way.”

“You can thank Kat for that; she bought it behind my back. I wasn’t going to wear it, but then I realized I’d the only girl wearing a one-piece.” Chuckling, I said, “Besides, it’s not like I’ve got a figured to be ashamed of..”

“Damn right, girl.” walking over, with next to her, Miranda said, “Don’t you think this deserves a little commemoration?”

Kat chuckled and gave Miranda a kiss on the cheek. “You read my mind, gorgeous.”

And so that’s how Kat, Miranda, Rachel and I huddled together on the pool deck; while Tommy took some photos. After checking the photos, and thanking Tommy, I posted the best one on Instagram; with the caption – Hangin with my girls #squad.

I’d no sooner posted it when a drunken voice came from the side yard. “Where's my son?”

I knew instantly what was happening; even as Tommy ran to Rachel’s side. With some concern in his voice, Jeff said, “What’s going on?”

“That would be my dad.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Pool Time

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
Everyone turned around to find Jeremy Lewis, Rachel’s father, stagger through the gate into the backyard. I’d known Uncle Jeremy for most of my life; as he and my dad’s sister, Amy, got married when I was 3 years old. But in all that time, I’d never seen him like this.

“I said, where’s my damn son?” Slurring his speech, Jeremy said, “So help me, he better not be wearing some stupid sissy outfit.”

I looked over towards Rachel, to see that his comment had visibly hurt her. Rachel was finally becoming who she was meant to be, I couldn’t understand why Uncle Jeremy was acting like this. Composing herself, Rachel stormed over and stopped right in front of her father. “I’m right here, daddy.”

“What the hell are you doing wearing that, Kyle?” trying to forcibly remove the bikini top, Jeremy said, “No son of mine is going to wear something like that.”

“I’m not your son, Daddy; I never was.” Pointing to her mom, who was being held back by my Mom and Mr. Maddox, Miranda said, “Mom’s accepted me for who I am; why can’t you? I’m finally happy for the first time in my life.”

Turning his anger towards Amy, Jeremy said, “You did this, you little bitch; you took my son away from me.” Stepping towards her, Jeremy said, “So help me, I’m going to make sure you never see Kyle again.”

Before Jeremy could get within five feet of Amy, my Dad stepped in front of him and said, “You better get out of here, Jeremy; before you do something you’ll regret.”

“Fuck you, Kent.” Jabbing Dad in the chest with his finger, Jeremy said, “You’re no better than your sorry excuse for a sister; you couldn’t even keep your son alive.”

Now it was my turn to get angry; I stormed over to him and said, “How dare you speak that way about my cousin. Marcus and Uncle Kent are 10x the men you’ll ever be.”

That turned out to be the worst thing I could do; because, before I could even react, Jeremy punched me in the nose and sent me flying backward onto the patio. As my head slammed against the patio, I saw Jeff tackle Jeremy to the ground and start beating on him repeatedly.

The last thing I remembered before blacking out was Tommy and Glenn trying to pull Jeff off of Jeremy.
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Confrontation

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
It didn’t take long for the cops to arrive and arrest Uncle Jeremy; he tried to maintain his innocence, but the cops weren’t falling for it. They interviewed everyone there; and they all said the same thing, Jeremy had assaulted me and Jeff had been defending me.

The party slowly started back up again after the cops and EMTs left; even if everyone was still on edge. With a bandage covering a decent-sized gash on my head, I walked over to where Jeff was nursing a fractured wrist. Taking a seat next to him, I said, “I appreciate what you did back there; I just wish you didn’t get hurt in the process.”

“It was bad enough how he talked smack about his daughter; I was about ready to escort him out with his arm twisted backward for that.” Sighing, Jeff said, “But then I lost all control when he hit you.”

“It’s nice to know that chivalry isn’t dead.” Giving Jeff a quick peck on the cheek, I said, “I appreciate that you came to my defense like that.”

I could tell that my quick peck on the cheek surprised Jeff; if his blushing was any indication. It was becoming obvious that he was attracted me to. Looking at me, Jeff said, “I’d do it again in a heartbeat, Cass.”

At that moment, Rachel and Tommy walked over to us; after giving Jeff another peck on the cheek, Rachel said, “I’m sorry that you had to do that, Jeff; I knew my dad was having a hard time with me being transgender, I just never realized it would come to this.”

“You don’t need that kind of negativity in your life, Rachel.” Gesturing to Tommy, Jeff said, “So long as you have a boyfriend and mother who support you, that’s all you need.”

“You’re a great guy, Jeff; I can see why Cassidy is so enamored with you.” Ignoring my dagger eyes, Rachel continued, “If I didn’t already have a boyfriend, I think Cassidy would have some competition.”

After Rachel and Tommy walked off, Jeff looked at me and said, "Why does it seem like everyone is trying to get us together?"

"I haven't the slightest; although I can't say I blame them." turning to Jeff, smiling, I said, "After all, I think we'd make a very cute couple."

"I'm all for us dating, Cass; but I think we should get to know each other a bit more." sighing, Jeff said, "What if there's something about one of us that the other doesn't like?"

Like that I was born a guy?
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Aftermath

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
The party eventually came to an end a few hours later; while Amy would be staying over with Rachel and Tommy, everyone else left. That was, of course, after I made plans to go to the beach the next day with Kat, Miranda, and Rachel.

After a quiet dinner, we were all pretty exhausted so everyone decided to go to bed early. Rachel and I would be in my room, while Tommy would sleep in Glenn’s room. If Glenn had any misgivings about that, he didn’t say anything. Even though he was supportive of both Rachel and Tommy, I got the feeling that he was still uneasy around Tommy.

Rachel and I were in the middle of rolling out our sleeping bags when a sudden realization hit me. Dr. Sheppard had explained that my first few periods would be more severe, and longer than normal; because my body was still adapting to them. So before I went to bed, I decided to pop in a fresh tampon.

Walking out of the bedroom, I went down the hall to the bathroom. I saw that the door was closed; but, without even thinking about it, I went to open the door anyway. I stopped when I heard Tommy’s voice on the other side, “Whoa, occupied!”

Stepping back from the door, I said, “Sorry Tommy.”

After a few moments, the door opened and I saw Tommy standing there wearing a borrowed pair of Glenn’s boxer shorts and a t-shirt. Looking at me, he said, “Sorry about that; I have a thing about new people seeing me naked, even if they know the truth about me.”

“There’s no need to explain, Tommy; I went through the same thing when I started changing.” Glancing at Tommy as I walked past him, into the bathroom, I said, “You should’ve seen the steps I took to keep Kat from finding out before I was ready to tell her.”

“There’s something I’ve been wondering pretty much all day; especially after I saw you flirting with Jeff.”

“I’m sure I have no idea what you’re talking about.” Blushing, I said, “I wasn’t flirting with Jeff.”

“Oh please; you were flirting with them, or are you telling me it was simply a coincidence you were showing off your cleavage in that bikini.”

Okay, Tommy was right; I had been flirting with Jeff. I made a concerted effort to lean over the railing in such a way that Jeff would get a glimpse of my cleavage. Judging from the look on his face, and the slight bulge in his shorts, it had the desired effect. “So I was flirting with Jeff, big deal; I happen to think he’s cute.”

“That right there is why I’ve been wondering about this all day. By all accounts, Marcus was your typical, heterosexual guy right?”

“He didn’t have a lot of luck with girls; but yeah, he was straight.” Getting confused, I said, “What are you getting at?”

“You went from that to being a boy crazy teenage girl in a week.” Pausing, Tommy said, “If I were in your position, I think I’d be worried about how quickly that happened.”

I hadn’t thought much about it until Tommy brought it up, but I had fleeting moments where I wondered if I was moving too fast towards girldom. “I guess I’m just able to adjust quickly; first with my accident and now this.”

“I don’t think it’s that; forgive me for saying this, but I don’t think you’ve processed everything that’s happened to you.” Sighing, Tommy said, “You went through something that Rachel, myself, and others like us would do anything to experience. But you went through it against your will.”

“Anyone ever tell you that you take forever to get to the point?” getting frustrated, I said, “What exactly are you saying, Tommy?”

“You need to slow down; let yourself adjust to everything that’s happened.” Looking at me, Tommy said, “It would be detrimental to your health if you don’t.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Getting Ready for Bed

choices: The Next Day (First Day of School
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I didn’t sleep well last night; I couldn’t stop thinking about what Tommy had told me in the bathroom. The more I thought about it, the more I realized that he was right; I wasn’t giving myself the time I needed to process what happened to me.

After a quick breakfast that morning, Rachel and I went upstairs to get ready for our girl’s beach day. It turned out that my concerns about Glenn being uneasy around Tommy were misplaced; before Tommy could leave the kitchen, Glenn asked him if he’d like to hang out with him and Jeff today.

To give Rachel some privacy, taking into account my conversation with Tommy the night before, I decided to get changed in the bathroom. I had just finished putting on the bottom and was working on the top when there was a knock at the door. “I’ll just be another minute.”

“It’s Tommy, I-I have a favor to ask you.”

Hurriedly pulling my top on, I opened the door. Seeing the nervous expression on Tommy’s face, I said, “You alright, Tommy?”

“No, not really.” Stepping into the bathroom, and closing the door, he said, “I’m kind of embarrassed to ask this, but do you have some tampons that I can borrow?”

For a brief moment, I wondered why Tommy would be asking me for tampons; until I realized what was going on. “Is it your time of the month?”

“Yeah; even if it's four days early.” Sighing, Tommy said, “I normally bring some with me, just in case; but I guess I forgot this time.”

Walking over to the closet, I pulled four tampons out of the package and handed them to Tommy. “Here you go.” Pausing, I said, “If you don’t mind me asking; what’s it like for someone in your position to have a period?”

“A lot of guys in my support group think that having a period makes them less of a man.” Looking at me, Tommy said, “But I don’t see it that way; in fact, I think it makes me even more of one.”

“How’s that?”

“Every guy I know says he couldn’t see himself handle having them.” Smiling, Tommy said, “But I’m a guy; and I’ve been having periods for the last two years. I guess that makes me pretty tough, huh.”

“I think you’re right; that does make you pretty tough.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: The Next Day

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.content:
After my conversation in the bathroom with Tommy, I quietly pulled Rachel aside and suggested that she always keep some tampons with her. When she asked why I explained that it would make things easier for Tommy and it would help other girls looked on her more kindly once word got out.

Rachel and I stepped out of the house wearing nothing but shorts and our suit tops. As Rachel started heading towards where Miranda and Kat were waiting, I turned to see Tommy sitting on the porch swing. I couldn’t help but notice the sullen look on his face; it didn’t take long to realize why that was.

I walked around the side of the house, where I found Jeff and Glenn standing by their bikes. Walking up to Glenn, I said, “Where are you guys off to?”

“We’re heading to the beach as well; don’t worry, we won’t embarrass you.” Gesturing to Jeff, Glenn said, “We thought we might check out the girl situation.”

I knew that meant they were going there to flirt with girls; the thought of which upset me. I couldn’t shake the concern that Jeff might find some other girl to fawn over. Pulling Glenn aside, I said, “You know how I feel about Jeff; why on Earth are the two of you going to flirt with girls?”

“There’s nothing wrong with a little harmless flirting, sis.” Looking at me, Glenn said, “Besides I’m pretty sure Jeff’s just coming along to hang out; it’s pretty obvious how he feels about you.”

I breathed a sigh of relief, “That makes me feel a little better.” Pausing, I said, “Before you go, I was wondering if you guys might bring Tommy along.”

Jeff, standing by his bike, chuckled as I said that. For his part, Glenn was able to contain himself. Looking at me, he said, “I’m not comfortable with having Tommy come along.”

I was shocked; I never thought my brother would respond that way. Looking at him, I said, “Are you serious, Glenn? Just because Tommy’s transgender doesn’t mean he’s any less of a guy than you are.”

“Tommy being transgender has nothing to do with why I’m uncomfortable.” He got a hurt look in his eyes as he looked at me and said, “How could you possibly think that was the case; after how supportive I’ve been of you.”

I felt two inches tall at that point; realizing that I’d overreacted. Glenn had been very supportive of me since I became a girl. “You’re right, I’m sorry for thinking that.” Looking at him, I said, “I don’t know what I’d do if you weren’t the supportive big brother that you are.”

“I accept your apology, sis; but come on, you know me better than that.”

Still wanting to get to the bottom of this, I said, “Then why are you so uncomfortable with the idea of Tommy coming along?”

“It’s because I barely know him, alright?”

“You barely know Jeff, but you have no problem hanging out with him.”

“That’s different.” Looking at me, Glenn said, “I’m only spending time with Jeff because I know how much you care about him. I have to make sure he’s right for you.”

“Trust me, Glenn; Jeff’s a good guy.” Hugging Glenn, I said, “But thanks for looking out for me.”

“Anytime, sis.” Hugging me back, Glenn said, “I’ll go ask Tommy if he wants to come; you have a problem with him using your bike?”

“Of course not.”
1111111111111111111111111111111111111111.title: Talking with Glenn

1111111111112.content:
Allison stood up as I approached the bed; giving me a strange look as I went past. “That’s funny; I didn’t realize until just now, but you’re shorter than the last time I saw you.”

I had no idea what Allison was talking about, and the expression on my face said as much. “What are you talking about?”

Grabbing my arm, Allison gently pulled me back into the bathroom. “The last time I saw you, before my trip, you were a good 3 inches taller than me.” Standing the two of us next to each other, she said, “Now we’re about the same height; I might even be a smidge taller than you.”

As I looked at the reflection in the mirror, I quickly realized Allison was right. Now I wasn’t that tall to begin with, no more than 5’9” at most, so to see that I had suddenly shrunk was disconcerting. “Okay, that’s weird; Dr. Sheppard didn’t say anything about me shrinking.” Concerned, I said, “What should I do?”

“You were given an experimental medicine, Marcus; I’d get in touch with Dr. Sheppard immediately.” Looking at me, she said, “If he’s planning to take this procedure public, he should know of any unforeseen issues.”

“I’m pretty sure he’s still working here at the hospital, I’ll have him paged.” With a sigh, I said, “Could you call my folks and tell them?”

“Of course.” Hugging me, Allison said, “It’s going to be okay, Marcus.”

“Thanks, Ally.”
1111111111112.title: You're Shorter

11111111111121.content:
Calling Dr. Sheppard turned out to be a wise call; because by the time he and my folks got to the room, I’d shrunk another two inches. I was now no taller than a 10-year-old. Everyone was understandably shocked when they walked into the room; seeing me sitting at the foot of the bed in a hospital gown that was now too big for me.

“What’s happening to me, Dr. Sheppard?” worried, I said, “You never said anything about me getting shorter.”

“I’m as confused as you are, Marcus; this never happened in any of the tests.” Looking at me, he said, “If it’s alright with you, I’d like to run some tests.”

“Go right ahead; the sooner you can undo the better.” Sighing, I said, “Starting high school again is going to be awkward enough, I’d rather not be the shortest member of the freshman class.”

Retrieving a syringe from a drawer, Dr. Sheppard prepared to take some blood. “I assure you that won’t happen, Marcus. I’ll send this to the lab; we should know more in an hour or so.”

**********************


The next hour seemed to drag by; as everyone, including me, patiently awaited the results of the test. Allison and Mom were in the corner, talking about the proposal, while Dad was using the bathroom. Glenn walked back into the room with a glass of water; as he handed it to me, he said, “How are you doing, bro?”

“How do you think I’m doing, Glenn?” taking the water, I said, “I get the use of my legs back, but I’ve shrunk to the size of a 10-year-old boy.”

“I wonder how Kat is going to feel; dating someone that’s practically the same height as her.” Seeing the dumbfounded look on my face, Glenn said, “Don’t look so surprised, I overheard you telling her you’d like to go out with her at some point.”

“You’re not going to make fun of me, are you?”

“Why would I do that?” looking at me, Glenn said, “Kat’s a good looking girl; who am I to say you can’t date her.”
“Phew. I was worried you’d say it was weird”

“It is a little weird; I mean, she’s practically our little sister.” Pausing, Glenn said, “But if neither of you has a problem with that, then I’d say go for it.”

Another few minutes went by before Dr. Sheppard entered the room again. He was holding a tablet in his hands and had a grim look on his face. The look told me that he didn’t have good news. “What’s wrong with me, Doc?”

“It seems that the series of injections had an unintended side effect.” Looking at me and my family, Dr. Sheppard said, “It did repair Marcus’ spine as planned; but instead of making him a 13-year-old again, it’s making him even younger.”

Everyone in the room made an audible gasp. Looking at Dr. Sheppard, worried, I said, “How much younger?”

“If my tests are correct; by this time tomorrow, you’ll be _____.”
11111111111121.title: Consulting with Dr. Sheppard

111111111111212.content:
"A toddler? This is a nightmare I'm having, right? Just a big, scary dream. And, when I wake up, I'll be my 13-year-old self, right?"

Dr. Sheppard shook his head. "I'm sorry, Marcus. You're no longer 13. You're 10. And you're still getting younger."

I looked up at Glenn. Way up at him. He really was the big brother now. "It... it wasn't supposed to be like this."

"I know, bro."

"None of us expected something like this," Dr. Sheppard said. He took a breath. "Among other things, you'll need a new wardrobe. I'll pay for it, out of my own pocket. And I'll start an account for you, to help fund your education."

"For now, Doctor," Dad said, "we'd like to be alone."

"Of course," he said, and left my room.

The words filled my mind. A toddler. Forget junior high, it was day care for me. Video games? More like blocks. And then there's the bathroom, assuming I don't wind up back in diapers.

Glenn cleared his throat. "What are you going to tell Kat?"

Kat! She had had a crush on me since forever, and she was looking forward to us dating. Fat chance of that happening now. I'll be too young for her, now, instead of the other way around. "I can't talk to her just now," I said. "Maybe later."

"You might not want to make it too much later," Glenn said.

I nodded. Who knew how long I'd be able to speak to her?
111111111111212.title: A Toddler

1111111111112121.content:
I must’ve fallen asleep at some point after getting that unwanted news; because when I woke up, I quickly realized I was no longer in my hospital room. I tried to look around at my surroundings but found that my body wasn’t responding the way it should. Oh no; please don’t let me be paralyzed again.

It took some effort but I managed to turn my head enough to see a baby lying in a crib next to me. I soon realized where I was, when I discovered that I was in a similar crib – I was in the hospital’s nursery. Oh god, I’m a baby again. I tried to speak in a desperate attempt to get some attention, but found the only noise I could make was a whimper.

I quickly grew frustrated and did the only thing I could do – I started crying. I don’t know how long I cried for, but soon enough a nurse appeared above me. “Hello little one.” Lifting me up, she said, “Are you hungry?”

No, I’m not hungry! I’m a teenager, not a baby! But the only thing I could do was keep crying. After a few minutes of this, the nurse placed me back in the crib and wheeled me out of the room. Where is she taking me?

I soon had my answer when I was wheeled into a hospital room. I could hear voices but had trouble determining who they were. After a moment, I heard a voice I could identify – my mom’s. “Is he alright?”

For some reason her voice had a calming effect on me, and I slowly stopped crying. As the nurse picked me up, she said, “I think he’s probably a little hungry; after all, he’s been through quite an ordeal.”

You can say that again. I figured the nurse would hand me off to mom so she could give me a bottle. So imagine my reaction when I saw Mom lying in a hospital bed. She looked different; like she’d gained weight since I last saw her. As the nurse handed me off to her, Mom said, “Its okay sweetie, Mommy’s here.”

My confusion turned to horror when, instead of reaching for a bottle, Mom took out one of her breasts. Oh god no. I was powerless to stop her as she slowly guided me towards the exposed nipple. The memory of me latching on and breast feeding was one I’d give anything to forget. The fact that I was doing so without even thinking was very disturbing.

So there I was, being breastfed by my mom, when Glenn appeared in my line of sight. I focused on him as he looked at me and said, “I bet that wheelchairs looking pretty good right about now.”

Suddenly everything went dark. When I could finally see again, I was relieved to find that I was back in my hospital room. I did get a little worried, however, when I saw Mom standing over me. Much to my relief, she simply looked down on me and said, “Marcus, sweetie, are you alright?”

I was quite relieved when I found I was able to talk. “Yeah, I’m fine.” Looking at her, I said, “Why does my body feel weird?”

“I’m afraid your...regression is complete; you’re now approximately 14 months old.”

As Mom reached down to pick me up, I wanted nothing more than for her to be wrong. Alas, that wasn’t meant to be; while holding me in her arms, she turned to face the mirror. Staring back at me, clear as day, was the reflection of a horrified looking 14-month-old baby. Before I knew what was happening, I started crying.
1111111111112121.title: A Nightmare

11111111111121211.content:
It took Mom the better part of 10 minutes to calm me down. Once I was no longer crying, holding me in her arms, she sat down on the bed. “Your regression accelerated once almost immediately after you fell asleep; Dr. Sheppard thinks it was because the process was exhausting.” Looking at me, Mom said, “You were at 14 months for close to an hour before he finally declared it complete.”

As I looked around the room, I realized Dad and Glenn weren’t there. “Where’s Glenn and Da-da?”

I winced at my use of a word I hadn’t used since the first time I was a little kid. “I sent them home.” Choosing not to call attention to my choice of words, Mom said, “I figured you wouldn’t want everyone standing around you; showing you how small you’d become.”

“I suppose I got off lucky; all things considered.” Remembering my dream, I said, “I dreamt that I was a newborn baby; it must’ve been the first time because you were in a hospital bed. The nurse handed me to you and, well...I’d rather not talk about it.”

“Let’s be grateful we won’t be doing anything like that.” Looking at me, Mom said, “There’s going to be some adjustments, to be sure, but it’s not like I’ve never raised a toddler before.”

Looking at Mom, doing my best to raise an eyebrow, I said, “What kind of adjustments?”

“Well Dr, Sheppard thinks you’ll need to relearn how to walk; so that means you’ll be in a stroller for a while, even after you start walking again.” Sighing, Mom said, “Then there’s the car seat, the diapers...”

“Wait, did you say diapers?”

“Yes, I did.” Seeing my perplexed expression, Mom said, “You may know how to use the toilet, but your body has to relearn it; until then, you’re in diapers.”

Can this get any worse? “Is there anything good that came from this?”

“Forgive me for sounding a little selfish, but I must say I’m looking forward to the years ahead.” "Why?"

“I already got to watch you grow up once, but now I get a chance to see it all again.” Suddenly Mom scrunched her nose. Getting up, she lay me down on the bed. “Now I’m afraid I have some bad news for you.”

I braced myself for the worst. “What is it?

“Somebody in this room just made a stinky.” Looking at me, trying to hold back a smile, Mom said, “And it wasn’t me.”

I sighed and resigned myself to the indignity of the first of many diaper changes.
11111111111121211.title: Calmed Down

111111111111212111.content:
I was examined the next morning; not by Dr. Sheppard but by one of the hospital’s pediatricians. The examination was quite thorough; he took my weight and measurements, as well as checked my vitals. I was a little surprised when he took some blood for testing. Once he was finished, the pediatrician said, “Well Mrs. Scott, your son is a perfectly healthy 14-month-old boy.”

I swear I heard mom breathe a sigh of relief; like me being a healthy toddle was good news. Well, I guess it was good news; just not the kind I’d expected. “That sounds great, Doctor; the only problem is that I’m supposed to be 13 years old.”

“You’ll probably want to put more effort into keeping incognito.” Taking his gloves off, the doctor said, “Need I remind you that children your age aren’t supposed to be talking yet.”

“I did this so I could walk again; but now, not only can’t I walk, but I can’t even control my bladder or my bowels.” Looking at him, letting my frustration out, I said, “Forgive me if I’m not thrilled that I’m a healthy baby; since that’s not what was supposed to happen. Just be glad I’m able to control my tongue.”

The doctor ignored me and turned to my folks. “Since your son appears to be healthy, I see no issue with letting you take him home. That’s, of course, assuming you have an appropriate seat for your car.”

Dr. Sheppard walked out of the room; and returned with a sealed box containing a toddler’s car seat. As he handed it to Dad, he said, “They’re all taken care of.”
“Alright then, everything seems to be in order.” Looking at Dr. Sheppard, the pediatrician said, “Why don't we start the paperwork, Doctor?”

Everyone left the exam room a few minutes later; the two doctors went one way while my parents and I returned to my room. As we rounded the corner leading to my room, I saw Kat standing outside my room. When she saw us, she said, “I came by to check on Marcus, but he wasn’t there. Do you guys know where he is?”

Mom and Dad looked at each other for a moment; Mom turned to Kat and said, “You better come inside and have a seat, Kat.”

Kat followed us into the room and sat down. Once she did, she said, “What’s going on?”

"Kat," Mom said, "this is Marcus."

“You can’t fool me, Mrs. Scott.” Scoffing, Kat said, “Marcus and I are the same age now; he’s not a baby.”

“It was an unforeseen side effect.” Touching my head, Dad said, “Marcus kept getting younger until he was 14 months old."

"Do you honestly expect me to believe that?"

"What do you want me to do, Kat?" I asked. "Check your closet for monsters?"

Kat’s jaw dropped as she stared at me. “M-Marc?"

I nodded. "It's me."

She passed out.
111111111111212111.title: The Next Morning

1111111111112121111.content:
Alerted by my mom’s yelling, a doctor came running into the room. “What’s wrong?”

I wanted to respond, but remembered what the doctor said about blending in – I had to act like the baby I appeared to be. While Mom cradled me in her arms, Dad said, “She fainted; I think she hit her head.”

After an examination revealed a small bump on Kat’s head, the doctor returned with an ice pack and instructions to call for him if things changed. Once the doctor left the room, Mom slowly walked over to the bed; giving me a glimpse of Kat as she lay there.

As I stared at her, I started to whimper a little bit. Damn this immature body. Looking up at Mom, I said, “Is Kat going to be alright?”

“Of course, sweetie.” In a soothing manner, Mom said, “You heard the doctor; it’s just a mild bump.”

Kat suddenly started to groan; slowly opening her eyes and looking around the room. “W-What happened?”

“You fainted, Kat.” Handing her the ice pack, Dad said, “The doctor said you have a minor bump but otherwise you’re fine.”

As Kat placed the ice pack on her head, she looked at me and said, “I swear you said that baby was Marcus.”

Looking at her, worried that she’d faint again, I said, “It is me, Kat.”

“That’s really you, Marcus?” A dumbfounded look on her face, Kat said, “H-How is that even possible?”

As Mom turned so I could see Kat better, I said, “Dr. Sheppard doesn’t know what caused this, but he’s working on figuring it out.” Looking at her, I said, “If it helps, I’m a perfectly healthy 14-month-old.”

“Is there any chance this can be reversed?”

“He won’t know until he figures out what went wrong.” Sighing, I said, “I’m stuck like this in the meantime.”

Suddenly everyone in the room, including me, caught a whiff of a putrid smell. I immediately knew what it was, but was embarrassed to say it with Kat in the room – I’d soiled my diaper. Unfortunately, Mom didn’t have any qualms about being subtle. Looking at Kat, she said, “Kat, would you mind holding Marcus? I need to change his diaper.”
1111111111112121111.title: Repercussions

11111111111121211111.content:
Kat and I were left alone after Mom changed my diaper. Holding me in her arms, Kat sat down in the chair and looked at me. “I can’t believe this is happening, Marcus; how can something like this be possible?”

“I was chatting with Allison when she noticed that I’d shrunk a few inches.” Squirming a little bit, I said, “By the time Dr. Sheppard and my folks got here, I was the size of a 10-year-old. The changes continued while I was asleep, I woke up looking like this.”

“This has to be some cruel cosmic joke.” Tearing up, Kat said, “I was so excited by the thought of us dating, but now you’re a
toddler.”

“Dr. Sheppard said there’s a chance this can be reversed, so we could still have a chance at dating.” Sighing, I said, “In the meantime, I’ll have to pretend I’m really a 14-month-old; which shouldn’t be too hard, seeing as I can’t walk and I’m forced to use a diaper.”

“You have my word that I’ll do everything I can to help you, Marcus.” Leaning down, Kat kissed me on the forehead, “Toddler or not, I still love you.”
