Hearing Kat telling me that she loved me brought a smile to my face. “I love you too, Kat.”

Kat started to chuckle a little as she looked at me. Seeing my perplexed look, she said, “You have a really cute smile, Marcus.”

I wanted to say Kat was crazy, since I only had four teeth, but decided not to. Looking at her, I simply said, “If you say so, Kat.”

The two of us just stayed like that for a while, Kat just holding me in her arms. I don’t know what it was, maybe the feeling of her heartbeat, but I felt safe in Kat’s arms. After a while, I started to feel drowsy, and I soon fell asleep in her arms.
11111111111121211111.title: Alone With Kat

111111111111212111111.content:
I must’ve been asleep for a while; because when I was rudely awakened by a loud noise, I quickly realized I wasn’t in the hospital anymore. I was momentarily confused by my surroundings until I realized I was in a car seat facing towards the rear of the car.

“Is anyone there?” looking around, I said, “What’s going on?”

The sound of an opening door grabbed my attention; I turned to find Mom standing just outside the car. “Did you have a nice nap, sweetie?”

I looked behind Mom and caught a glimpse of the Wal-Mart sign in the distance. “Why are we at Wal-Mart?”

“We needed to get some things for you; a crib, high chair, etc.” smiling, Mom said, “You must’ve been tired because you slept the entire time we were in the store.”

“How long was I even asleep? The last thing I remember was talking with Kat.”

“That was almost two hours ago.” With a chuckle, Mom said, “You looked adorable, asleep in Kat’s arms.”

I was about to say something when I spotted Dad and Glenn pushing two fully loaded shopping carts through the parking lot. It didn’t take a genius to realize what was in the boxes; sure Mom told me what they’d bought, but seeing the boxes in person just served as a painful reminder that I was now a baby.

It took all of 10 minutes to get everything into the back of the car; Glenn got in the back seat next to me. Looking at me, he said,
“Hey bro, how was your nap?”

“Not that bad, considering I’m a toddler.” Looking at him, seeing a bottle of soda in his hands, I said, “Could I have a drink of that soda, I’m kind of thirsty.”

“Oh no, Marcus; you can’t have soda at your age.” Peaking over from the trunk, Mom looked at Glenn and said, “Glenn, there’s some pre-made bottles of formula in the bag behind Marcus.”

As Glenn reached for the bag, I said, “Hell no, I’m not drinking from a bottle!”

“You’re 13 months old, Marcus; it comes with the territory.” With a stern look on her face, Mom said, “Also please watch your language; I don’t want to explain why my 13-month-old is talking, let alone swearing.”

Glenn had retrieved one of the bottles by that point. Handing it to me, he said, “Here you go, bro.”

I looked at the bottle in disgust; there was no way I was going to drink from that. After a moment, however, my resolve started to dissipate; soon enough, to my embarrassment, I was drinking from the bottle. What made the situation worse – I was enjoying it.
111111111111212111111.title: Waking Up

1111111111112121111111.content:
Barely an hour had passed since we got home from the hospital, but already Dad and Glenn had managed to baby proof most the house. After they’d finished baby-proofing the bathroom, they moved on to my bedroom; where they would change it from a teenager’s room to that of a toddler’s.

While all that was going on, I was strapped into a baby walker downstairs. I couldn’t help but feel like fate was playing a cruel joke on me; the whole reason I’d gone through with the treatments was to get the use of my legs back, only now I’d been regressed to an age where I couldn’t walk even if I wanted to.

Mom had just put me in a fresh diaper, so I was set loose once again. I was rolling around downstairs, checking out the work Dad and Glenn had done. They’d put corner protectors on everything I could possibly reach, baby proof locks on the cabinets, and a retractable gate across the basement door. Okay, maybe that last one was a good idea.

A noise in the kitchen attracted my attention, so I decided to see what it was. Thanks in large part to my short baby legs; it took me a few moments to traverse the eight feet from the basement door to the kitchen. But once I was there, I saw Mom moving some cans from a bag to a shelf in the pantry. I caught a look at one of the cans as she stacked it on the shelf; it was baby food.
“Mom, do I really have to eat that stuff?”

“I’m afraid so, Marcus.” Continuing to work, she said, “Since you’ve only got four teeth you're not able to eat the same food that you used to.”

“But that stuff tastes disgusting!”

“It’s the only thing you’re able to eat at your age, sweetie,” looking at me, she said, “There’s going to be a period of adjustment for all of us; I just ask that you remain patient.”

“What’s there for you to adjust to?” getting at annoyed, I said, “I’m the one who got turned into a damn toddler.”

“Watch your mouth, Marcus.” Sighing, Mom said, “Everyone will have to adjust to your new condition, just like we did after your accident.”

“What about the all the baby proofing Dad and Glenn have been doing?” looking up at her, trying my best to sound serious, I said,
“You do realize I’m only a toddler physically, right?”

“If we have people over, and they see you roaming around, they'll expect to see precautions against you getting into anything you shouldn't. Just like how I don't want you cursing.”

“Okay, I guess that’s fair.”

Mom was about to go back to work on the food when Glenn walked into the kitchen. Seeing the two of us, he said, “We’ve got Marcus’ room all set up if you guys want to come upstairs.”
1111111111112121111111.title: At Home

11111111111121211111111.content:
In retrospect, I should have realized how extensive the changes to my room would’ve been. My first clue that something was amiss was when Mom carried me upstairs; I saw several boxes of my things lining the hallway. Please don’t give that stuff away. Then I saw my bed frame, and mattress, leaning against the wall just outside my room.

I prepared myself for what was sure to come, but even that wasn’t going to be enough. Glenn led us into my room, where I saw what they had done. Now my room wasn’t all that big, it was slightly smaller than Glenn’s, but seeing how empty it was without my bed, my desk, and my TV was just shocking.

Situated against the wall where my bed had been was a brand new crib, with one of those spinning mobiles attached to it. Oh god, it’s like they forgot I’m still mentally me. Next, I saw a motorized swing in the corner, along with what looked like every stuffed animal Glenn and I ever had growing up. Okay, that’s a nice touch.

Dad, in the process of lifting a changing table onto my dresser, said, “What do you think, Marcus?”

I tried my best to come off as being incredulous, but I couldn’t pull it off in my current condition. “It’s almost like this room never belonged to a teenager.”

“We had considered setting up your crib in our room, but we thought you wouldn’t like that.”

“Damn right I wouldn’t!” getting annoyed, I said, “You guys did all this without even asking me what I wanted.”

“Come on, Marcus.” Looking at me, worried about my increasingly poor attitude, Glenn said, “We’re just trying to take care of you.”

“I’m 18 years old; I don’t need you to take care of me!”

At that point, my emotions grew beyond what I could control, and I started not only crying but kicking as well. As far as anyone was concerned, I was just a 14 month old having a tantrum; which only served to get me even more riled up.

My cries echoed through the nearly empty room; until Mom managed to get Glenn’s attention. “Glenn, there’s an unopened package of pacifiers in the bathroom; go get one of them.”

Glenn quickly ran out of the room while I continued crying, at one point I even kicked Mom in the mouth. Glenn returned about a minute later, pacifier in hand. “Here you go, Mom.”

Taking the pacifier, Mom looked at me and said, “I didn’t want to do this, Marcus; but you aren’t giving me much choice.” Placing the pacifier in my mouth, she said, “If you’re going to act like a toddler, then we’re going to treat you like one.”

The feeling of the pacifier in my mouth was disturbing, but it did have a surprising effect on me. I slowly calmed down and stopped crying; until I was quietly sucking on the pacifier. This is so embarrassing, but it does feel right.

I was content to suck on the pacifier as Dad appeared in my field of vision. Looking at Mom, he said, “Perhaps we should let him lie down; he’s had a rough day.”

“I suppose you’re right.” Looking down at me, Mom said, “Hopefully when you wake up, you’ll be in a better mood.”

My eyelids had started to droop at that point; as Mom gently lowered me into the crib, tucked me in under a blanket, and put a stuffed giraffe in my hands. The last thing I remembered before falling asleep was seeing Glenn and my parents looking down at me.
11111111111121211111111.title: Baby's First Tantrum

111111111111212111111111.content:
It was almost night time when I woke up again; the last ray of sunlight was bouncing off the far wall. Even though I was on my back, I knew that I was alone in the room. Memories from earlier came back as I lay there; from what had been done to my room, to the subsequent temper tantrum.

It took some doing, but I managed to roll over onto my stomach. After a moment on my stomach, using the sides of the crib, I managed to pull myself up to a standing position. My legs wobbled a little bit, but I was confident that I wasn’t going anywhere.

I caught a glimpse of myself in a nearby mirror, and I was taken aback by what I saw. Wearing nothing but a diaper, and with a pacifier in my mouth, there was no way I’d be mistaken for anything but a toddler. This is so humiliating; I’m supposed to be in High School, not diapers.

Suddenly the bedroom door opened and Mom walked into the room. She must’ve been watching me through a camera because she wasn’t surprised to see that I was awake. “That was quite a tantrum you had earlier, Marcus.” Walking over to the crib, Mom said, “You aren’t doing yourself any favors, acting like that. If you act like a baby, we’re going to treat you like one.”

Spitting the pacifier out, I said, “Sorry Mom, I-I guess I kind of lost it.”

“I have a bruised lip that would agree with you.” Picking me up from the crib, Mom said, “Let’s go downstairs, it’s time for dinner.”

I tried to get excited about that, as Mom carried me downstairs, but I quickly remembered what that meant – canned baby food for me, while they got to eat regular food.
111111111111212111111111.title: Waking Up

1111111111112121111111111.content:
Mom carried me into the kitchen and placed me in the high chair. After placing a bib around my neck, she sat down and looked at me, “Now Marcus, as I explained earlier, you can longer have any of the food you normally would.” Reaching for two jars of baby food on the table, Mom said, “You have a choice between chicken & gravy or mashed carrots.”

I looked at the jars for a moment; neither of them looked very appetizing. After a minute, I looked at Mom and said, “Can I please have what you guys are having?”

“I’m afraid not, sweetie.” Sighing, Mom said, “I know it’s frustrating; but, given time, I think you’ll get used to it.”

I crossed my arms in an attempt to look tough; only I’m sure it didn’t have the effect I’d intended. “If I can’t have what you’re having, then I’m not eating.”

Getting up, Mom went over to the fridge and returned with a bottle. “Okay then, if that’s how you want to do this.” With the bottle in one hand and the jars of baby food in the other, Mom said, “Either you have the baby food, or I’ll give you a bottle.”
1111111111112121111111111.title: Downstairs

11111111111121211111111111.content:
I kept my arms crossed defiantly, looking away from Mom. There was no way I was going to eat baby food.

I realized that was a mistake once Mom lifted me out of the high chair. "Fine, bottle it is then" she said, putting the baby bottle into my mouth.

This is so fucking humiliating! was all I could think while sucking on the baby bottle; both Glenn and Dad were watching too! But I was unable to protest, and I didn't want the baby food either, so I just kept sucking on the baby bottle.

Wait... is this yummy? To my discomfort, I was actually enjoying the baby formula. It certainly didn't taste yucky like the baby food, and it was warm. In fact by the time I was almost done with my bottle, I was starting to feel sleepy again.

"There, all done" Mom said, putting away the baby bottle and cradling me. "Now was that so bad?" She said

I had to admit that I had actually enjoyed the formula "it was yummy I guess". I spoke very queitly, still hesitant to accept being a baby again. Before I could say anything else, I was yawning.

"Aw, someone's sleepy" Mom cued, taking me back upstairs.

"But Glenn and Dadda"

"You already had your dinner sweetie, and from the looks of things you look very sleepy" Mom said placing me back in my crib

I wasn’t ready to go back to sleep. Although there were plenty of other things I wasn't ready for. "Momma? Is this a nightmare?"

Mom replied "I know this must be very frustrating for your Marcus, but this is just how things turned out."

"But this what not what I signed up for!" I complain

"Life doesn't always go as we expect, and we can always do anything about it. All we can do is go with the flow sweetie". Mom kisses my forehead, "goodnight".

And with that Mom turned off the lights and left me alone in my room once more.

I was now regretting not taking the baby food as the warm formula was making me feel sleepy. I don't know how long it took before I finally fell asleep, but it didn't feel like it was that long...
11111111111121211111111111.title: I Resist

11111111111121211111111112.content:
While neither option was appealing to me, I figured that the baby food was the lesser of two evils. With my arms still crossed, I silently pointed towards the baby food. Putting the bottle down, for the moment, Mom turned back to me. “Okay, now which flavor would you like?”

“They both sound disgusting.” Sighing, I said, “Why don’t you just choose.”

Grabbing on the jars, Mom said, “Fine, chicken & gravy it is then.”

I watched, hesitantly, as Mom opened the jar of food. I can’t believe my life’s come to this. Dipping the spoon into the jar, she was about to feed it to me when the phone suddenly started ringing. As she got up from the table, Mom called out, “Glenn, could you come in here!”

God, please no! Before I could protest, Glenn came running into the kitchen. “Yeah, Mom?”

As Mom went to answer the phone, she said, “Could you do me a favor, and feed your brother?”

Glenn quickly obliged and sat down across from me. Grabbing the jar, and spoon, he looked at me and said, “Is widdle baby hungry?”

It was bad enough that I was in this situation; I didn’t need Glenn teasing me about it. But, as he looked at me with a goofy smile, I decided to have a little fun with him. I thought back to the few times I had to help feed him, back when he was a baby. He was one of the fussiest babies that I’d ever seen; more than a few times I had to sample the food just to get him to eat it.

That was when I got an idea; I would pretend to be fussy and refuse to eat, in the hopes that he would eat some of the food. The plan started perfectly; every time he would bring the spoon near my mouth, I would do my best to avoid it. After a few failed attempts, Glenn was starting to get frustrated. “Mom! Marcus won’t eat his dinner.”

“You have to be patient, Glenn; he’s still getting used to this.” Sighing, covering the phone’s mouthpiece, Mom said, “Why don’t you try eating some it yourself; that always worked when you were a baby.”

Bingo. A smile formed on my mouth as Glenn slowly, but surely, brought the spoon up to his mouth. I started to giggle as he ate a spoonful of food; only to stop when he immediately spat it out in disgust – all over my face.

I don’t know why it happened; maybe I was tired or maybe I was just surprised. But before Glenn even had a chance to apologize, I started crying.
11111111111121211111111112.title: I Gave In

111111111111212111111111121.content:
The sound of me crying caused Mom to come running back into the room. “I’m glad that wasn’t an important phone call. What’s going on here?”

“I tried the baby food, just like you said.” Spitting a little bit, Glenn said, “It tasted like dog sh - "

“Watch your language, young man.”

“I mean, it tasted terrible.” Gesturing to me, he said, “I spit it out, and that’s when he started crying.”

It was then that Mom noticed that my face was covered in baby food. While Glenn went to get something to drink, mom grabbed a moist towelette and wiped my face. “Here you go, sweetie; let’s get you cleaned up.” Looking at me, she said, “Is that better?”

I’d stopped crying at that point. “I guess so.”

“Good, now where were we? Oh, yes, feeding you.”

As Mom picked up the spoon again, I sensed I wouldn’t be able to trick Mom the way I’d tricked Glenn. So I decided to open my mouth, and instantly regretted it – the texture and the taste were just wrong. While Mom scooped up another spoonful of baby food, I looked at her and said, “What’s wrong with me, Mom?”

“There’s nothing wrong with you, sweetie.” Looking at me, she said, “Apart from being a 14-month-old, you’re in perfect health; otherwise you’d still be in the hospital.”

Swallowing another spoonful of food, I said, “That’s not what I meant.” Sighing, I said, “A little spit up shouldn’t have made me cry, just like I shouldn’t have cried over my old stuff. So what's wrong with me?"

"Hmmm.” Mom dipped the spoon back in the jar but didn’t immediately remove it. “Well, you know how you’ve lost physical maturity?"

"I've noticed it once or twice."

"Perhaps your emotional maturity has taken a hit as well. Intellectually, you know what’s worth getting upset over and what isn't.” pointing towards my heart, she said, “But in here, it hurts like it was a direct attack on you. You can't lash out physically at whatever upsets you, so you break down and cry. Does that make sense?"

I nodded in agreement; Mom had a point.”

“There is a silver lining in all of this.” Smiling, Mom said, “If we can keep you intellectually stimulated, your emotions would be less likely to take control, and then you’ll be fine.”

"Do you think so?"

Holding up the empty jar of baby food, Mom said, “You tell me.”

For dessert, Mom mushed up a scoop of vanilla ice cream and fed it to me.
111111111111212111111111121.title: Continue

1111111111112121111111111211.content:
Mom had put in the crib almost an hour ago, but I’d yet to fall asleep. While the sounds of a movie playing downstairs didn’t help, I was too focused on everything that happened to me over the last few days.

To say my life took an unexpected turn would be an understatement. Going into the procedure, I expected to come out as a 13-year-old High School freshman with a girlfriend. But that isn’t what happened. Sure, I’d eventually be a High School freshman again, and I’d start dating, but I had to wait more than 10 years before I got to that point again.

In the meantime, I’d be forced to grow up all over again; learning to walk again, learning to use the toilet. All those milestones I’d crossed years ago, I’d have to cross again. During dinner, Mom and Dad discussed putting me in daycare while they were at work. I protested that idea; as it was bad enough being a toddler again, I didn’t want to be around them.

But my protests were ignored; Mom said she’d call around and see if any local daycares had openings. Thinking back to our conversation about my emotional maturity, I wondered what kind of damage would be done if I was around actual toddlers. Would I start acting like them without thinking?

What upset me the most, was how quickly it seemed my family had adjusted to my new condition. They hadn’t even given me time to adjust before they transformed my room and started treating me like a toddler. I mean it’s only 7:30; I shouldn’t be in bed yet. I had to remember to discuss that with them tomorrow.

I was starting to feel drowsy at that point; I could feel my eyelids slowly getting heavier. I reached for the stuffed giraffe as I drifted off to sleep; the last thing I heard – the *suck* *suck* of the pacifier.
1111111111112121111111111211.title: Who Am I?

11111111111121211111111112111.content:
When I woke up the next morning, I was immediately confronted with what I assumed was going to be a regular occurrence – the smell of a dirty diaper. As if the smell wasn’t bad enough, I could feel the dampness on my bottom.

I don’t know if it was because I was still waking up, or what; but I discovered that I was unable to speak. I wanted nothing more than to be rid of this soiled diaper, but I was unable to communicate. Then I remembered something from when Glenn was a baby; he would wake up crying at all hours of the night because of a soiled diaper.

As much as it pained me to do so, especially after my episodes yesterday, I realized I had little choice. So...I started crying. It wasn’t long before Dad came running into the room. Coming over to the crib, he looked down at me and said, “What’s wrong, Marcus?”

Because I was still unable to speak, I kept crying. After a moment Dad bent over and picked me up; catching a whiff of the diaper in the process. “Don’t worry Marcus; I’ll get you cleaned up.”

I slowly started to calm down as Dad carried me over to the changing table. Mom walked into the room at that point, and said, “I heard crying, what’s wrong?”

“Marcus had a dirty diaper.” Glancing at mom, over his shoulder, Dad said, “Don’t worry, I’ve got it taken care of.”

Walking towards me, while Dad continued to change my diaper, Mom said, “How did you sleep, Marcus?”
“I...sleep...okay.”

Mom gave Dad a worried glance before looking back at me. “Marcus, are you alright?”

“I...no...talk...right.” getting worried, I said, “What...happening?”

Finished with the diaper, Dad looked at Mom and said, “Call Dr. Sheppard, this could be a new complication.” Turning to me, Dad said, “Don’t worry, Marcus; we’ll figure out what’s going on.”
11111111111121211111111112111.title: The Next Morning

111111111111212111111111121111.content:
Once we got to the hospital, I was put through a barrage of tests to find out what was going on. Being unable to speak, the only way I could express my fear was through crying. By far the worst part was the loud noises of the MRI machine.

**************************


Eventually, after almost two hours, the tests were finished. We were waiting in a private room off of the pediatric wing. I was still feeling a little fussy, so Mom was resorting to swinging me back and forth in her arms. “Shush Marcus; everything is going to be alright.”

How is everything going to be alright? I can’t talk!

The door to the room opened and for a moment I thought it was Dr. Sheppard, but it was only Dad and Glenn. Walking over to mom, a cup of coffee in each hand, Dad said, “Let me take Marcus for a few minutes.”

After the incident last night, I didn’t expect Glenn to ever want to help with me again. So I was surprised when he stepped forward and said, “I’ll take Marcus; you guys just relax.”

“Thank you, sweetie,” as she handed me off to Glenn, Mom said, “Be sure to support his head.”

While Mom and Dad took seats at the nearby table, Glenn carried me over to the bed. Sitting down, he looked at me and said,
“I’m sorry you’re going through this, Marcus.” Taking a deep breath, Glenn said, “You went through all this to get your life back; but, thanks to some 1-in-a-million fluke, you ended up like this.”

This whole situation is pretty screwed up, isn’t it?

“I don’t know what the tests are going to tell us; if you’re becoming a toddler mentally, or what.” On the verge of tears, Glenn said,
“I want you to know that whatever happens, I will be here to protect you. I won’t let anything bad ever happen to you...baby brother.”

Damn it, Glenn, you’re going to make me start crying again. Thankfully I never got the chance; as Dr. Sheppard walked into the room at that moment. “Marcus’ results are in.”

Glenn got to his feet as Mom and Dad walked over to join us. “What do they say?” Looking at Dr. Sheppard, with bated breath, Dad said, “Please tell me Marcus’ inability to speak is only temporary.”
111111111111212111111111121111.title: At the Hospital

1111111111112121111111111211111.content:
“It appears that his condition is only temporary.”

All of us, especially me, breathed a sigh of relief. Looking at Dr. Sheppard, Dad, “That’s what we were hoping you’d say.”

“However, I’m afraid this isn’t the end of it.”

My relief quickly turned to concern, as Mom said, “What do you mean, Kevin?”

“The way I see it; Marcus’ mind is inside a body that isn’t developed enough to handle it.” Sighing, Dr. Sheppard said, “I believe that Marcus will continue experiencing moments like this, where Marcus will act like a toddler, for quite some time.”

That wasn’t what I wanted to hear. It was bad enough being in the body of a 14-month-old, I didn’t like the idea that I’d occasionally act like a toddler against my will. Looking at Dr. Sheppard, I struggled to speak. “How...How...long…?”

“I won’t lie, Marcus; there’s a very good chance this will continue until you’re a teenager again.” Sighing, Dr. Sheppard continued, “As time goes by, the length of these...lapses might even get longer.”

I said a word in response, one that probably would have gotten me considerable attention if anyone else had heard it. In this case, however, even Mom didn't reprimand me.
1111111111112121111111111211111.title: It's Temporary

11111111111121211111111112111111.content:
I’d regained some of my ability to speak by the team we got back to the car. It wasn’t perfect, but I was better able to get my point across. “What will we do...now?”

“I don’t know, Marcus.” Sighing, as she transferred me from the stroller to the car seat, Mom said, “For now, I think our only option is to keep your mind engaged.”

“What...you...mean?”

“If we can force you to use your mind more, perhaps that will help with the mental regression.” Looking at me, Mom said, “I can’t know for sure, because I’m not a neurologist.”

Glenn took his seat next to me while Mom finished strapping me in. “Maybe this is a good thing.” As he buckled his seat belt,
Glenn looked at me and said, “Remember how Mom and Dad discussed putting you in daycare?”

“Don’t...remind...me.”

“Perhaps you acting like a toddler will make things easier for you.” Pausing, Glenn said, “It could be less stressful for you; if you didn’t have to force yourself to act like a toddler.”

As much as I hated to admit it, Glenn did have a point. Acting like a toddler without even thinking about it would make my life easier in public.

********************


After stopping off at Dunkin Donuts, we finally returned home about 25 minutes later. While Mom and Dad went off to do their things, Glenn carried me into the family room. As he placed me inside my new playpen, I looked up at him and said, “Thank you.”

“For what?”

“For what you said at the hospital before Dr. Sheppard came in.” Grateful to have the ability to speak back, I said, “It made me feel...safe, knowing you would look out for me.”

“I meant every word of it, Marcus.” Kneeling in front of the playpen, Glenn said, “I’ll do everything in my power to keep you safe. After all, that’s what big brothers do.”

“I do have one favor to ask.”

“Oh?” looking at me, Glenn said, “What’s that?”

“Next time you feel the need to spit up some baby food; can you please do it in a napkin?”

Glenn let out a chuckle as he tousled my hair a little. “Sure thing, little man.”

Glenn walked off after that, leaving me alone in the playpen. As I sat there, looking at the assortment of toys to play with, I wondered what I would do next.
11111111111121211111111112111111.title: Going Home

111111111111212111111111121111111.content:
I wasn’t sure if it was because I was in a toddler’s body or if it was because I had nothing else to do, but I was enjoying playing with the toys in the playpen. Sure most of the toys were childish, like the one where you had to put shapes in the right hole, but some were genuinely entertaining.

One of the nice things about still having a teenage mind was that I could use the toys in ways other toddlers wouldn’t think of. Case in point, I was currently pretending that two teddy bears were having a police interrogation. I was in the middle of doing that when I heard the doorbell ring.

Using the side of the pen to pull myself up, I watched as Mom walked out of the laundry room to answer the door. I couldn’t see who was there, but I didn’t have to wait long before finding out. “Allison, Jamie, what brings the two of you over here?”

Walking into the house, as Mom closed the door, Allison said, “We were coming back from Wal-Mart when we decided to come and see how Marcus was doing.” Pausing, Allison said, “I heard from a co-worker that you guys had brought him to the hospital this morning.”

“It seems Marcus can’t catch a break.” Sighing, Mom said, “He was unable to speak this morning, so we brought him to the hospital to get him checked it. I’m still not sure exactly what the problem was, but Dr. Sheppard said that Marcus was experiencing a sudden mental regression.”

“What’s that supposed to mean?

“For a time, he had the mental abilities of a normal 14-month-old.” Pausing, Mom said, “He’s back to normal now, but Dr. Sheppard said he’ll continue to experience episodes like that for a while.”

“Oh wow.” With concern in her voice, Jamie said, “How is he handling that?”

“You can ask him yourself; we’ve got him in his playpen around the corner.” Smiling, Mom said, “I’ll go make some drinks while ladies talk with him.”

With that, Mom walked down the hall to the kitchen while Allison and Jamie came around the corner. While Jamie and I had only known each other for a few years, I always enjoyed spending time with her. She was a rather attractive brunette; to this day, I kept thinking about how lucky Allison was to find her.

As soon as they saw me they had to cover their mouths to keep from laughing. “Wow, Marcus.” Barely containing her laughter, Allison said, “I must say, you look adorable in that onesie of yours.”

“Please, do you have any idea how embarrassing this whole situation is?” my legs getting tired, I plopped back down on my butt. Looking at them, I said, “I was supposed to be starting high school next week; now they’re talking about putting me in daycare.”

“Try to look on the bright side, Marcus.” Kneeling in front of the pen, Jamie said, “You’ve gotten a second chance to grow up again; something which I’m sure everything would love to have.”

“I suppose you’re right; I just wish I hadn’t ended up this young.”

“It’s funny you mention that; because there’s something we wanted to ask you.” Kneeling next to Jamie, Allison said, “Do you remember, after the procedure, how I said we wanted to have your family be part of the wedding?”

“You said you wanted to have Glenn and me be junior groomsmen or something.” Looking at them, I said, “I’ll understand, given my current situation, if you don’t want me to be in the wedding.”

“We still want you to be in the wedding, Marcus; just not as a junior groomsman.” Smiling, Jamie said, “We were kind of hoping you’d agree to be our ring bearer.”

The idea that they wanted me to play such an important part of the wedding was touching. “I’d love to be the ring bearer, but there’s just one small problem.” Looking at them, I said, “Your wedding is in two months; you realize the chances of me walking by that point are pretty slim, right?”

“We’ll work the logistics out later on; we just want to know if you’d like to do it.”

I sat on the floor of the pen, thinking about what they were asking me. I did want to be a part of the wedding, but that was before I became a toddler. On the other hand, Allison and Jamie were like family to me. After giving it a moment, I looked at them both and said, “I’ll do it.”

“Great!” smiling, Allison said, “You’ll look so adorable in a little tux.”
111111111111212111111111121111111.title: Playtime

1111111111112121111111111211111111.content:
There have been many times since I came home from the hospital, where I hated the fact that I couldn’t walk anymore. After what I’d been through for the last hour, I hated it more than ever. That’s because I’ve spent the last hour stuck inside my playpen; forced to listen as Mom discussed the upcoming wedding with Allison and Jamie.

I was struggling to comprehend how they could remain so interested in the topic for so long; after an hour of listening to them talking about wedding dresses, I was struggling to stay awake. Thankfully I was saved when lunchtime rolled around; I was getting hungry and my whining made that known.”

“I’m sorry, Marcus; I must’ve lost track of time.” Getting up, Mom walked over and picked me up from the playpen. “Let’s go into the kitchen; I’ll feed you then put you down for a nap.”

“How about I feed him, Gloria?” coming over to us, Jamie said, “It’ll give us some time to talk; besides, I think Allison has something to discuss with you.”

“Okay, if you feel up to it.” Handing me off to Jamie, Mom said, “There’s some food in the pantry; but I should warn, he can be pretty stubborn.”

“This isn’t my first rodeo, Gloria.” Chuckling, Jamie said, “I think I can handle it.”
“Well holler if you need anything.”

With me in her arms, Jamie walked into the kitchen. Placing me in the highchair, she tied a bib around my neck before grabbing a can of mashed carrots from the pantry. As she down across from me, while unscrewing the lid, Jamie looked at me and said, “I’d imagine this whole situation must be embarrassing for you, but I think I’d have a hard time with the baby food.”

“It’s not that bad if you don’t think about it.” Swallowing a spoonful of the food, I said, “So what did Allison need to talk with my mom about?”

“Since Allison’s mom passed away a few years ago, Allison was wondering if your Mom would be willing to fill in.”

I had the pleasure of meeting Allison’s parents several times over the years; I was saddened when her mom died of cancer. “What does that mean exactly?”

“She’d go with Allison to the dress fittings, help us plan the wedding; all that fun logistical stuff.” Chuckling, as she took another spoonful of food, Jamie said, “It looks like your entire family’s going to be in the wedding.”

“I just hope I don’t have one of my episodes on the day of the wedding.” Sighing, as I swallowed again, I said, “I’d hate to ruin your wedding because I was acting like a toddler.”

“You won’t be the only toddler there, Marcus; my niece is going to be the flower girl.” with a chuckle, Jamie said, “Who knows; you two could hit it off.”

“Don’t get your hopes up, Jamie.”
1111111111112121111111111211111111.title: Lunchtime

11111111111121211111111112111111111.content:
When I woke up from my nap, I was greeted by the all too familiar feeling of a dirty diaper. God, this is so embarrassing. Because I made matters worse the last time I moved around in a dirty diaper, I decided to stay where I was. Alternating between squirming and whining, I finally called out, “I’ve got a dirty diaper here; a little help would be nice!”

After a few moments passed, the bedroom door opened and Glenn walked into the room; looking like I just woke him up. “What’s with all the yelling?”

Squirming again, I said, “I’ve got a dirty diaper.”

“Okay.” Confused, Glenn said, “Why are you telling me?”

“I can’t change my diaper on my own, dummy.” Glaring at him, I said, “You’ve got to do it.”

Glenn’s eyes went wide as he realized what I was asking him. “No way; not after what happened last night.” Turning around, heading towards the door, he said, “You’ll just have to wait until Mom or Dad or get back.”

“Do you have any idea how uncomfortable it is for me to lie here in my own feces?” taking a breath, I said, “If you don’t change my diaper now; then I’ll probably end up with a nasty rash, or worse.”

“Okay fine, I’ll do it.” Walking back to my crib, Glenn said, “Just don’t expect this to become a regular occurance.”

Glenn picked me up from the crib; grimacing when he caught the stench of the diaper. He carried me, at arm’s length, over to the changing table. Placing me on the table, he was about to get to work when we were interrupted by the doorbell. Looking at him, I said, “Who could that be?”

“Hell if I know.” Picking me back up, I said, “Come on, let’s go see who it is.”

“Glenn, what about my diaper!?” seeing the changing table shrink into the distance, I said, “You were supposed to change my diaper!”

“Let me see who’s at the door; then I’ll change your damn diaper.”

Glenn carried me downstairs; trying his best to ignore the diaper smell. As we approached the front door, I caught a glimpse of _____ standing out on the porch.
11111111111121211111111112111111111.title: Diaper Trouble

111111111111212111111111121111111111.content:
Once Glenn saw that it was Eric at the door, he turned to look at me. “What should I do, Marcus?”

“I’m not ready for him to know what happened to me; you’ll have to lie to him.” Looking at Glenn, I said, “I’ll act like a normal baby; just, please, get him out of here.”

After composing himself a bit, with me in his arms, Glenn opened the door. “Hey Eric, what brings you here?”

“Hey Glenn, I just thought I’d come to see how Marcus was doing; I haven’t heard from him in a while.” Spotting me, Eric said,
“Who’s this little guy?”

While I squirmed and cooed, in the hopes that I could pass for a toddler, Glenn said, “This is my little cousin Kevin; my folks are taking care of him for a while.”

I would say I was impressed with that, but Glenn was always quick on his feet that way. Looking at me, Eric said, “He looks a little bit like Marcus.” Reaching towards me, Eric said, “How are you doing, little guy?”

I was genuinely put off by Eric’s behavior at this moment; something about the way he was acting freaked me out. I wanted to break character and yell at him but decided that wasn’t my best option. So what did I do? I started crying hysterically; complete with kicking. While Eric quickly pulled his hand away, Glenn said, “Sorry about that, man; I was just about to change his diaper when you came by.”

“I won’t keep you then; just tell Marcus I stopped by.” Waving at me, Eric said, “See you later, Kevin.”

One thing I’d noticed since this all started; once I started crying, I had a difficult time stopping. After closing the door, Glenn tried to calm me down. “Marcus, it’s alright; you can stop crying.”

I continued like that for a few more minutes until I eventually cried myself out. After Glenn gently wiped my face, I looked at him and said, “Sorry about that; but something about Eric gave me the creeps.”

“You sure?” confused, Glenn said, “I didn’t see anything weird about how he acted.”

“I don’t know; maybe it was just me.” Pausing, I said, “Can we please change my diaper now?”
111111111111212111111111121111111111.title: Eric

1111111111112121111111111211111111111.content:
Despite a little trouble during the diaper change, including forgetting to clean me, Glenn did a pretty decent job. Once he was all finished, he picked me up and said, “Now I know what you must’ve gone through; helping mom when I was this age.”

“At least you had the added benefit of actually being a 14 month old.” Sighing, as Glenn carried me out of the bedroom, I said, “You have no idea how embarrassing it has; having the mind of a teenager in a toddler’s body.”

“I don’t know, bro; I think you’re looking at this all wrong.” As we went downstairs, Glenn said, “Do you remember, when we were kids, how Mom would take us into the ladies' room with her?”

“Yeah, I guess.” Shrugging, I said, “What’s your point?”

“Think about it for a moment; you have the mind of a teenager in the body of a toddler.”

It took me a moment to fully realize what Glenn was getting at; but when it hit me, I chuckled. “Wow, I never thought of it that way; I get to look at all the attractive girls I want and no one can say anything because I’m a toddler.”

Placing me in the baby walker, Glenn looked at me and said, “Just don’t go overboard with it; because others won’t know what’s going on, but mom sure will.”

“Thanks for the advice.” Smiling, I said, “By the way, that was quick thinking earlier; telling Eric I was your cousin.”
Glenn had already started walking off at that point, but he then stopped and looked back at me. “I actually can’t take the credit for that one.” Wincing, Glenn said, “While you were taking your nap, Mom told Allison about the need to make a new identity for you.”

“Why do I feel like I’m not going to like where this is going?”

“Long story short, Allison offered to help speed up the process.” Kneeling in front of me, Glenn said, “Mom and Dad are at the courthouse right now; filling out the necessary paperwork.”

I felt like Glenn was holding something back; something that I wouldn’t be happy about. Looking at him, I said, “The necessary paperwork for what, exactly?”

“Well, bro, they’ve got to explain everything that’s happened; like what happened to the 18-year-old you and where the 14-month-old you came from.” Sighing, Glenn said, “It’s not perfect, but they came up with something.”

I was starting to get worried at this point; wondering what exactly they’d decided on. “What did they come up with?”

“Did you think I pulled the name Kevin out of nowhere? That’s going to be your name from now on.” looking at me, trying to figure out what to say next, Glenn said, “As for Marcus; well...they’re declaring him dead.”

I was floored; unable to process the fact my parents had made such a life-altering decision without me. While I struggled to comprehend everything, I felt myself starting to lose control. The shock was too much for me to bear; as I experienced another mental regression. Glenn could only watch in despair as I started crying hysterically.
1111111111112121111111111211111111111.title: An Unwelcome Surprise

11111111111121211111111112111111111111.content:
Four days had passed since Glenn’s bombshell; I was still mad that I didn’t have any say in the decision, but I realized it was the smart thing to do. My parents placed an obituary in the paper the day after they visited the courthouse; we’d been inundated with mourners ever since.

Today was going to be a rough day for everyone; but especially for me. After all it’s not every day you attend your own memorial service. Thanks to an ill-timed dirty diaper, Glenn and I were in a secluded room at the Funeral Home. There was no one around, so I wasn’t forced to act like a baby.

“I can’t imagine how strange this is for you, Kev; attending your own memorial service.” Looking at me, Glenn said, “Just know that I’m here if you need a shoulder to cry on.”

Glenn had really stepped up in a big way the last few days; consoling me when I needed it and generally taking care of me. I doubted that I could ever repay him. “Thanks.”

Suddenly there was a knock at the door; followed by Kat poking her head inside. “The service is about to start, your mom told me to come to get you guys.”

I didn’t have the opportunity to see Kat much over the last few days; because neither of us could figure out a plausible reason why a 13-year-old girl would spend so much time with a toddler. This was the first time I’d had prolonged contact with her. She looked nice in her dress; even if it was slightly more revealing than one would wear to a memorial.

“I have to use the bathroom real quick.” Finishing up my diaper, Glenn said, “Can you bring Kevin to my mom?”

“Sure, that won’t be a problem.”

After Glenn left the room, I looked at Kat as she came to pick me up off the table. “You look nice in that dress, Kat.”

“Thanks.” Blushing, Kat said, “I-I was going to wear it on our first date.”

That just served as another reminder that I was a toddler; I was years away from being able to date, by the time I was 13 again Kat would be in her late 20s. I had to admit though; I did like the feeling when Kat held me close to her chest. “How many people are out there?”

“There are a decent number of people; most are probably family but there are a fair number of classmates.” Looking at me, Kat said, “I must admit, I’m still not used to calling you, Kevin.”

“I don’t think anyone is yet; last night, before putting me to bed, Mom called me Marcus.”

“I’d love to talk some more, but we would probably get out there.” Picking the pacifier up from the table, Kat placed it in my mouth and said, “I guess that means you’re stuck in toddler mode for a while.”
11111111111121211111111112111111111111.title: The Day of the Memorial

111111111111212111111111121111111111111.content:
You have no idea how surreal an experience it was to witness your own memorial service. Even though it was a closed, and empty, casket; it was still weird to watch people come through.

With me in her arms, Kat was sitting in the chairs behind Mom, Dad, and Glenn; the two of us watching as well-wishers came through to pay their respects. I saw several classmates and teachers come through; even people that I never imagined would come by.

I did my best to hold it together; but I would occasionally start whining as people said how nice a person I was and how much I’d be missed. Everything was going fine until an older woman, who I vaguely recognized as one of my grandparent’s neighbors, came over where Kat and I were sitting.

After saying a quick hello to Kat, she turned her attention to me. “He’s such a beautiful little boy; he looks so much like his father at that age.” Touching my cheek, she said, “You must be so proud.”

Kat and I exchanged confused glances; neither of us could figure out what was going on. Turning her attention back to the woman, Kat said, “I-I beg your pardon?”

The woman’s eyes bugged out briefly; before she relaxed and said, “I’m sorry; I didn’t mean to upset you.” Pausing, she said, “All this must be very hard on you; being a mother at such a young age and then losing your baby’s father.”

Kat and I were astonished; this woman, who I hadn’t seen in years, thought that I was my own son. I don’t know what was worse; that she’d make that conclusion, or that she thought I’d have sex with such a young girl. I wanted nothing more than to correct her but felt that would cause more problems than it would fix.

Thankfully Kat had more restraint; she played along with it. “I-It’s been tough this last week.” Tearing up, Kat said, “I don’t know what’s worse; that Marcus is gone, or that Kevin will never know his father.”

“For what it’s worth, I’m sure Marcus was good to both you and the baby.”

Kat’s emotions got the better of her; she excused herself and walked out of the room with me in her arms. She made her way to the funeral director’s office; closing the door just as Glenn walked into the room. “What’s going on? You kind of made a scene with your exit back there.”

Wiping tears from her eyes, Kat said, “Some woman thought that I was Kevin’s mother.”

Glenn burst out laughing; looking at the two of us. “Seriously?”

“Yeah.” Nodding, I said, “Nana & Papa’s neighbor came up; she assumed that Marcus got Kat pregnant and I was the result.”

“Oh, wow.” Turning serious, Glenn said, “We should tell Mom; have someone put a stop to this.”

“How would we do that?” scoffing, I said, “By telling everyone that I’m Marcus?”

“No, of course not; but we need to say something.” Sighing, Glenn looked at me and said, “Unless you want people thinking you got a 12-year-old girl pregnant.”

Now it was my turn to freak out. Looking at Glenn, I said, “That’s a good point; we need to nip this in the bud.”
111111111111212111111111121111111111111.title: The Memorial Service

1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.content:
Before we could even get close to my mom, Kat’s parents found us and pulled us off to the side. Looking at Kat and me, Mr. Maddox said, “Would you two mind explaining why I’ve had four people tell me how adorable my grandson is?”

There were people within earshot, so I was unable to respond. Kat, taking a deep breath, said, “A woman who lived next to Kevin and Glenn’s grandparents thought I was Kevin’s mother.” Pausing, she said, “I was so surprised that I didn’t correct her; in hindsight, I probably should have.”

“You think?” looking at Kat, her dad said, “Unless you want people to think you got pregnant at 12 years old, you’ll find a way to fix this.”

“Don’t worry, Mr. Maddox; we’re going to have my mom make an announcement."

“I wish you luck then, Glenn; because I don’t think she’s in the right frame of mind for that.”

***************************


Mr. Maddox was correct; my Mom was an emotional wreck from the memorial, but she still stood up in front of everyone and made sure everyone was on the same page. Kat and I were in a secluded corner of the parlor, a bottle in my mouth when my grandparent’s neighbor approached us.

“I’m sorry if I embarrassed you earlier, young lady.” Taking a breath, she said, “I was under the misguided impression that you were the same age as Marcus; not a 13-year-old girl.”

“It’s alright, ma’am; no harm was done.” Chuckling, Kat said, “Marcus’ parents decided, long before he died, to adopt Kevin. I’m just keeping an eye on him for them.”

“I must say, you’ve got quite a talent for that.” Pausing, she looked down at me and said, “I’ve never seen a better-behaved toddler in my life.”

As if on cue, I suddenly found myself dealing with a dirty diaper. I started whining to get Kat’s attention. “Sorry ma’am, but I’ve got to change a diaper.”

After grabbing the diaper bag, Kat and I went to the bathroom to change my diaper. I couldn’t say that I was thrilled to have Kat be the one to change my diaper; after all this was the first time she’d seen my junk. While she went to work changing my diaper, I started laughing, “Can you believe she thought you were my mom?”

“Hey, you’d be lucky to have me for a mom.” Retrieving a clean diaper, Kat said, “I’d be an amazing mom.”
1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.title: Fixing the Problem

1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.content:
Things moved quickly after the memorial and funeral; I was not only enrolled in daycare, much to my annoyance, but I was enrolled signed up for one of those stupid Mommy & Me classes at the local pool.

You better believe that I had a few choice words to say about that last part; but I was quickly overruled. Both Mom and Dad insisted that it would do me some good to be around kids my age. I tried to tell them that I was only physically the same age; but they didn’t budge.

So that’s how I ended up, once again, in my car seat; while Mom drove us to the local pool. I was already wearing one of those one-piece toddler swimsuits; which, as much as I hated to admit, actually felt pretty comfortable. “Do we have to do this, Mom?” groaning, I said, “Need I remind you, I already know how to swim.”

“I’m well aware of that, Kevin; but that’s not the sole reason we’re doing this.” Coming to a stop at a light, Mom looked back at me and said, “There are loads of benefits from these classes; mostly for you, but some are for me.”

“Oh please, what possible benefit could I get from this?”

“Right now your only friends are your brother and Kat; and, as much as I’m sure they’d want to, it’s not fair for them to spend all their time with you.” Sighing, as the light turned green, Mom said, “You need to develop friends with children your age.”

“But I have nothing in common with the average 14-month-old!” getting annoyed, I said, “How can I possibly be friends with someone who cries for no reasons, or spits up their food.”

“That’s rich coming from you; or have you forgotten what happened yesterday when Glenn dropped his cell phone?”

I was minding my own business yesterday afternoon, messing around in the playpen. Suddenly Glenn comes in and accidentally dropped his cell phone on the hallway floor. Normally the sound wouldn’t have done anything but yesterday, for whatever reason, it startled me so much that I cried for 10 minutes. “Okay, maybe I’m more like them then I’d like to admit.” Sighing, I said, “But Mom, look at this from my perspective; except for the memorial service, I’ve hardly spent any time around people who didn’t know the truth. Now you’re taking me someplace where I’ll not only be surrounded by adults but actual toddlers as well.”

“Since you put it that way, I suppose I can understand your reluctance to go through with this.” Pulling into the pool’s parking lot, Mom said, “If I were in your position, I’d probably be worried about what could happen as a result of being surrounded by toddlers.”

I couldn’t help but gulp when I heard that. That idea hadn’t even crossed my mind; what could spending so much time around toddlers do to me?
1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.title: A Few Days Later

1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.content:
By the time Mom and I entered the building, I’d more or less resigned myself to the fact that I wasn’t getting out of this. Mom pushed the stroller, with me in it, down the hallway towards the pool area.

As we got closer, I could start to hear the sound of crying toddlers. I was in public so therefore was unable to speak; but that didn’t stop me from making my feelings known. I look up at Mom with a worried look on my face; to which she responded, “It’s going to be alright, Kevin; we’ll get through this.”

As we rounded the corner into the waiting area, I was confronted with the sight of a dozen babies inside a cordoned-off section in the corner; they were either crawling around or crying. While Mom knelt to undo my straps, I prayed that she was right – that I’d get through this.

After Mom placed me with the other babies, she went off to mingle with the other mothers. I couldn’t help but be jealous of the fact that she got to have an actual conversation with someone. With nothing else to do, I decided to join the other babies – the crawling ones, not the crying ones.

I can’t even begin to explain how surreal of an experience this was. I crawled around for a few minutes; having the occasional interaction with another baby. It was mostly harmless, the occasional touching and accidental kicking.

That all changed when a baby girl, probably a month or two older than me, came over and bit me in the arm. She didn’t have many teeth yet so there wasn’t much damage, but it still hurt. As a result I did the only thing I could do; I started crying, which in turn got several nearby babies, including the biter, to start crying as well.

Mom and several other mothers rushed into the play area to comfort us; with the biter’s mom coming over to apologize. “I’m so sorry for that; Grace is at the phase where she has to bite everything.”

As she rubbed my arm, to get me to stop crying, Mom said, “It’s alright; she didn’t even leave a mark.”

Yeah, but it still hurt! After the mother walked off, Mom carried me across the hall to the bathroom. Once we were alone, and out of earshot, she looked at me and said, “Are you alright, sweetie?”

“No, I’m not alright!” Still upset about the incident, I exclaimed, “Why did she have to bite me?”

“It’s something toddlers your age do; it’s how they vent their frustrations.” Sighing, Mom said, “I suspect little Grace was upset about something, and she took her frustrations out on your arm.”

Rubbing my tender arm, I said, “I suppose I should be glad that I’ve got no reason to act like that.”

“It’s funny you mention that; because I think you should act like that sometimes.” Looking at me, Mom said, “It might help sell that
you’re a toddler if you act like one normally would; you already have the tantrums and crying down, so this shouldn’t be too difficult for you.”

Mom did have a point, but I wasn’t exactly a fan of it. “If I act too much like a toddler, wouldn’t that risk me acting like one without realizing it?”

“That’s a risk, yes; but I think you’re capable of preventing that from happening.” Grabbing her things, Mom said, “Come on, I think it’s time for class.”
1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.title: Interacting with Other Babies

1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.content:
The pool session was about what I’d expected it to be; a bunch of mothers, some barely in their 20s, standing in a pool holding onto their babies. All the babies, me included, were wearing tiny life jackets. The instructor, David, was a guy in his late 20s; the type of guy whose sole purpose for teaching the class was to meet single mothers.

The class was the first of a six-week session, so this first class was just a means to introduce us to the water. Oh god, I’m already thinking of myself as one of them. Once he was satisfied that we were all acclimated to the water, climbing out to turn on some music, David said, “Okay mothers, now that your little ones are acclimated; we’re going to have a little fun.”

I couldn’t have been less thrilled about the music; annoying toddler songs like Baby Shark and I’m a Little Teapot. As he climbed back into the water, David said, “What we’ll do is dance along to the music; it’s been found that soothing music and rhythmic motion are beneficial to babies.”

So that’s what we did; all the moms got into a circle and started dancing around in the water; walking a few steps then spinning, that sort of thing. I began to notice something interesting after a while; I was beginning to enjoy this. I even let out a few giggles in the process, which made Mom smile as a result. Maybe this isn’t so bad after all.

But all good things must come to an end; and it did so in spectacular fashion. We were in the middle of another dance when, I’m not exactly sure how it happened, I accidentally swallowed a fair amount of water. I started coughing for a few seconds; until, out of the blue, I started to cry. I don’t know if it was because of the taste or the fact that I’d swallowed water; nevertheless, I was crying.

That got some of the other babies crying; while also getting Mom and me nasty looks from some of the parents. It didn’t take long to realize it was because I’d been the instigator in two instances where babies started crying. Mom tried her best to calm me down, but for some reason I was having none of it.

After I’d been crying for two minutes, Mom held me close to her chest and spoke in a soothing tone. I couldn’t explain why, but that did have a calming effect on me. After another minute went by, I stopped crying and calmed down enough to where we rejoined the group.
1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.title: Swim Class

1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.content:
The class ended a short time later. After drying me off and changing me out of my bathing suit, Mom and I left the pool. Once we left the parking lot, Mom said, “You seemed to have enjoyed yourself, for the most part at least.”

“Yeah, I guess it was okay.” Pausing, I said, “Except for getting bitten and almost drowning.”

Chuckling as she turned onto the main road, Mom said, “Don’t be so dramatic, Kevin; you swallowed a little bit of pool water.”

That got me thinking about the aftermath of the situation. I knew from experience that my moods tended to stay around for a while, so I was impressed by how quickly Mom had calmed me down. “How exactly did you calm me down so quickly?”

“I have a theory about that if you're interested.”

I couldn’t help but feel Mom was burying the lead a little. “A theory, huh? That’s certainly more than I’ve got.”

“Well, Kevin; the short version is that I’m your mother.”

Yeah; she was burying the lead. “You know, the more you dance around this; the scarier you make it sound.”

“When I held you to my chest, you could hear my heart beating.” Stopping at a stoplight, Mom said, “That sound took you back to when you were in my womb; and that sense of familiarity calmed you.”

“Oh god; that’s certainly worse than anything I could’ve come up with.”

"How so?"

I was silent for a moment as I thought about what I was going to say. “It feels like just another nail in the coffin of my life as Marcus.”

“Really?” glancing back at me, as traffic started moving again, Mom said, “I would’ve thought the memorial was the final nail in that coffin."

“What do you mean?”

“In case you haven’t noticed, sweetie; ever since the service, we’ve been calling you Kevin.” Pausing, Mom said, “It’s helped us to adjust; and maybe it can do the same to you.”

“What do you mean?”

“Say to yourself: Marcus is dead. I’m Kevin now.”

I thought the idea was bologna, but it was worth a shot. Sighing, I said, “Marcus is dead. I am Kevin now."

To my surprise, it worked. I didn’t have to worry about how all this made Marcus look, because I wasn’t him anymore. None of Marcus’ friends would care that I had fun at a Mommy and Me class with my mom; because I was Kevin and it was okay for me to have fun with baby things. Marcus didn’t wear diapers or suck on a pacifier, but Kevin did. Marcus wouldn’t have liked baby food; whereas Kevin had his favorites.

*************************


As another example that I was moving on from my former life; later that night, I didn’t complain when Mom put me to bed at 7:30. Looking down at me, lying snug in the crib, Mom said, “Goodnight, Kevin.”

Giggling, as I held onto my teddy bear, I said, “Goodnight, Mommy.”
1111111111112121111111111211111111111111.title: Later On

1111111111112121111111111211112.content:
“I’m sorry, Kent; but I’m afraid this is permanent.” With a heavy sigh, Dr. Sheppard said, “The scans of Marcus’ brain show extensive regression.”

A hand on her chest, Mom said, “What exactly does that mean, Kevin?”

“I’m afraid Marcus now has the mind of an actual 14-month-old baby.” Pausing, he said, “Since nothing like this has ever happened before, I can’t be 100% certain; but chances are good that by the end of the day, it will be as though Marcus was born only 14 months ago.”

Mom started to cry at that point; Dad went to comfort her while Glenn could only stare at me. “Doc, is there any way to reverse this?”

“I’m afraid not, Glenn.” Stepping over towards us, Dr. Sheppard said, “In fact, I’ll be surprised if Marcus can even understand us at
this point.”

Dr. Sheppard was almost correct in that regard; I struggled to understand most of what had been discussed. I understood that I was both mentally and physically a toddler now; but beyond that, I didn’t know what was going on.

Having composed herself slightly, Mom said, “What do you suggest we do, Kevin?”

“The only thing I can suggest is this; treat Marcus like you would any other toddler.” Pausing, Dr. Sheppard said, “Teach him to walk, talk; everything a toddler needs to know.”

“Will he remember anything of his old life?”

“It pains me to say this; but no, I don’t think he will.”

I started to a little fussy at that point, so Glenn handed me off to Mom. As she took a bottle from the baby bag, Mom looked at me and said, “Marcus, if you’re in there; I want you to know that we’ll love you no matter what happens.”

***********************


So there it is; the story of how I went from being an 18-year-old paraplegic to a 14-month-old baby. I did manage to hold onto my memories for a few more days, but they eventually disappeared.

A week after the procedure, my parents announced that I’d died as a result of complications from surgery. They renamed me Kevin and told everyone that I’d given up for adoption by a family member struggling to make ends meet. I should mention that I retained one memory from my former life; a memory of Glenn promising that he'd never let anything bad happen to me.

When I was old enough, they told me about my big brother Marcus; and how brave he was despite everything that happened to him.
1111111111112121111111111211112.title: It's Permanent

1111111111113.content:
Allison stood up as I approached the bed, giving me a strange look as I went past. "That's funny," she said. I didn't realize until just now, but I think you've gotten more muscular since I saw you last. Have you been working out?"

"Oh, go on," I replied, "you're just saying that."

"I promise you, I'm quite serious," she said. "Leaving aside the fact that I'm a lesbian, do you really think I'm the kind of person who would hit on a 13-year-old?"

Those were both good points. I turned back around and went back to the bathroom, shutting the door behind me.

Taking off my hospital gown, I saw that Allison had been telling the truth: my muscles were a bit bigger, and my body fat substantially less. I had picked up a bit of a spare tire over four and a half years of sitting down; now, I looked as physically fit as an Olympic swimmer. I tied my gown back on, opened the door, and went back to my bed.

As I climbed back into bed, I said to Allison, "You're right. I am a bit more muscular. I had to increase my protein intake before the procedure, maybe that's why."

"I suppose that could be it," Allison said. "Well, congratulations on getting out of the wheelchair. I'll see you and your family again soon. Take care, now."

"You, too, Allison," I replied, and we both waved goodbye.

After Allison left, something occurred to me: what with two bathroom trips in a matter of minutes, I had walked more than I had since the accident. Four and a half years ago.

So why wasn't I the least bit tired?
1111111111113.title: You're More Muscular

11111111111131.content:
As unusual as this development was, I decided it wasn’t worth telling Dr. Sheppard; unless something changed. In the meantime, I decided that growing bigger muscles was a good thing. It certainly wouldn’t harm my relationship with Kat; what girl wouldn’t want to date a strong, muscular guy.

With nothing to do and no one to talk to, I became bored rather quickly. I tried watching TV but found that I was incredibly antsy. After a while, I gave up and climbed out of bed. With my phone in hand, I returned to the bathroom and closed the door.

I know it might seem shallow, but I really wanted to take pictures of my new body. I took off my hospital gown and started flexing a little bit. Damn, I look good. I did that for a few minutes until I heard footsteps on the other side of the door. That was quickly followed by the sound of Glenn said, “Marcus, where are you?”

“I’m in the bathroom.”

Putting my phone down, I went to the door and walked outside; without realizing I hadn’t put the robe back on. I didn’t realize my mistake until Glenn said, “What the hell happened to you?”
11111111111131.title: It's Probably Nothing

111111111111311.content:
I stood motionless in the bathroom door as Glenn just stood there; fixated on my nascent six-pack. After a moment where neither of us said anything, I said, “Before you ask, I have no idea how this happened.”

“Shouldn’t that be something you’d want to find out?” walking over, Glenn gingerly put a hand to my abs and said, “I’m pretty sure this isn’t normal.”

After retrieving the robe from the bathroom, I put it on and said, “Don’t you think I know that?”

“I’m going to call Mom and Dad; they need to see this.” Grabbing his phone from the bed, Glenn said, “Has anything else out of the ordinary happened?”

“Surprisingly I’m not at all winded; considering I’ve walked further in the last 20 minutes than I have the last four years.” Moving to sit down on the bed, I said, “As weird as this all is, I wonder if this is a bad thing.”

“Let's wait for Dr. Sheppard to figure that out, alright?”
111111111111311.title: Continue

1111111111113111.content:
Mom and Dad shared the same stunned reaction that Glenn had when they came into the room. If I’m being honest, the looks that everyone was giving me were starting to freak me out. “Marcus sweetie, are there any other issues that we need to know about?”

“Not really, Mom.” Shrugging, I said, “Besides, I don’t see this as an issue; I mean, I’m in better shape now than I was before the accident.”

“That’s what worries me, Marcus; something like this isn’t natural.” Giving my dad a worried look, Mom said, “Let’s wait and see what Dr. Sheppard tells us.”

“Whatever.” Sitting down on the bed, I said, “I still think you’re making a big deal out of nothing.”

“I think your mom is right, Marcus.” Pointing to me, Dad said, “Your arm muscles having grown twice as big since we arrived.”

Going into the bathroom, I looked in the mirror and saw that Dad was right. If it hadn’t been for the fact that I was still the same height, I could have passed for a college football player. The fact that this all started since this morning was enough to give me pause. “Okay, maybe you’re right.” Getting worried, I walked out of the bathroom and said, “Perhaps this is something to worry about.”

Thankfully we didn't have to wait long before Dr. Sheppard showed up. Once he saw what was going on, he rushed me out of the room for some testing.
1111111111113111.title: Familial Concerns

11111111111131111.content:
I spent the next few hours undergoing every test that Dr. Sheppard could think of; even some that I didn’t think were necessary. Once the tests were finally finished, I realized two things; I’d done more jogging, and exercises, in the last two hours then I had in four years, and I’d barely broke a sweat.

I’d only been back in the room for 10 minutes before Kat came by to visit; and boy, was she surprised by what she saw. I won’t go into detail; I’ll just say that if my family hadn’t been in the room she’d be all over me.

Kat was in the middle of rubbing my arm muscles when Dr. Sheppard came into the room, tablet in hand. “Oh good, you’re all here.” Closing the door, Dr. Sheppard said, “I’ve got your results, Marcus.”

Everyone gathered around my bed; while I looked at Dr. Sheppard. “Just give it to me straight, Doc.” Sighing, I said, “I think I’ve had enough with the waiting game for a while.”

“Very well then.” Looking at me, Dr. Sheppard said, “Marcus, you’re in perfect health.”

“Thank god for that; I was worried there for a moment.” Breathing a sigh of relief, I said, “I thought you were going to say something bad.”

“You don’t understand, Marcus; you’re in perfect health, yes, but it’s the extent to which you are that worries me.” Pausing, Dr. Sheppard said, “You ran a mile in 6 minutes but barely broke a sweat; your resting heart rate never went above 50.”

“Kevin, you’re making this sound like it’s a negative thing.” Looking at Dr. Sheppard, Mom said, “Don’t forget, until a few days ago Marcus was completely paralyzed from the waist down.”

“I’m just advising that we remain cautious; we don’t yet know the extent of these changes.”
11111111111131111.title: Awaiting the Results

1111111111114.content:
Allison stood up as I approached the bed; giving me a strange look as I went past. “That’s funny; I didn’t notice it until just now, but have you gotten taller?”

I had no idea what Allison was talking about, and the expression on my face said as much. “What are you talking about?”

Grabbing my arm, Allison gently pulled me back into the bathroom. “The last time I saw you, before my trip, we were about the same height.” Standing the two of us next to each other, she said, “Now it looks like you’re good two inches taller.”

As I looked at the reflection in the mirror, I quickly realized Allison was right. Allison and I were both about 5’9” but it now looked like I was just under 6’ tall. “Okay, that’s weird; Dr. Sheppard didn’t say anything about me getting taller.” Confused, I looked at Allison and said, “What should I do, Ally?”

“It may just be a sudden growth spurt, but it could be something to do with the medicine you were given.” Looking at me, she said, “Just to be safe, I suggest you get in touch with him.”

“I don’t have his number, but I think he’s still got an office here at the hospital. Do you think you could page him for me?” pausing, I said, “I’ll call my folks and have them come over here.”

“Of course, Marcus.” Hugging me, Allison said, “It’s probably nothing to worry about, but it’s good that we’re checking to be sure.”
1111111111114.title: You're Taller

11111111111141.content:
Dr. Sheppard saw what Allison had been talking about the moment he entered the room. “I’m glad your friend contacted me, Marcus; this is definitely something we need to look at.”

“Do you think it’s serious?” noticing that my voice sounded deeper, I said, “Why does my voice sound different?”

“It does sound a little deeper.” Looking at me, Dr. Sheppard said, “Let me run some tests; so we can figure out what’s going on.”

******************************


I went through more tests then when I was being considered for the trial. By the time they were finished, I’d grown to 6’1” – the same height as my Dad. Finally, after an hour of waiting, Dr. Sheppard came back into the room. The expression on his face told everyone the news wasn’t good. Trying not to sound worried, although I knew she was, Mom said, “What is it, Doctor?”

“Marcus is having an unforeseen reaction to the treatment.” Closing the door, Dr. Sheppard said, “The treatment did make him younger, as it was supposed to, but something has caused it to reverse. He’s now aging at an accelerated rate.”

I was suddenly worried that if the aging didn’t stop, I would be older than my parents. Looking at Dr. Sheppard, I said. “Are you absolutely sure, Doc?”

“I’m quite certain Marcus.” Looking at me, he said, “Right now you’re at the approximate age of a college senior, and I don’t think it’s finished.”

“How long will this go on for?”.

“The aging is slowing, Gloria; I believe he’ll be somewhere in his early to mid-20s.” sighing, Dr. Sheppard said, “We won’t know for sure until it’s finished.”

Dr. Sheppard left the room after that; leaving everyone in stunned silence. As impossible as it seemed, I’ve become an adult; only without the required education and experience.
11111111111141.title: A Consult

111111111111411.content:
Dr. Sheppard was right about the changes slowing down. I’m not sure how I knew but, after about an hour, I felt that the changes had stopped. I had no way of knowing how old I was now, but I was no longer a teenager.

I was in the bathroom, looking at myself in the mirror when Dad walked into the room. As he stood next to me, I could easily see a striking resemblance between the two of us. If he’d been younger, we probably would’ve passed for twins.

“I’m scared Dad.” Looking at him, I said, “What am I supposed to do now?”

“I don’t know, Marcus; but we’ll get through it together.” Placing a hand on my shoulder, he said, “But on the plus side, you’re able to walk again; so that’s something to be happy about.”

“This couldn’t have come at a worse time, Dad.” Turning around, I leaned against the bathroom sink and said, “Kat and I were about to become a couple.”

“I see; and what brought this on?”

“Apparently she’s had a crush on me for the last couple years; she never did anything because of the age difference.” Sighing, I said, “She was so excited that we were the same age; but now, I’m even older than I was before this.”

“I’m sorry to hear that, son.” Looking at me, he said, “Although, to be honest, I’m not sure you two dating would’ve been the most appropriate decision.”

“Why not?”

“Because you two were practically siblings.” Taking a breath, Dad said, “God forbid something happened and you two broke up; what if you two couldn’t be in the same room anymore?”

As I leaned against the sink, I realized Dad was right. I couldn’t live with myself if I damaged my friendship with Kat. “I suppose you’re right, Dad; I just hope she sees it that way.”

“I suspect you’ll have plenty of time to figure out how to tell her.”

Dad and I walked out of the bathroom after that, and immediately realized that Dad was wrong. As soon as I stepped out of the bathroom, I saw Kat walk into the room.
111111111111411.title: Talking With Dad

1111111111114111.content:
I don’t know who was more shocked; me or Kat. The two of us just stared at each other; frozen like deer in headlights. My heart was racing a mile a minute; as I wondered what was going to happen next. Kat eventually found her voice first; taking several steps towards me, she said, “M-Marcus?”

I couldn’t speak, so I simply nodded in acknowledgment. Taking several more steps, Kat came up and placed a hand on my chest. “H-How is this possible?” Looking up at me, she said, “You’re an adult.”

Finally finding my voice, I said, “According to Dr. Sheppard, I’m somewhere in my mid-20s.” clearing my throat, I said, “Believe me, I’m even more surprised than you are.”

“I think shocked would be more accurate.” Stepping back, Kat said, “Are you alright?”

Before I could answer Dad, who had walked over by Mom and Glenn, said, “We’ll give you two some privacy.”

Once everyone was gone, Kat and I sat down on my hospital bed. Looking at her, I cleared my throat and said, “Apart from aging almost 10 years in less than a day, I’m surprisingly okay.” Pausing, I said, “Dr. Sheppard says that, despite the radical changes, I’m in perfect health.”

“I suppose that’s a good thing.” Looking up at me, Kat said, “I guess the question now is; what does this mean for us?”

“Unless they find a way to reverse this, and make me 13 again; I’m afraid there isn’t going to be an us.” Sighing, I said, “I’m almost 10 years older than you now; I’d end up in jail if we stayed together.”
1111111111114111.title: Talking with Kat

1111111111114112.content:
I wish I could say that I was ready to face this new challenge: explaining to Kat about are new circumstances. Afterall, I was technically a man at this point. But I wasn't ready by a long shot. I pulled Dad back into the bathroom and had a bit of a breakdown.

"You okay son?" My dad asked with a raised eyebrow.

"There is just too much going on. I'm not ready to face Kat yet. I haven't processed it myself, nevermind trying to explain it to someone else."

My dad gave me a stern, yet understanding look. "I know this is a lot son, but you are going to have to face it. You can't hide this."

I ran my hands through my hair frantically and took a deep breathe. "I know Dad. I will face it. I will have a serious face-to-face conversation with Kat. But I literally found out all this today. I just need some time alone to really come to terms with is all. Just tell her that I will catch up with her tomorrow. Please Dad, I just need some time to adjust."

Dad studied me for a while. "If you are sure I can take care of it. But are you okay son? Really ok?"

I wanted to immediately answer yes. But I paused and really thought about it. Was I ok? I was fully prepared to spend the next few years re-doing my high school years. Now I was old enough to be past college. Once again my would was being turned upside down. Just like all those years ago when I woke in a hospital room to find out I was paralyzed. At least this time, the changes were a consequence of my choices. I have made it through a lot. I could walk now at least. I would be ok. I had made it through worse. I looked him in the eyes (which were on my level now), and replied shakily, "Yes I am...or rather will be ok. I just need.." My voice trailed off. I didn't know what I needed.

"Ok" Dad left to break the break the news to Kat.

I leaned against the bathroom door and slumped down to the floor. Through the door I could hear my dad talking to Kat. I heard him tell her that there were some complications. Yes I was ok but I needed some time. While Kat's voice was too soft for me to make out her words. I could tell that she was upset. I hated the fact that she was upset.
