A part of me wanted to go to her. To comfort her like I have done so many times before. Times when she got in trouble or was having problems with friends. What was I going to say to her. No I couldn't think about that right now.

I heard the door open and close. Then I heard my dad's voice "Son she's gone now. She said she would wait on your call and to let you know she was here for you. Do you want me to hang around or leave you alone?
1111111111114112.title: Something Else

choices: Hair color looks a little different
1111111111115.content: What other things could perhaps be different?
1111111111115.title: Something Else

11111111111151.content:
"But....your hair looks a little different," Allison said in a peculiar voice.

I raised an eyebrow a bit, not entirely sure what she was talking about. I didn't think I noticed anything particularly strange while I was in the bathroom. Well, for the sake of at least humoring her and the fact that I didn't have much else to do when stuck in this hospital room anyway, I supposed it wouldn't hurt to at least check.

"How so?" I asked as Allison was coming up to me, looking my hair over like she was scrutinizing me.

"Like, it's shaggier than before," she said as she placed a hand on my head, ruffling my hair a bit which I presumed was her teasing me a little as well by virtue of my younger age. "Like, your ears are almost covered."

"I just had a haircut, Ally," I sighed before going back into the bathroom to get a look.

"Well, if that was the only thing, I probably wouldn't have noticed," Allison spoke up, getting my attention as she flipped the bathroom light on for me. "No, what really stood out to me was your hair color."

"Huh?" I asked, before I turned back to the mirror to see just what she was getting at.

Well...my hair certainly did seem a little longer, though not to a great extent. I mean, it was still definitely boyish, right around the same length my hair was when I got my haircut, or slightly longer. However, Allison's comment about my hair color being a little different got my attention more. At first glance, I didn't think there was anything strange about my normally light brown hair.

Except....the more I looked at my hair in the mirror, the more something felt a little off. Light brown was always my hair color, even as I got older. Yet, it almost looked more like a dirty blonde or something, perhaps even a little darker in some places. Or perhaps it was just the lighting in the bathroom.

"Okay....that's strange," I spoke, not entirely sure how to process this.

"Well, it's probably just me worrying too much," Allison smiled a bit. "You are back to the start of puberty after all. Hormones can do weird stuff, even to hair."

"How does that relate?" I asked. "I know hair can sometimes change color when people get older but mine didn't before."

"It's maybe a little strange, but probably nothing too odd," Allison shrugged. "Perhaps puberty might be just a little bit different for you this time."

Well, I wasn't exactly an expert in genetics, so I took her word for it. And honestly, a little darkening of my hair probably meant nothing in the grand scheme of things, though having to get another haircut would kind of suck.

Still, since that was all, Allison excused herself, all the while still happy to see me up and about again, to which the feeling was mutual. With that, I sat back down on the hospital bed, not having too much to entertain my thoughts.
11111111111151.title: Hair color looks a little different

111111111111513.content:
Overall, the rest of the day was pretty much normal. Mom did come by again just to check up on me and see if I was doing well, which I was. From what I was being told, all that was really left was a little bit of therapy just to make sure my leg strength was back. Of course, such a thing was likely to continue even after I left the hospital, but I had to go through a little just to make sure I could finally leave here.

That wouldn't happen until tomorrow though, so I basically had the rest of today to do nothing but laze around. Mom was the only one who came by today, as everyone else did have their own lives to attend to.

However, alleviating the boredom somewhat was a call from Kat not too long after Mom left.

"Hey Kat. How's it going" I asked.

"Pretty good. Just kind of hanging out at home right now," she replied. "Maybe I could come by sometime tomorrow?"

"That'd be nice, although I should let you know I do have some therapy to go through tomorrow," I replied. "They want to see what leg strength I have before discharging me. Of course, I'll likely have to do something of that regard once I'm out."

"I gotcha. Just let me know whenever that is and maybe I can come by afterwards," she said. "Maybe get some time without anyone else around."

"Heh, you seem to be adjusting to this quickly," I couldn't help but snicker.

"Can I help it?" she seemed to giggle on the other end. "I thought it was a pipe dream until this happened."

I could certainly believe that, and I was happy that she was happy. Still, I couldn't help but feel a reservation or two myself. I mean, I just learned she had a crush on a guy quite a bit older than her, that being me. I still had memories of helping her on things when she was a child, like riding a bike and all sorts of other things. To go from that dynamic to a romantic one in the span of one day...it was certainly a quick adjustment.

Don't get me wrong, I did like it, but I'd be lying if I said there weren't concerns.

"Hey Kat....you really sure about this?" I asked after a bit. "You don't worry that we might be going a little too fast."

To my surprise, she didn't seem surprised.

"Well, I honestly did think of that after I came home. It is strange, given I had a crush on you but you were mostly out of my age range and now suddenly we're around the same age," she said in one breath. "So....I do share your concerns on it. We'll see where it takes us one step at a time."

Sounded fair enough to me.

"Good enough for me," I smiled to myself. "Sorry if I worried you."

"It's no problem. I'm glad we talked about it," she sounded like she was smiling too. "Well, I got to run. See you tomorrow, hopefully."

"Hope so too. Good bye," I replied before the line hang up.

Well, it was nice to get that off my chest at least. It made me feel a little more at ease as I went back to just enjoying the lazy time, occasionally walking around and seeing if there was anything good on TV.
111111111111513.title: Later on

1111111111115131.content:
And so the day came and went. Nothing too big that stood out. I just hoped that the next day would be my last one. Should be free to go if my therapy goes well.

Eventually, the morning came, and I finally got some details on how the day would go. I'd have my therapy session in the morning, and if it went well as everyone expected, then I'd be ready to get out by lunch time. I sent the details to Kat, which worked out just fine as she was going to be busy this morning anyway.

At about 10:00, I was finally able to change out into some decent clothes, and had to make my way down to a room suited for rehabilitation. There, I was met by who I was told would be helping me.

"Hello Marcus," the woman smiled at me and shook my hand. Her name was Jennifer Summers, a specialist in this field who would be putting together what I would be doing for rehabilitation.

"Hello," I smiled back, allowing her to get everything ready. "So, this shouldn't be too long?"

"Not too much. It's really just to see if you're strong enough to be discharged," she replied. "You seemed to be just fine coming down."

"Yeah, maybe I don't need too much work," I chuckled a bit.

"Oh, I wouldn't believe that just yet," she smiled. "Focusing on certain muscles in your legs is a whole lot different from casual walking."

And I certainly believed that once we actually started on the first exercise. Honestly, my legs had felt fine aside from a little soreness since I got walking again. However, the moment she actually started putting a little pressure on one of them, that's when the aching really hit in.

"Yowch!" I cried out.

"Told you," she said. "It shouldn't be painful, but you should feel some pressure to stretch those muscles out."

And that's how it went for a few more exercises. On the plus side, I did seem to be responding well, and it looked like I would be good enough to head home after this.

Although, about halfway though, I noticed her eyebrows move a bit after I responded to one of her questions.

"Is something wrong?" I asked her.

"Oh, no it's nothing," she shook her head. "Just that you talk a little different at times."

"At times?" I asked. I know I sometimes had a bit of a lazy way of talking, to which some people didn't understand what I was saying. I was used to that.

"Like, you sound a little different whenever you say something with an "l" sound," she said. "Almost sounds like a little accent. But, you're probably just tired."

Guess that made sense. I didn't feel anything particularly odd, though there was this strange feeling I had about how this was the second time someone pointed out something weird about me, such as my hair before.

Well, if nothing else, the exercises seemed to go off without a hitch. I would be free to go soon enough.
1111111111115131.title: The next day

11111111111151311.content:
Some time later, I finally went back up to the hospital room and was able to change into the normal clothes my Mom had left the other day just in case I finally got the okay to do so. They had already been notified that I would be discharged soon, with my mom giving me a text that certainly sounded very excited. I couldn't help but laugh a bit.

Though, I laughed more when I texted Kat about it, as her reply seemed to mostly be emoji's, and rather rushed ones too, which made me wonder if she happened to be in a rush when I texted her.

So, everything seemed to be working out. I was all ready to head out and just waiting for Mom and Dad to pick me up. The couple of odd occurrences from the other day were pretty much put out of mind. I figured they weren't really enough to get worked up over If anything, I was more concerned about other matters.

With all this time to myself, I got to thinking what would happen. Naturally there would need to be an explanation for why 18-year old Marcus was suddenly gone, though that was nothing new since we knew that was coming this whole time. I remembered worrying that we'd have to do something extreme like hold a "funeral" for me but it was doubtful that it'd be needed. Eric, as a close friend, also knew, and I think he and Kat were the only ones outside my family to know. Hopefully that would be enough for things to be pretty seamless from here on.

"Hi Marcus!" Mom cried out for me once she arrived.

I greeted both of my parents and Glenn once they finally arrived. They were all glad of course that I was free to go, and I got the rundown on what exercises I would have to do for rehab.

"So, I might still need your help," I smirked at Glenn.

"Well, I suppose it can't be any worse than pushing you around," Glenn smirked back. "If anything, I might get some payback."

He gave me a playful smack on the shoulder for that, which amused both of us. In any case, after thanking everyone once again, it was finally time to head out. The plan was that we'd get a little take-out on the way and then head home. That of course got me excited. Finally, some good food!

And with that in mind, and with a lot of satisfaction of me climbing into the car on my own at long last, Dad began to drive the car out of the parking lot.
11111111111151311.title: Heading out finally

111111111111513111.content:
The drive home was relatively uneventful, though Glenn did give me some flack for how my hair looked. I just gave him the same explanation Allison gave me, not that he didn't find some amusement out of that too. Mom did find it slightly strange herself though, as that particular shade wasn't common within the family, not immediate anyway.

"Could probably just be a little side affect anyway," I shrugged as I was holding some of our take-out. "I mean, with becoming 13-years old again..."

"That's true," said Mom, accepting that answer.

Speaking of which, getting used to 5'9" again was becoming more apparent, especially when compared to Glenn. As cool as getting my legs back was, it was beginning to dawn on me the ramifications of being 13 again, even if it was something we had prepared for. I could only wonder how Eric would feel upon seeing me like this again. Kat already seemed more than fine with it, which reminded me of how the issue with dating her would surely come up very soon.

Well, all that aside, it was nice once we finally arrived home. I was glad to be able to put all of that aside for a moment to just enjoy lunch and get settled into home again. I had some time before school started up too, so I would have lots of time to relax aside from time dedicated to rehabilitation.

"Honestly, I could get used to this," Glenn smirked as he was sitting next to me. "Considering how much enjoyment you got over being older than me."

"I still am though, mentally at least," I smirked back, getting another round of ribbing from him.

Aside from that, it was a nice time just getting settled into home again. I did text Kat though, saying that I was home again. Surely she'd like to come by and see once she was done with her own affairs, to which she did eventually reply that she'd love to. With a time set for 2:00, I spent that time getting settled into my room again, as well as looking through my clothes that I would be using from now on.

At least it would be easy to save the clothes I had before for when I grew to that size again.
111111111111513111.title: Finally home

1111111111115131111.content:
Things got a little quiet after lunch, as the high from the successful operation was kind of wearing off a little. Since I just got out, I didn't have to start the rehab today, leading to me basically just lazing around. Of course, Mom and Dad were quick to ask if I needed help with anything, which was nice but I was good. The only time I had any problems walking was any time I had to put on more pressure, which wasn't often.

Granted, I did still have moments where I ended up not walking as I intended to. I guess it made sense that after years of not walking, it would be much to expect to walk perfectly after a couple days. I actually did end up backing up into a wall at one point thanks to my legs not quite going the way I had hoped, but it wasn't anything I couldn't handle.

For the most part, I just settled myself with being able to arrange my room however I wanted, now that I could actually do that without having to bother Glenn. Of course, as 2:00 came around, I heard the doorbell.

"Punctual as usual," I shook my head and smiled. Kat was really good at nailing a time on the dot. Probably thanks to her parents.

I went down the stairs, already seeing Kat by the open door, greeting my Mom. I made sure to let her know Kat was coming up as I usually did. Immediately, Kat came inside with a big smile, coming up to hug me.

"Nice to see you up and about," she giggled a bit.

"Me too," I smiled back. "Did you have anything in mind?"

"Just figured we'd hang around. I'm kind of pooped from my errands this morning anyway," Kat replied.

I nodded in agreement. Probably best for me not to walk around the neighborhood anyway.

"Just make sure you don't do anything too serious," Mom called back, sounding a little playful in there.

"Mom, we're better than that," I rolled my eyes, while Kat just giggled.

With that, the two of us went up to my room, which wasn't an unfamiliar sight to her, though of course she noticed all the rearranging I had been doing.

"Tidying up?" she asked.

"A little," I said as I sat on my bed. "Now that I can reasonably walk around, I wanted to finally add some things."

"Makes sense," Kat nodded, sitting down next to me, though she smirked immediately and patted me on the gut then. "The more you move around, the more you'll get rid of this at least."

"Hey, all things considered, I turned out pretty well!" I smiled and shot back. I was used to cracks about my weight due to my condition, particularly from Kat. "I really shouldn't have too much trouble. Just maybe cut back on a thing or two, though I imagine Mom will want to celebrate for a while."

"That I can imagine," she nodded, before rubbing my hair a bit. "Your hair looks darker. I kind of like it. It's closer to mine."

"Yeah, that was pointed out before," I said. "I don't think it's anything too weird. Perhaps another side effect, or maybe puberty might be slightly different this time."

"Well, you would know more than me. I wouldn't understand half of the stuff the doctors said about you," Kat shrugged.

However, right then, Kat got an odd look in her eye, to which I raised an eyebrow. Without warning, she stood up and asked me to do the same, which was little problem. Right when I did though, she put her hand to her head before putting it on mine. From what I could tell, it was like she was comparing height.

"Hey, what did they said your height was now?" she asked.

"Um, about 5'9"," I replied.

"Funny, I'm 5'8".....and we seem to be really close," she added. "If anything it's like you might be a tiny bit shorter than me. They didn't get the measurement wrong did they?"

I thought about it for a bit, though I didn't think it was too odd. I was around 5'8" or 5'9" when I was this age so either one sounded right to me.

"Well, you have been growing lately, so maybe it's you that's grown," I suggested.

"Hmm, maybe," Kat said, still thinking before she shook her head. "Oh well, I'm just being fussy."

Guess it made sense. Becoming younger like this was unusual, so I didn't mind a little fussiness. I didn't think it was anything too serious.

And in that case....

"Well, what now?" I asked her. "We could....uh make out if you like....or play something....."

Really, figuring out how to proceed with the relationship was something I was still figuring out.

"You know....I like the idea of making out," Kat giggled, before turning to my TV. "But I could settle for beating you in something."

Oh, it was on now.
1111111111115131111.title: Kat comes over

111111111112.content:
Looking at my reflection in the mirror, I instantly noticed that I had shrunk almost two inches since yesterday. Okay, that’s weird. As I stood there, staring at the mirror, I tried to explain what I was seeing. The only logical explanation I could think of was that the serum was returning me to my original 13-year-old height.

As I sat on the toilet, relishing the fact that I was doing it on my own, I wondered whether or not I should tell someone about this. Dr. Sheppard had mentioned there would likely be some unknown side effects from the serum, perhaps this was one of them. If Dr. Sheppard was hoping to make this procedure readily available, he deserved to know if there was something happening to me.

So, after using the toilet, I made my way back to bed. Once I was sitting in bed, I reached over and grabbed the call button. An unfamiliar nurse walked into the room a few minutes later. “Is something wrong, Marcus?”

“Can you have Dr. Sheppard called to my room?” pausing, I said, “I think I’m experiencing a side effect from his serum.”

***********************


As it turned out, calling Dr. Sheppard turned out to be a good thing. He was as baffled as I was about what was happening. He called my folks back to the hospital; by which time I’d shrunk another three inches, making me the same height as a typical 11-year-old.

Dad was understandably upset that something like this was happening; after all, Dr. Sheppard assured us there’d be no complications. Glenn was worried as well; afraid of what else might happen to me. To her credit, Mom was doing a great job of keeping it together; although I could tell she was an emotional wreck.

“Do you think Dr. Sheppard will be able to fix this?” a worried look on my face, I said, “If I get any shorter people will think I’m a little kid.”

“Don’t you think like that, Marcus; I’m sure Dr. Sheppard will be able to fix this.” Taking my hand, Mom said, “In the meantime, just be grateful that the procedure had its intended effect.”

Dr. Sheppard walked into the room a few minutes later; a tablet in his hand and a grim look on his face. The look told me that he didn’t have good news. “What’s wrong with me, Doc?”

“It seems that the series of injections had an unintended side effect.” Looking at me and my family, Dr. Sheppard said, “It did repair Marcus’ spine as planned; but instead of making him a 13-year-old again, it’s making him even younger.”
Everyone in the room made an audible gasp. Looking at Dr. Sheppard, worried, I said, “How much younger?”

“If my tests are correct; by this time tomorrow, you’ll be _____.”
111111111112.title: A Few Inches Shorter

113.title: 他还好吗？

12.content:
Main Characters:

Marcus Scott – Formerly an 18-year-old boy; an experimental cure for his paralysis caused unforeseen side effects:
• Cassidy Scott - 13 year old girl.
• Kevin Scott - a 14 month old boy; ostensibly adopted by the family following the 'death' of Marcus.
• Kyle Scott - 24 years old; ostensibly Marcus' older cousin, who moves in with the family while attending college.

Katherine "Kat" Maddox – 13-year-old girl; lives next door to Marcus and his family. A close friend of Marcus, she’s had a crush on him for several years. She’s a new member of the High School Cheer squad.

Glenn Scott – Almost 15 years old; was Marcus’ younger brother, he’s now the older brother. He is grateful to no longer have to help Marcus in the bathroom.

Secondary Characters:

Kent & Gloria Scott – Marcus and Glenn’s parents.
Mr. & Mrs. Maddox – Kat’s parents
Eric Williams – 18 years old; a childhood friend of Marcus.
Allison Park – ICU nurse and family friend. Recently engaged to her girlfriend Jamie.
Dr. Kevin Sheppard – The doctor who created the serum used to cure Marcus.

Thread-Specific Characters:

Jeff - A 13-year-old boy who befriends Cassidy and Kat.

info.brief_description: A teenage boy's life is changed forever by an untested medical procedure.
info.description:
Marcus Scott has been paralyzed from the waist down since he was 14 years old. Four years later, an untested procedure repairs the damage but with some unexpected side effects.
